184.desc: Furnishing: A Grand Duchy of Jeuno flag. The white background represents Jeuno's long history of neutrality. A shining sun and three-pointed bridge are emblazoned in the center.

277.name: Prishe Statue
277.desc: Furnishing: A statuette of Prishe, spirited heroine of Tavnazia. So lifelike are her facial features, you can almost hear her say, "What the hell are you looking at?" when you gaze into her eyes.

278.name: Cardian Statue
278.desc: Furnishing: A charmingly crafted statuette of a Cardian. YoU★aLMOsT★EXpEct★iT★tO★COme★tO★liFE★BeFoRe★YoUR★EyES.

279.name: S. Lord Statue II
279.desc: Furnishing: A meticulously chiseled statuette of the Shadow Lord at the pinnacle of his true power. Its implacable gaze is colder than the winters of Xarcabard.

280.name: S. Lord Statue III
280.desc: Furnishing: A meticulously chiseled statuette of the Shadow Lord outfitted with a suit of armor so grisly that any who behold the figure break out in a cold sweat.

281.name: Atomos Statue
281.desc: Furnishing: A statuette of Atomos so expertly sculpted and lifelike that wisps of the Devourer of Time spill forth from the hovering cavernous maw he uses as his conduit.

282.name: Yovra Replica
282.desc: Furnishing: A masterfully crafted yovra model that not only looks, but also floats like the real thing.

284.name: Goobbue Statue
284.desc: Furnishing: A statuette of a goobbue with its maw agape, about to swallow a mandragora. It is so masterfully crafted that you can almost feel the rows of razor-blade-like teeth severing your flesh.

286.name: Nanaa Mihgo Statue
286.desc: Furnishing: A statuette of the infamous Cat Burglar Nanaa Mihgo that replicates her so vividly you instinctually reach for your coin purse.

287.name: Nanaa Mihgo S. II
287.desc: Furnishing: A lovingly crafted statuette of Nanaa Mihgo emphatically raising a violin in triumph and gripping her bow suspiciously, as a cutpurse would a dagger.

403.desc: Furnishing: A luxurious San d'Orian bed with a green canopy.

410.name: Red Round Table

411.name: Blue Round Table

417.name: Storage Hutch

422.desc: Furnishing: The glossy green leaves of this tropical specimen have won it great popularity as an ornamental tree far from the humid climes from which it hails.

423.name: Tavern Bench
423.desc: Furnishing: A long and narrow bench such as those found at an inn, suitable for revelry and merriment in large groups.

424.name: Feasting Table
424.desc: Furnishing: A stately oak dining table in the Jeunoan style, fit for service at feasts, fetes, and banquets of all manners and sizes.

425.name: Girandola
425.desc: Furnishing: An elegant branched candlestick of Jeunoan craftsmanship.

426.name: Orchestrion
426.desc: Furnishing: A clockwork device able to reproduce many a memorable melody.

427.name: Rococo Table
427.desc: Furnishing: A lavish Jeunoan table often used to decorate drinking establishments aimed at a more refined clientele.

428.name: Semainier
428.desc: Furnishing: A refined Jeunoan cabinet designed to hold small articles of clothing for any occasion.

429.name: R. Storm Lantern
429.desc: Furnishing: A tabletop lantern adorned with a stylish red umbrella devised to prevent the flame from guttering out in inclement weather.

430.name: B. Storm Lantern

435.desc: Furnishing: This moderately sized dresser is a replica of that designed for a wealthy merchant in ages past.

436.name: Jeunoan Armoire
436.desc: Furnishing: This expansive armoire holds enough clothing for one Galka, two Humes, or four Tarutaru.

437.name: Floral Nightstand
437.desc: Furnishing: The neutral tones of this wooden table crafted in Jeuno accent the colors of all types of flowers, dried and fresh alike.

438.name: Orblight
438.desc: Furnishing: This elegant streetlight of Jeunoan make adds a touch of class as an interior decoration.

439.desc: Furnishing: This versatile flat table of Jeunoan make is a welcome addition to any home.

444.name: Luxurious Chest
444.desc: Furnishing A chest gilded with gold flakes often used by San d'Orian royalty.

445.name: Vaisselier Royale
445.desc: Furnishing: An extravagant cupboard of San d'Orian design.

446.name: Gilded Shelf
446.desc: Furnishing: Shelving used to house a variety of objects both decorative and utilitarian.

447.desc: Furnishing: A table often used for dining.

448.desc: Furnishing: A standard table with no particularly unique qualities.

449.name: Personal Table
449.desc: Furnishing: A folding table that becomes quite compact when not in use.

450.name: Candelabrum
450.desc: Furnishing: An elaborate candelabrum of solid gold, used throughout the Kingdom in rites and ceremonies.

451.name: Egg Stool
451.desc: Furnishing: A charming, egg-squisitely designed stool that will egg-liven the decor of any room!

452.name: Egg Table
452.desc: Furnishing: A charming, egg-squisitely designed table that will egg-liven the decor of any room!

455.name: Egg Buffet
455.desc: Furnishing: A charming, egg-squisitely designed buffet table that will egg-liven the decor of any room!

456.name: Pepo Lantern

457.desc: Furnishing: A seasonal decoration carved from a pumpkin. A sinister glow emanates from behind its baleful visage.

458.name: Calabazilla Lant.
458.desc: Furnishing: A seasonal decoration carved from a pumpkin. A sinister glow emanates from behind its baleful visage.

492.name: Drachenfall Water

493.desc: A Bastokan canteen made of brass.

494.desc: Quadavs use this land crab shell for divination.

495.desc: A Quadav charm made of darksteel.

496.name: Qdv. Chestplate
496.desc: A chestplate worn by the Quadav. It is far too bulky for people to wear.

497.name: Gigas Socks

498.name: Yagudo Necklace
498.desc: A bead necklace worn by the Yagudo. It is too large for people to wear.

499.name: Gigas Necklace
499.desc: A pearl necklace worn by the Gigas. It is too large for people to wear.

500.name: Polar Fir Sapling
500.desc: This northern fir is known for its rapid growth.

501.name: Quadav Helm
501.desc: A helmet worn by the Quadav. It is far too bulky for people to wear.

503.name: Sunsand
503.desc: A pinch of rare gold dust from the Valkurm Dunes.

504.name: Rhinost. Canteen
504.desc: A Windurstian canteen made of bamboo.

506.name: Coeurl Leather
506.desc: This cured coeurl leather is light, yet durable.

507.name: Goblin Mail
507.desc: Bronze armor worn by Goblin warriors. Due to its odd shape, only Goblins can wear it.

508.name: Goblin Helm
508.desc: A bronze helm worn by Goblin warriors. Due to its odd shape, only Goblins can wear it.

509.name: Orc Mask
509.desc: A mask worn by Orcish mesmerizers. Due to its odd shape, only Orcs can wear it.

510.name: Goblin Armor
510.desc: Armor made of cloth and leather. Due to its odd shape, only Goblins can wear it.

511.name: Goblin Mask
511.desc: A mask that filters out bad odors. Due to its odd shape, only Goblins can wear it.

512.name: New Linkshell
512.desc: These widely popular magical shells allow communication at long distances. Unopened.

513.name: Linkshell
513.desc: These widely popular magical shells allow communication at long distances. Opened.

514.name: Pearlsack
514.desc: This sack is filled with linkpearls taken from linkshells.

515.name: Linkpearl
515.desc: Linkpearls are used to communicate with linkshells. They have no market value.

516.name: Ripped Pearlsack
516.desc: There are no linkpearls in this ripped pearlsack.

517.name: Broken Linkpearl
517.desc: This linkpearl is crushed and no longer works.

529.name: Luminicloth
529.desc: This cloth is woven from an astral fabric that glows faintly. It is too frail to be used for tailoring.

530.name: Castle Floor Plans
530.desc: A copy of the floor plans used in the construction of Chateau d'Oraguille.

531.name: Lanolin
531.desc: The Goblins make these cubes out of fat harvested from sheep wool. It is a main ingredient in Goblin soap.

532.name: Magicmart Flyer
532.desc: A flyer for Regine's Magicmart. It advertises deep price cuts.

533.name: Derfland Humus
533.desc: This humus is only found in Derfland. Its richness is said to come from crawler droppings in the mix.

534.name: Gausebit Grass
534.desc: This grass is similar to gysahl greens. Gausebit wildgrass has medicinal properties for chocobos, but doesn't taste very good.

535.name: Engraved Key
535.desc: This brass key is engraved with the San d'Orian royal family's insignia.

536.name: Adventurer Cpn.
536.desc: This coupon is distributed to promising recruits to get them started.

537.name: Damselfly Worm
537.desc: A parasitic worm that lives in the stomachs of damselflies.

538.name: Magicked Skull
538.desc: This skull is inhabited by the cursed soul of its former owner.

539.name: Crab Apron
539.desc: The soft abdominal shell of a land crab.

540.name: Bloody Robe
540.desc: Ragged and bloodied robes that belonged to a ghost.

541.name: Dhalmel Saliva
541.desc: A cup of dhalmel saliva, known for its phenomenal stench.

542.name: Wild Rabbit Tail
542.desc: Wild rabbit tails are believed to bring good luck.

543.name: Rusty Key
543.desc: This rusty iron key looks extremely brittle.

544.name: Test Answers
544.desc: These terribly blotched and smeared  sheets of paper are answer sheets  from a test given at Windurst's  School of Magic.

545.name: Shelling Piece
545.desc: A shell used by Tarutaru as currency in olden times.

546.name: Starfall Tear
546.desc: Dew that forms for a few hours only on Starfall Hillock.

548.name: Tenshodo Invite
548.desc: A letter of invitation to the Tenshodo, an underground trade syndicate.

549.name: Delkfutt Key
549.desc: This cermet key has an unusually complex shape.

550.name: Steam Clock
550.desc: This precise Bastokan timekeeper runs on steam power.

551.name: Bruised Starfruit
551.desc: A star tree fruit used in the construction of a Cardian. It looks damaged.

552.name: Hiwon's Hair
552.desc: A lock of Hiwon-Biwon's golden hair.

553.name: Dangruf Stone
553.desc: Legend says that this precious red stone is the coagulated blood of a great Galkan warrior.

554.name: Gold Orcmask
554.desc: A golden mask worn by Orcish royalty. Due to its odd shape, only Orcs can wear it.

555.name: Tower Petition
555.desc: A petition protesting the plans to demolish the clock tower in Jeuno.

556.name: Divination Sphere
556.desc: A polished crystal ball used for divination.

557.name: Ahriman Lens
557.desc: A giant crystal lens taken from an Ahriman.

558.name: Tarut:The Fool
558.desc: Tarutaru use these cards for divination. When upright, the Fool symbolizes "freedom." When reversed, "escape."

559.name: Tarut:Death
559.desc: Tarutaru use these cards for divination. When upright, Death symbolizes "sorrow." When reversed, "release."

560.name: Zeruhn Soot
560.desc: Soot taken from monsters in the Zeruhn mines. It is highly flammable and should be handled with care.

561.name: Tarut:The King
561.desc: Tarutaru use these cards for divination. When upright, the King symbolizes "leadership." When reversed, "distrust."

562.name: Tarut:The Hermit
562.desc: Tarutaru use these cards for divination. When upright, the Hermit symbolizes "wisdom." When reversed, "isolation."

563.name: Glocolite
563.desc: This bumpy, precious stone shines dimly.

564.name: Fingernail Sack
564.desc: This grimy burlap sack contains a fingernail collection. Used for making Goblin rot-tea.

565.name: Teeth Sack
565.desc: This filthy burlap sack contains rotting teeth. Goblins believe that rotting teeth bring good luck to bad people.

566.name: Goblin Cup
566.desc: Goblins use these iron cups for drinking their rot-tea.

567.name: Well Water
567.desc: A waterskin filled with water drawn from a well in San d'Oria.

568.name: Goblin Die
568.desc: A die used in the Goblin game "Mithra Tails." The sides are uneven. Goblins don't like to take chances.

569.name: Gusgen Clay
569.desc: This clay can be found in one corner of the Gusgen Mines.

570.name: Clay Tablet
570.desc: This clay tablet has had letters pressed into it with a stone stylus.

571.name: Selbina Clay
571.desc: This clay is taken from the outskirts of Selbina.

572.name: Herb Seeds
572.desc: Seeds for some kind of herb.

573.name: Vegetable Seeds
573.desc: Seeds for some kind of vegetable.

574.name: Fruit Seeds
574.desc: Seeds for some kind of fruit.

575.name: Grain Seeds
575.desc: Seeds for some kind of grain.

576.name: Siren's Tear
576.desc: This teardrop-shaped precious stone sparkles with a bluish tint.

578.name: Eagle Button
578.desc: This silver button is engraved with the likeness of an eagle.

579.name: Gilt Glasses
579.desc: These glasses feature a finely wrought gold frame.

580.name: Bronze Box
580.desc: This bronze box sat on the ocean floor for many years.

581.name: Starfruit
581.desc: The white fruit of the star tree. A magical process prepares these for use as Cardian heads.

582.name: Meteorite
582.desc: This black stone is said to have fallen from the sky.

583.name: Smooth Stone
583.desc: This round stone is the perfect size to use as a weight for making pickles.

584.name: Torn Epistle
584.desc: This torn envelope appears to contain a love letter addressed to a man.

585.name: Muddy Bar Tab
585.desc: This paper appears to be a list of items consumed at a tavern. Smeared mud conceals part of the list.

586.name: Odd Postcard
586.desc: A card sent to celebrate something. It shows a great deal of wear and tear.

587.name: Damp Envelope
587.desc: An envelope sealed with wax. It is damp and somewhat warped.

588.name: Art for Everyone
588.desc: This how-to art book for young people is written in the Windurstian script. Features works by famous artists such as Pell-Mell and Pisoso.

589.name: Koru-Moru Thesis
589.desc: Koru-Moru wrote this in his youth. Consists of several hundred pages on the art of alchemy.

590.name: Sailor's Cap
590.desc: This black cap is awarded to sailors after many years of faithful duty. Owning one is every youth's dream.

591.name: Ripped Cap
591.desc: This black cap is slightly ripped and falling apart at the seams. It closely resembles the coveted "sailor's cap."

592.name: Supplies Order
592.desc: This paper appears to be an order for a clump of batagreens.

593.name: Magic Shop Parcel
593.desc: A parcel addressed to Regine's Magicmart.

594.name: Auction Parcel
594.desc: A parcel addressed to the North Auction House in San d'Oria.

595.name: Pub Parcel
595.desc: A parcel addressed to the Rusty Anchor Pub.

596.name: Quadav Backplate
596.desc: A darksteel shell worn by the Quadav. It is far too heavy for people to wear.

597.name: Mine Gravel
597.desc: This is gravel collected from the mines around Bastok. Mixed in with the gravel are shiny bits of ore.

598.name: Sharp Stone
598.desc: A rough-hewn stone with sharp edges. The ore in the rock glitters.

599.name: Mythril Sand
599.desc: This mythril sand is obtained by refining gravel from the mines. The purity is too low for it to have any market value.

600.name: Ointment
600.desc: This wound ointment is kept in a shell case. It has a rather pungent scent.

601.name: Ointment Case
601.desc: This container, made from shells, is used for holding ointment.

602.name: Blessed Waterskin
602.desc: A high priest has bestowed the Goddess's blessings on this waterskin.

603.name: Cheval Water
603.desc: This waterskin is filled with water from the Cheval River.

604.name: Vatical Fragment
604.desc: This papyrus fragment is entirely covered with ancient writings.

605.name: Pickaxe
605.desc: These pickaxes are used by mine laborers to excavate passages.

606.name: Fetich Head
606.desc: This appears to be the head of a brass fetich used in Quadav magic.

607.name: Fetich Torso
607.desc: This appears to be the torso of a brass fetich used in Quadav magic.

608.name: Fetich Arms
608.desc: These appear to be the arms of a brass fetich used in Quadav magic.

609.name: Fetich Legs
609.desc: These appear to be the legs of a brass fetich used in Quadav magic.

610.name: San d'Or. Flour
610.desc: This flour was made at the Konschtat Flourmills.

611.name: Rye Flour
611.desc: This rye flour has a deep, dark color.

612.name: Kazham Peppers
612.desc: These ultra-hot peppers will have you breathing fire.

613.name: Faded Crystal
613.desc: This dull crystal's energy has been completely drained.

614.name: Mhaura Garlic
614.desc: This garlic is as nourishing as it is pungent.

615.name: Selbina Butter
615.desc: Butter made from the milk of giant sheep.

617.name: Ginger
617.desc: This root has a pleasant tang that makes it a fine addition to cuisine.

618.name: Blue Peas
618.desc: These bluish-hued peas are enjoyed all across the Quon continent.

619.name: Popoto
619.desc: This relative of the potato is cultivated throughout both continents.

620.name: Tarutaru Rice
620.desc: This grain is imported to Sarutabaruta from a land far to the east.

621.name: Crying Mustard
621.desc: This traditional condiment is manufactured by the Goblins.

622.name: Dried Marjoram
622.desc: This spice is good for getting rid of unpleasant odors.

1088.name: Ancient Papyrus
1088.desc: The content of this papyrus describes forgotten magic. It is written in the old tongue.

1089.name: Exoray Mold
1089.desc: A clump of sticky material that forms the majority of the exoray's body.

1090.name: Bomb Coal
1090.desc: A carbonized chunk of an explosure.

1092.name: Regal Die
1092.desc: Goblins believe that a divine power rests within this die. Its name is derived from the legend of a former owner who is said to have taken the throne with the power of this die.

1093.name: Antique Coin
1093.desc: An ancient coin, weathered and worn beyond recognition.

1094.name: Nail Puller
1094.desc: This tool is used in carpentry for pulling nails from wooden boards.

1095.name: Well Weight
1095.desc: A weight attached to well buckets. It allows stronger Orcs to monopolize drinking water.

1096.name: Tavnazia Pass
1096.desc: A pass issued by the Marquisate of Tavnazia, a country destroyed in the Great War.

1097.name: Yag. Holy Water
1097.desc: This canteen holds water that has been blessed by a secret Yagudo ritual. The Yagudo use this water to keep the undead away.

1098.name: Tavnazia Bell
1098.desc: A bell made in the Marquisate of Tavnazia, a country destroyed in the Great War.

1099.name: Parasite Skin
1099.desc: The pliable skin of a Yagudo parasite.

1100.name: Xalmo Feather
1100.desc: A feather taken from the Yagudo champion, Huu Xalmo the Savage.

1101.name: Mottled Qdv. Egg
1101.desc: A strangely patterned Quadav egg. Those who are born from these eggs are assured the highest of ranks among the Quadav.

1102.name: Blazing Peppers
1102.desc: These fiery-hot peppers are not usually used in cooking.

1103.name: Dav. Storage Key
1103.desc: A key to a storage hole in Davoi.

1104.name: Glowstone
1104.desc: This strange stone glows with a cleansing light, and is too brittle to have any value as a gem.

1106.name: Whine Cellar Key
1106.desc: A key to a room believed to hold someone whiny.

1107.name: Glittersand
1107.desc: This sand refracts light in a beautiful pattern.

1108.name: Sulfur
1108.desc: A sulfurous powder found in volcanic regions.

1109.name: Artificial Lens
1109.desc: A lens produced by an alchemical formula.

1110.name: Beetle Blood
1110.desc: Blood taken from a black beetle.

1111.name: Gelatin
1111.desc: A culinary ingredient made by boiling animal bones.

1112.name: Orcish Mail Scales
1112.desc: Scales taken from the armor of an Orcish warrior.

1113.name: Old Earring
1113.desc: This old earring is dirty and dented.

1114.name: Qdv. Mage Blood
1114.desc: This bottle contains blood spilled by a powerful Quadav mage.

1115.name: Skeleton Key
1115.desc: A tool used by thieves to open locks. It changes its shape to fit the lock.

1116.name: Manticore Hide
1116.desc: This hide, taken from a manticore, is soft to the touch.

1117.name: Manticore Lth.
1117.desc: This cured manticore leather is very strong, yet supple.

1118.name: Antican Pauldron
1118.desc: This piece of shoulder armor is worn by Antican warriors. It cannot be worn by people.

1119.name: Tonberry Coat
1119.desc: This small linen coat is worn by Tonberries. It cannot be worn by people.

1121.name: Antican Robe
1121.desc: This cotton robe is worn by Antican workers. It cannot be worn by people.

1122.name: Wyvern Skin
1122.desc: This wyvern skin is strong and rough to the touch.

1123.name: Manticore Fang
1123.desc: A giant canine taken from a manticore.

1124.name: Wyvern Wing
1124.desc: A giant wing taken from a wyvern.

1125.name: Carbuncle's Ruby
1125.desc: A rare gem believed to have a will of its own.

1126.name: Beastmen's Seal
1126.desc: The beastmen use this seal as a checkpoint pass. It is used by pressing its surface onto a piece of clay.

1127.name: Kindred's Seal
1127.desc: A rare seal believed to be used  by the Kindred as a checkpoint pass.  It is used by pressing its surface onto  a piece of clay.

1128.name: Wyvern Codex
1128.desc: The Ballista rulebook. A fabulous wyvern graces its black leather cover.

1129.name: Griffon Codex
1129.desc: The Ballista rulebook. A noble griffon graces its violet leather cover.

1130.name: Moon Orb
1130.desc: A very solid-looking gem. (Level restriction: characters Lv. 61 and above will be reduced to Lv. 60)

1131.name: Star Orb
1131.desc: A very solid-looking gem. (Level restriction: characters Lv. 41 and above will be reduced to Lv. 40)

1132.name: Raxa
1132.desc: This thick square of cloth feels smooth to the touch. Its manufacture is taught in a land far to the west.

1133.name: Dragon Blood
1133.desc: This blood appears to have come from a dragon.

1134.name: Bast Parchment
1134.desc: This piece of parchment is made of fiber taken from tree bark.

1135.name: Norg Shell
1135.desc: A shell of a bivalve shellfish found in underwater caves near Norg.

1136.name: Uggalepih Key
1136.desc: This key opens a door in the Temple of Uggalepih.

1137.name: Prelate Key
1137.desc: Only Tonberry high priests are allowed to carry this key.

1138.name: Unlit Lantern
1138.desc: The flame in this luxurious Tonberry lantern has gone out.

1139.name: Rancor Flame
1139.desc: The quivering flame in this Tonberry lantern burns yellow.

1140.name: Crimson Rancor
1140.desc: The quivering flame in this Tonberry lantern burns crimson.

1141.name: Blue Rancor
1141.desc: The quivering flame in this Tonberry lantern burns blue.

1142.name: Judgment Key
1142.desc: Opens the door to the top floor of Castle Oztroja.

1143.name: Cursed Key
1143.desc: A key to a room used by a Rhinostery reseacher.

1144.name: Rafflesia Nectar
1144.desc: Nectar taken from the giant Rafflesia flower. Its scent is exceptionally foul.

1145.name: Tonberry Board
1145.desc: Tonberry chefs use this cutting board when preparing meals.

1146.name: Elshimo Marble
1146.desc: An expensive stone quarried in Elshimo.

1147.name: Ancient Salt
1147.desc: A variety of rock salt found deep in the ground.

1148.name: Shoalweed
1148.desc: A rare variety of seaweed that grows only in a certain cave.

1149.name: Star Spinel
1149.desc: It is said that stars can be seen inside this rare crystal.

1150.name: Snobby Letter
1150.desc: Someone is asking for help, but in a very rude manner.

1151.name: Oriental Steel
1151.desc: Scrap iron forged in a method from a far-eastern country.

1152.name: Bomb Steel
1152.desc: A lump of steel forged with the flames of a bomb.

1153.name: Sacred Branch
1153.desc: A branch taken from the sacred tree in the Sanctuary of Zi'Tah.

1154.name: 3Leaf Mandra Bud
1154.desc: This rare three-leaf bud is found only on the island of Elshimo.

1155.name: Iron Sand
1155.desc: Iron dust is mixed in with this sand.

1156.name: Crawler Calculus
1156.desc: A stone formed in the intestines of crawlers to help their digestion.

1157.name: Sands of Silence
1157.desc: This sand has soundproofing qualities when applied to walls.

1158.name: Wandering Bulb
1158.desc: The bulb of a famous missing treant.

1159.name: Wyvern Egg
1159.desc: An egg of the legendary Wyvern.

1160.name: Frag Rock
1160.desc: A rock produced inside the body of a bomb. It is highly explosive and may detonate at the slightest shock.

1161.name: Uchitake
1161.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Katon."

1162.name: Tonberry Lantern
1162.desc: Tonberry are not found without their lanterns.

1163.name: Manticore Hair
1163.desc: Hair from a manticore's mane. This lock of hair is strong yet supple.

1164.name: Tsurara
1164.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Hyoton."

1165.name: Doll Shard
1165.desc: A fragment from the remains of a broken doll.

1166.name: Banishing Charm
1166.desc: A charm made in a land far to the  east. It is believed to banish evil.

1167.name: Kawahori-Ogi
1167.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Huton."

1168.name: Sack of Fish Bait
1168.desc: This large sack is squirming with  shell bug larvae.

1169.name: Earth Pendulum
1169.desc: A magic pendulum created by the  Rhinostery. It is designed to swing  when brought near a source of  earth elemental power.

1170.name: Makibishi
1170.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Doton."

1171.name: Ice Pendulum
1171.desc: A magic pendulum created by the  Rhinostery. It is designed to swing  when brought near a source of  ice elemental power.

1172.name: Ltng. Pendulum
1172.desc: A magic pendulum created by the  Rhinostery. It is designed to swing  when brought near a source of  lightning elemental power.

1173.name: Hiraishin
1173.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Raiton."

1174.name: Wind Pendulum
1174.desc: A magic pendulum created by the  Rhinostery. It is designed to swing  when brought near a source of  wind elemental power.

1175.name: Clotho Orb
1175.desc: White light swirls inside this gem.

1176.name: Mizu-Deppo
1176.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Suiton."

1177.name: Comet Orb
1177.desc: A very solid-looking gem. (Level restriction: characters Lv. 51 and above will be reduced to Lv. 50)

1178.name: Lachesis Orb
1178.desc: White light swirls inside this gem.

1179.name: Shihei
1179.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Utsusemi."

1180.name: Atropos Orb
1180.desc: White light swirls inside this gem.

1181.name: Goobbue Humus
1181.desc: A rare type of peat moss that is only found on the backs of goobbues. The mandragora use this soft material to line their beds.

1182.name: Jusatsu
1182.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Jubaku."

1183.name: Uggalepih Offering
1183.desc: This terrible-smelling chunk of rotten meat is used as an offering to the goddess Uggalepih.

1184.name: Uggalepih Whistle
1184.desc: Tonberries use this whistle to warn their comrades of danger. It emits a high-pitched sound that hurts one's ears at close range.

1185.name: Kaginawa
1185.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Hojo."

1186.name: Bomb Queen Core
1186.desc: The burning soul of the legendary bomb queen.

1187.name: Bomb Queen Ash
1187.desc: Ash taken from the legendary bomb queen.

1188.name: Sairui-Ran
1188.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Kurayami."

1189.name: Rattling Egg
1189.desc: Something rattles inside this mysterious egg.

1190.name: Antican Tag
1190.desc: These identification tags are pounded into the bodies of every Antican soldier. The name and rank of an individual can be obtained by running one's fingers over the stamped letters.

1191.name: Kodoku
1191.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Dokumori."

1192.name: Fallen Star
1192.desc: Light that passes through the brilliant center of this rare gem is bent and reflected off in infinite directions.

1193.name: H.Q. Crab Shell
1193.desc: The quality of this crab shell is beyond compare.

1194.name: Shinobi-Tabi
1194.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Tonko."

1195.name: Ro'Maeve Water
1195.desc: This crystal clear water bubbles up from beneath Ro'Maeve.

1196.name: Qiqirn Cape
1196.desc: Qiqirn outerwear made from a variety of sheep leather. Due to its odd shape, only a Qiqirn can wear it.

1197.name: Sahagin Key
1197.desc: A key carved from a Norg shell.

1198.name: Sacred Sprig
1198.desc: A newly sprouted sprig from the sacred tree in Zi'Tah.

1199.name: Northern Fur
1199.desc: An animal skin covered with thick, long fur. Import is prohibited to protect the local industry.

1200.name: Eastern Pottery
1200.desc: This pottery is world-renowned for its color and texture. Import is prohibited to protect the local industry.

1201.name: Southern Mummy
1201.desc: Advanced mummification techniques  have been used to preserve the body  of this strange creature.  It is considered a hazardous item  and its import is strictly prohibited.

1202.name: Bubbly Water
1202.desc: This sparkling water contains a large amount of diluted oxygen.

1203.name: Egil's Torch
1203.desc: This torch is said to have been made from the ancient tree of the gods.

1204.name: Eye of Nept
1204.desc: This rare gem is said to be the eye of a giant sea serpent.

1205.name: Desert Light
1205.desc: This medicine is said to help remedy high fevers.

1206.name: Elder Branch
1206.desc: A branch taken from the Elder's Tree, located in a far off land.

1207.name: Rust 'B' Gone
1207.desc: This wondrous product is guaranteed to take the rust off anything, or your money back!

1208.name: Ancients' Key
1208.desc: A strangely shaped key used by the first peoples of Vana'diel.

1209.name: Desert Venom
1209.desc: This infamous scorpion poison attacks the nervous system of its victim. It is harmless to people.

1210.name: Damp Scroll
1210.desc: This scroll of far-eastern origin is a little damp.

1211.name: Brz. Bolt Heads
1211.desc: The striking tips of bronze bolts.

1212.name: Mtl. Bolt Heads
1212.desc: The striking tips of mythril bolts.

1213.name: Dst. Bolt Heads
1213.desc: The striking tips of darksteel bolts.

1214.name: Stone Arrowhd.
1214.desc: The striking tips of stone arrows.

1215.name: Bone Arrowhd.
1215.desc: The striking tips of bone arrows.

1216.name: Iron Arrowheads
1216.desc: The striking tips of iron arrows.

1217.name: Slv. Arrowheads
1217.desc: The striking tips of silver arrows.

1218.name: Fire Arrowheads
1218.desc: The striking tips of fire arrows.

1219.name: Ice Arrowheads
1219.desc: The striking tips of ice arrows.

1220.name: Ltng. Arrowhd.
1220.desc: The striking tips of lightning arrows.

1221.name: Chocobo Fltchg.
1221.desc: Chocobo feathers that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

1222.name: Yagudo Fltchg.
1222.desc: Yagudo feathers that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

1223.name: Bird Fletchings
1223.desc: Bird feathers that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

1224.name: Insect Fltchg.
1224.desc: Insect wings that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

1236.name: Cactus Stems
1236.desc: Stems from a certain species of cactus.

1237.name: Tree Cuttings
1237.desc: Cuttings from a certain species of tree.

1238.name: Tree Saplings
1238.desc: Saplings from a certain species of tree.

1239.name: Goblin Doll
1239.desc: An automated doll built to resemble a Goblin. Young Goblin girls often use these toys when playing "house."

1240.name: Koma
1240.desc: A wooden "top" from the Far East.

1241.name: Twinkle Powder
1241.desc: This strange dust gives off a fluorescent light.

1242.name: Bln. Bolt Heads
1242.desc: The striking tips of blind bolts.

1243.name: Acid Bolt Heads
1243.desc: The striking tips of acid bolts.

1244.name: Holy Bolt Heads
1244.desc: The striking tips of holy bolts.

1245.name: Vnm. Bolt Heads
1245.desc: The striking tips of venom bolts.

1246.name: Bld. Bolt Heads
1246.desc: The striking tips of bloody bolts.

1247.name: Slp. Bolt Heads
1247.desc: The striking tips of sleep bolts.

1248.name: Beetle Arrowhd.
1248.desc: The striking tips of beetle arrows.

1249.name: Horn Arrowheads
1249.desc: The striking tips of horn arrows.

1250.name: Scp. Arrowhd.
1250.desc: The striking tips of scorpion arrows.

1251.name: Demon Arrowhd.
1251.desc: The striking tips of demon arrows.

1252.name: Poison Arrowhd.
1252.desc: The striking tips of poison arrows.

1253.name: Sleep Arrowhd.
1253.desc: The striking tips of sleep arrows.

1254.name: Blk. Chc. Fltchg.
1254.desc: Black chocobo feathers that are fitted to the nock end of arrow shafts. They add to the directional stability of arrows in flight.

1263.name: Leshonki Bulb
1263.desc: The bulb of the mobile plant known as the Leshy. Though currently in stasis, it can grow with the proper nutrients.

1264.name: Grt. Boyahda Moss
1264.desc: This Boyahda moss has been growing on the back of a goobbue for many decades.

1265.name: 4Lf. Korrin Bud
1265.desc: The Tarutaru prize this extremely rare four-leaved korrigan sprout, said to bring bad luck.

1266.name: Tonberry Rattle
1266.desc: A warning device built by the Tonberries. If touched, it makes a strange rumbling sound that alerts those nearby.

1267.name: Bee Larvae
1267.desc: The fattened larvae of bees. They constantly squirm inside the bag.

1268.name: Doll Gizmo
1268.desc: A cylindrical gadget found inside dolls. It makes a strange hummimg noise.

1269.name: Mana Barrel
1269.desc: A gadget found inside magic pots. It makes a strange bubbling noise.

1270.name: Arachne Web
1270.desc: A small piece of the Arachne's web. Used as a material for Arachne thread.

1271.name: Pigeon's Blood
1271.desc: This extremely rare ruby gets its name from its deep red color.

1272.name: Arioch Fang
1272.desc: A tiny fang of the Arioch.

1273.name: Namtar Bone
1273.desc: A tiny bone fragment from the Namtar.

1274.name: Southern Pearl
1274.desc: A pearl found inside shall shells from the southern seas. It is known for its creamy luster.

1275.name: Amemet Skin
1275.desc: The thick skin of the Amemet.

1276.name: Tarasque Skin
1276.desc: The thick skin of the Tarasque.

1277.name: Lindwurm Skin
1277.desc: The supple skin of the Lindwurm.

1278.name: Twinthread
1278.desc: This thick silk is spun from rare twincoons. When woven into cloth, the thread's unequal thickness causes diffused reflection.

1279.name: Taffeta Cloth
1279.desc: This soft, lustrous cloth has been woven using extremely complex techniques originating from countries in the Far South.

1280.name: Sarcenet Cloth
1280.desc: This finely textured cloth has been woven using extremely complex techniques originating from countries in the Far East.

1281.name: Cheviot Cloth
1281.desc: This soft cloth is woven from a rare type of wool, using techniques originating from an island in the Far West.

1282.name: Flauros Whisker
1282.desc: A whisker cut from the Flauros. These whiskers are said to detect future wind direction.

1283.name: Ose Whisker
1283.desc: A whisker cut from the Ose. These whiskers are said to detect even the slightest change in wind direction.

1284.name: Inferno Core
1284.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this adaman ingot emits scorching rays of heat.

1285.name: Luminous Core
1285.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this adaman ingot emits a powerful electric charge.

1286.name: Spirit Core
1286.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the hardness of this adaman ingot has more than doubled.

1287.name: Frigid Core
1287.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the temperature of this adaman ingot has been pushed well below freezing.

1288.name: Wooden Hktk. Eye

1449.name: T. Whiteshell
1449.desc: This ancient Windurstian currency, valuing 1 mumu, dates back to C.E. 294. The engraving commemorates the joining of the Tarutaru tribes and the establishment of the Federation.

1450.name: L. Jadeshell
1450.desc: This ancient Windurstian currency, valuing 100 mumus, dates back to C.E. 373. The engraving commemorates the warlord Lungo-Nango's invasion of Elvaan occupied Southern Quon.

1451.name: R. Stripeshell

1452.name: O. Bronzepiece

1453.name: M. Silverpiece

1454.name: R. Goldpiece

1455.name: 1 Byne Bill
1455.desc: This ancient Bastokan currency dates back to C.E. 857. It commemorates the opening of the ferry route between Mhaura and Selbina. A musket and a Galkan soldier are portrayed.

1456.name: 100 Byne Bill

1457.name: 10,000 Byne Bill
1457.desc: This ancient Bastokan currency dates back to C.E. 857. It commemorates the opening of the ferry route between Mhaura and Selbina. The Metalworks and a laborer's raised fist are portrayed.

1458.name: Mammoth Tusk
1458.desc: The tusks of the legendary mammoth are prized by bonecrafters across Vana'diel.

1459.name: Griffon Leather
1459.desc: Remarkably light and sturdy griffon leather.

1460.name: Koh-I-Noor
1460.desc: This transparent jewel is larger than a bird egg.

1461.name: Wootz Ingot
1461.desc: Wootz ore was added during this steel's smelting process.

1464.name: Lancewood Log
1464.desc: A rough-cut log of lancewood timber.

1465.name: Granite
1465.desc: A large, smooth piece of speckled granite.

1466.name: Relic Iron
1466.desc: These unidentifiable iron items were forged centuries ago.

1467.name: Relic Steel
1467.desc: This type of steel is formed by refining piles of relic iron.

1468.name: Marksman's Oil
1468.desc: Applying this lubricant drastically improves the performance of all firearms.

1469.name: Wootz Ore
1469.desc: This ore contains a natural form of the wootz alloy.

1470.name: Sparkling Stone
1470.desc: Translucent gems are embedded in this large stone.

1471.name: Sticky Rice
1471.desc: This grain is imported from a land far to the east. When cooked, it has a consistency slightly more glutinous than normal Tarutaru rice.

1472.name: Gardenia Seed
1472.desc: This spice is used to make dyes and ward off evil spirits.

1473.name: H.Q. Scp. Shell
1473.desc: The quality of this scorpion shell is beyond compare.

1475.name: Curry Powder
1475.desc: Over eleven herbs and spices have been blended to form this pungent powder. The mixture is said to have originated in a land far to the south.

1476.name: Xhifhut Strings
1476.desc: These animal hairs are bound to the body of the instrument known as the xhifhut. The sound created by the xhifhut closely resembles the fricative noises made within the Antica's necks.

1477.name: Xhifhut Body
1477.desc: This hollow wooden case is the body of the instrument known as the xhifhut. The sound created by the xhifhut closely resembles the fricative noises made within the Antica's necks.

1478.name: Xhifhut Bow
1478.desc: This bow is used when playing the instrument known as the xhifhut. The sound created by the xhifhut closely resembles the fricative noises made within the Antica's necks.

1479.name: Xhifhut Head
1479.desc: This tuning device is attached to the body of the instrument known as the xhifhut. The sound created by the xhifhut closely resembles the fricative noises made within the Antica's necks.

1480.name: Mermaid Head
1480.desc: This shrunken head is often mistaken for that of a mermaid. However, it is simply the mummified remains of an Opo-opo, used by the Sahagin when bartering with the pirates of Norg.

1481.name: Mermaid Body
1481.desc: This shriveled body is often mistaken for that of a mermaid. However, it is simply the mummified remains of an Opo-opo, used by the Sahagin when bartering with the pirates of Norg.

1482.name: Mermaid Hands
1482.desc: These shrunken hands are often mistaken for those of a mermaid. However, they are simply the mummified remains of an Opo-opo, used by the Sahagin when bartering with the pirates of Norg.

1483.name: Mermaid Tail
1483.desc: This shriveled tail is often mistaken for that of a mermaid. However, it is simply the mummified remains of a pugil, used by the Sahagin when bartering with the pirates of Norg.

1484.name: Rancor Mantle
1484.desc: This type of weblike mantle is used in the lanterns of Tonberry assassins. The lantern's mysterious light is said to have hypnotic powers.

1485.name: Rancor Globe
1485.desc: This globe, constructed from thin beetle shells, is found on the lanterns of Tonberry assassins. The lantern's mysterious light is said to have hypnotic powers.

1486.name: Rancor Tank
1486.desc: This putrid-smelling tank is found on the lanterns of Tonberry assassins. The lantern's mysterious light is said to have hypnotic powers.

1487.name: Rancor Handle
1487.desc: This Hume bone lid and handle is found on the lanterns of Tonberry assassins. The lantern's mysterious light is said to have hypnotic powers.

1488.name: "A" Egg
1488.desc: This bird egg has a small "A" printed on the bottom.

1489.name: "B" Egg
1489.desc: This bird egg has a small "B" printed on the bottom.

1490.name: "C" Egg
1490.desc: This bird egg has a small "C" printed on the bottom.

1491.desc: This bird egg has a small "D" printed on the bottom.

1492.desc: This bird egg has a small "E" printed on the bottom.

1493.desc: This bird egg has a small "F" printed on the bottom.

1495.desc: This bird egg has a small "H" printed on the bottom.

1506.desc: This bird egg has a small "S" printed on the bottom.

1507.name: "T" Egg
1507.desc: This bird egg has a small "T" printed on the bottom.

1508.name: "U" Egg
1508.desc: This bird egg has a small "U" printed on the bottom.

1509.name: "V" Egg
1509.desc: This bird egg has a small "V" printed on the bottom.

1510.name: "W" Egg
1510.desc: This bird egg has a small "W" printed on the bottom.

1511.name: "X" Egg
1511.desc: This bird egg has a small "X" printed on the bottom.

1512.name: "Y" Egg
1512.desc: This bird egg has a small "Y" printed on the bottom.

1513.name: "Z" Egg
1513.desc: This bird egg has a small "Z" printed on the bottom.

1514.name: Chocobo Ticket
1514.desc: This "free ride" ticket is part of a campaign by the chocobo stables to encourage more adventurers to utilize their services.

1515.name: Fang Arrowhd.
1515.desc: The striking tips of fang arrows.

1516.name: Griffon Hide
1516.desc: The lustrous hide of a griffon.

1517.name: Giant Frozen Head
1517.desc: The head of what looks like an enormous mammal lies encased within this block of ice.

1518.name: Colossal Skull
1518.desc: This giant, half-petrified skull closely resembles that of a Hume.

1519.name: Fresh Orc Liver
1519.desc: This pungent liver was only recently cut out of a healthy Orc.

1520.name: Goblin Grease
1520.desc: A Goblin Alchemist has brewed this lubricant from a secret recipe.

1521.name: Slime Juice
1521.desc: Only certain, rare species of slime produce this highly sought-after fluid.

1522.name: Fresh Marjoram
1522.desc: When dried, this spice is useful for getting rid of unpleasant odors.

1523.name: Apple Mint
1523.desc: This herb is said to have relaxing properties as well as assist digestion. It has a light, apple scent.

1524.name: Fresh Mugwort
1524.desc: When dried, this spice is said to assist digestion.

1525.name: Adamantoise Egg
1525.desc: The tiny egg of an adamantoise. Due to the traces of metal found in their shells, these eggs are often mistaken for chunks of ore.

1526.name: Wyrm Beard
1526.desc: This beard of the legendary wyrm is actually an overgrown scale taken from under its chin.

1527.name: Behemoth Tongue
1527.desc: This giant tongue of the behemoth is covered with hundreds of tiny lumps.

1528.name: Red Cryptex
1528.desc: A red stone used by the Orcs to deliver orders. The red color signifies "Ronfaure," and the number of nails imbedded in its surface signifies "Attack."

1529.name: Dst. Engraving
1529.desc: A sheet of darksteel used by the Quadav to deliver orders. Upon it is a map of Gustaberg with arrows indicating an invasion route.

1530.name: 7-Knot Quipu
1530.desc: A knotted rope used by the Yagudo to deliver orders. The type of knot signifies the order (attack), while the number of knots signifies the location (Sarutabaruta).

1531.name: Galka Fang Sack
1531.desc: A fang-filled bag used by Goblin thieves to relay messages. It is believed that the type of fang (Galka) represents the place of meeting (Zulkheim).

1532.name: Jade Cryptex
1532.desc: A green stone used by the Orcs to deliver orders. The green color signifies "Norvallen," and the number of nails imbedded in its surface signifies "Attack."

1533.name: Silver Engraving
1533.desc: A sheet of silver used by the Quadav to deliver orders. Upon it is a map of Derfland with arrows indicating an invasion route.

1534.name: Mithra Fang Sack
1534.desc: A fang-filled bag used by Goblin thieves to relay messages. It is  believed that the type of fang (Mithra) represents the place of meeting (Kolshushu).

1535.name: 13-Knot Quipu
1535.desc: A knotted rope used by the Yagudo to deliver orders. The type of knot signifies the order (attack), while the number of knots signifies the location (Aragoneu).

1536.name: T. Leather Missive
1536.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of Fauregandi.

1537.name: B. Leather Missive
1537.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of Valdeaunia.

1538.name: R. Leather Missive
1538.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of Qufim.

1539.name: Hound Fang Sack
1539.desc: A fang-filled bag used by Goblin  thieves to relay messages. It is believed that the type of fang (hell hound) represents the place of meeting (Li'Telor).

1540.name: D. Leather Missive
1540.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of Kuzotz.

1541.name: Bunny Fang Sack
1541.desc: A fang-filled bag used by Goblin thieves to relay messages. It is believed that the type of fang (beach bunny) represents the place of meeting (Vollbow).

1542.name: S. Leather Missive
1542.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of the Elshimo Lowlands.

1543.name: C. Leather Missive
1543.desc: These secret communications were widely used by the beastmen during the Great War to signal attacks. The pictographs drawn upon this missive represent the region of the Elshimo Uplands.

1544.name: Mini Fork of Fire
1544.desc: A special tuning fork of fire developed by the Rhinostery.

1545.name: Mini Fork of Ice
1545.desc: A special tuning fork of ice developed by the Rhinostery.

1546.name: Mini Fork of Wind
1546.desc: A special tuning fork of wind developed by the Rhinostery.

1547.name: Mini Fork of Earth
1547.desc: A special tuning fork of earth developed by the Rhinostery.

1548.name: Mini Fork of Ltn.
1548.desc: A special tuning fork of lightning developed by the Rhinostery.

1549.name: Mini Fork of Wtr.
1549.desc: A special tuning fork of water developed by the Rhinostery.

1550.name: Ark Pentasphere
1550.desc: A burning circle has been drawn in illuminink upon this parchment.

1551.name: Cloudy Orb
1551.desc: It is difficult to see what is inside this milky-colored gem. (Level restriction: characters Lv. 21 and above will be reduced to Lv. 20)

1552.name: Sky Orb
1552.desc: It is difficult to see what is inside this milky-colored gem. (Level restriction: characters Lv. 31 and above will be reduced to Lv. 30)

1553.name: Themis Orb
1553.desc: It is difficult to see what is inside this milky-colored gem.

1554.name: Turmeric
1554.desc: This bright yellow spice is made by grinding the dried roots of the turmeric plant.

1555.name: Coriander
1555.desc: This spice is made from the dried seed-like fruit of the coriander plant.

1556.name: Atst. of Might
1556.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered hand-to-hand combat.

1557.name: Atst. of Celerity
1557.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the dagger.

1558.name: Atst. of Glory
1558.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the sword.

1559.name: Atst. of Right.
1559.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the great sword.

1560.name: Atst. of Bravery
1560.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the axe.

1561.name: Atst. of Force
1561.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the great axe.

1562.name: Atst. of Vigor
1562.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the scythe.

1563.name: Atst. of Fortitude
1563.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the polearm.

1564.name: Atst. of Legerity
1564.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the katana.

1565.name: Atst. of Decisiv.
1565.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the great katana.

1566.name: Atst. of Sacrifice
1566.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the club.

1567.name: Atst. of Virtue
1567.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the staff.

1568.name: Atst. of Transcen.
1568.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the bow.

1569.name: Atst. of Harmony
1569.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered music.

1570.name: Atst. of Accuracy
1570.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered firearms.

1571.name: Mystic Fragment
1571.desc: A fragment from a forbidden pair of knuckles.

1572.name: Ornate Fragment
1572.desc: A fragment from a forbidden dagger.

1573.name: Holy Fragment
1573.desc: A fragment from a forbidden longsword.

1574.name: Intricate Fragment
1574.desc: A fragment from a forbidden claymore.

1575.name: Runaeic Fragment
1575.desc: A fragment from a forbidden tabar.

1576.name: Seraphic Fragment
1576.desc: A fragment from a forbidden great axe.

1577.name: Tenebrous Frag.
1577.desc: A fragment from a forbidden scythe.

1578.name: Stellar Fragment
1578.desc: A fragment from a forbidden spear.

1579.name: Demoniac Frag.
1579.desc: A fragment from a forbidden kunai.

1580.name: Divine Fragment
1580.desc: A fragment from a forbidden tachi.

1581.name: Heavenly Frag.
1581.desc: A fragment from a forbidden hammer.

1582.name: Celestial Frag.
1582.desc: A fragment from a forbidden staff.

1583.name: Snarled Fragment
1583.desc: A fragment from a forbidden longbow.

1584.name: Mysterial Frag.
1584.desc: A fragment from a forbidden horn.

1585.name: Ethereal Fragment
1585.desc: A fragment from a forbidden firearm.

1586.name: Titanictus Shell
1586.desc: The shell of a giant titanictus.

1587.name: H.Q. Pugil Scls.
1587.desc: These scales, taken from a large fish, are in extremely good condition.

1588.name: Tufa
1588.desc: A friable rock formed of compacted volcanic ash.

1589.name: Necropsyche
1589.desc: The essence of a soul trapped in Dynamis.

1590.name: Holy Basil
1590.desc: This fragrant leaf is often used as a spice.

1591.name: H.Q. Coeurl Hide
1591.desc: This coeurl hide is in excellent condition.

1592.name: Cactuar Root
1592.desc: Over thousands of years of evolution, the roots of the cactuar have developed into "legs" capable of carrying the plantoids long distances.

1593.name: Dire Candle
1593.desc: This item first appeared long ago in a Hume epic poem. It is written that the emperor who lights this candle is plagued with misfortune.

1594.name: Bloody Candle
1594.desc: This item first appeared long ago in a Hume epic poem. It is written that the empress who lights this candle has the life essence slowly drained from her.

1595.name: Tome of Whispers
1595.desc: Used in Elvaan rituals, this forbidden parchment lists worldly sins. However, it is rumored that the souls of those who read it are damned for eternity.

1596.name: Tome of Moans
1596.desc: Used in Elvaan rituals, this forbidden astrological document is said to reveal the future. However, it is rumored that the souls of those who read it are damned for eternity.

1597.name: Snickering Skull
1597.desc: A Tarutaru lullaby warns that bad children who forget their chores will be visited by the snickering skull, and those who have heard the skull's snickering cannot stop laughing themselves.

1598.name: Sniffling Skull
1598.desc: A Tarutaru lullaby warns that bad children who do not go to bed early will be visited by the sniffling skull, and those who have heard the skull's sniffling cannot stop crying themselves.

1599.name: Yhoator Flea
1599.desc: Mithran urban myth states that if bitten by the legendary Yhoator flea, one's entire body will itch for days.

1600.name: Rusty Tail Cutter
1600.desc: Galkan ghost stories tell grim tales of those who had the misfortune to come across these dreaded cutters. Tails lopped off by the tool are said to never grow back...

1601.name: Mannequin Head
1601.desc: The wooden head of a display mannequin.

1602.name: Mannequin Body
1602.desc: The wooden body of a display mannequin.

1603.name: Mannequin Hands
1603.desc: The wooden hands of a display mannequin.

1604.name: Mannequin Legs
1604.desc: The wooden legs of a display mannequin.

1605.name: Mannequin Feet
1605.desc: The wooden feet of a display mannequin.

1606.name: Burning Memory
1606.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1607.name: Bitter Memory
1607.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1608.name: Fleeting Memory
1608.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1609.name: Profane Memory
1609.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1610.name: Strtlng. Memory
1610.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1611.name: Somber Memory
1611.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1612.name: Radiant Memory
1612.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1613.name: Malevolent Mem.
1613.desc: A suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1614.name: Corse Bracelet
1614.desc: The amber-laden bracelet of a corse. A curse has been placed upon the accessory, preventing anything living from wearing it.

1615.name: Buffalo Horn
1615.desc: The twisted horn of a buffalo.

1617.name: Flytrap Leaf
1617.desc: Members of the flytrap family use these large, broad leaves to hover above the ground.

1618.name: Uragnite Shell
1618.desc: This hard shell protects the uragnite from predators.

1619.name: Hippogryph Fthr.
1619.desc: This feather grows on the head of a hippogryph.

1620.name: Taurus Horn
1620.desc: This dark horn is bent into an almost perfect half-circle.

1621.name: Taurus Wing
1621.desc: The blood that poured from the wound where the wing was removed has begun to coagulate...

1622.name: Bugard Tusk
1622.desc: The overdeveloped canine of a bugard.

1623.name: Eft Skin
1623.desc: Thousands of tiny scales make up this brilliantly sparkling skin.

1624.name: Bugbear Mask
1624.desc: A leather mask that filters out toxic gasses. Due to its odd shape, only bugbears can wear it.

1625.name: Moblin Helm
1625.desc: A simple brass helm. Due to its odd shape, only Moblins can wear it.

1626.name: Avatar Blood
1626.desc: This blood appears to have come from a terrestrial avatar. It glows faintly in the dark.

1627.name: Arm. Arrowhd.
1627.desc: The striking tips of Bodkin arrows.

1628.name: Buffalo Hide
1628.desc: The prickly hide of a buffalo.

1630.name: Cluster Ash
1630.desc: The burnt remains of a cluster.

1631.name: Moblin Armor
1631.desc: Armor made of cloth and leather. Due to its odd shape, only Moblins can wear it.

1632.name: Moblin Mail
1632.desc: Simple brass armor. Due to its odd shape, only Moblins can wear it.

1633.name: Clot Plasma
1633.desc: A miniature plasmoid sample taken from a clot species of the slime family. It is perfectly edible, and quite delicious, once detoxified.

1635.name: Paktong Ingot
1635.desc: An ingot of smelted paktong ore.

1636.name: Moblinweave
1636.desc: Light, sturdy, fire-resistant cloth woven from Moblin thread.

1637.name: Bugard Leather
1637.desc: Bugard leather is famous for being highly resistant to the elements.

1638.name: Moblin Mask
1638.desc: A leather mask that filters out dust and smoke. Due to its odd shape, only Moblins can wear it.

1639.name: Corse Robe
1639.desc: The velvet robe of a corse. A curse has been placed upon the clothing, preventing anything living from wearing it.

1640.name: Bugard Skin
1640.desc: The bumpy skin of a bugard.

1641.name: Fire Anima
1641.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits a fiery aura.

1642.name: Ice Anima
1642.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits an icy aura.

1643.name: Wind Anima
1643.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits an airy aura.

1644.name: Earth Anima
1644.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits an earthen aura.

1645.name: Lightning Anima
1645.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits a voltaic aura.

1646.name: Water Anima
1646.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits an aqueous aura.

1647.name: Light Anima
1647.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits a celestial aura.

1648.name: Dark Anima
1648.desc: This partially materialized quasispirit emits a dark aura.

1649.name: Scarlet Stone
1649.desc: Deep red gems are embedded within this rock.

1650.name: Kopparnickel Ore
1650.desc: This ore contains the metal kopparnickel.

1651.name: Moblin Thread
1651.desc: Thread spun from an artificial fiber developed by the Moblins.

1652.name: Rice Vinegar
1652.desc: A traditional Eastern vinegar made by fermenting rice.

1653.name: Demon Pen
1653.desc: This magical pen is made from the finger of a Demon. When placed on a piece of parchment, it will write one's diary for him.

1654.name: Igneous Rock
1654.desc: This rock is formed when magma cools.

1655.name: Leather Pouch
1655.desc: A small bag made from sheep leather.

1656.name: Brass Tank
1656.desc: This tank made from brass sheets is mainly used to store liquids.

1657.name: Bundling Twine
1657.desc: This twine is used by members of the Carpenters' Guild to bundle logs together.

1658.name: Sealion Crest Key
1658.desc: A sealion is engraved into the handle of this brass key.

1659.name: Coral Crest Key
1659.desc: A branch of coral is engraved into the handle of this brass key.

1660.name: Bronze Key
1660.desc: A simple key forged from bronze.

1661.name: Misareaux Garlic
1661.desc: This variety of garlic, known for its pungent odor and high level of nutrition, is only found on the Tavnazian Archipelago.

1662.name: Ctdl. Tapestry
1662.desc: Said to have been used by a former saint, this tapestry has been recognized by the San d'Orian Cathedral as an authentic relic.

1663.name: Arnica Root
1663.desc: When ground into a powder and served with hot water, the root of the arnica has the ability to cure various ailments.

1664.name: Eastern Gem
1664.desc: These flat, round jade stones are treasured in lands to the Far East.

1665.name: Copper Key
1665.desc: A tiny key made of pure copper.

1666.name: Chameleon Dia.
1666.desc: This rare diamond changes color depending on the surrounding air temperature.

1667.name: Cluster Core
1667.desc: The molten center of a cluster.

1668.name: Clean Snap Rod
1668.desc: This fastwater fishing rod has been snapped perfectly in two--almost too perfectly.

1669.name: Hoary Bomb Ash
1669.desc: The burnt remains of an ancient bomb.

1670.name: Sprt. Orichalcum
1670.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the hardness of this orichalcum ingot has more than doubled.

1671.name: Bwtch. Ash Lbr.
1671.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this ash has the ability to break powerful curses.

1672.name: Exor. Oak Lbr.
1672.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this oak has the ability to reflect magic.

1673.name: Ether. Oak Lbr.
1673.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this oak has the ability to teleport.

1674.name: Indurated Gold
1674.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this gold's hardness has more than doubled.

1675.name: Neutralizing Slv.
1675.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this silver has the ability to neutralize even the strongest of poisons.

1676.name: Frgd. Orichalcum
1676.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the temperature of this orichalcum ingot has been pushed well below freezing.

1677.name: Aqueous Ocl.
1677.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this orichalcum has the power to call forth the spirits of water.

1678.name: Rogue's Silver
1678.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this silver shines brighter than most precious metals.

1679.name: Bewitched Gold
1679.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this gold has the ability to break powerful curses.

1680.name: H.Q. Bugard Skin
1680.desc: This bugard skin is in excellent condition.

1681.name: Light Steel
1681.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this steel weighs half that of a normal ingot.

1682.name: Treasury Gold
1682.desc: The Kingdom of San d'Oria stores these ingots in the heavily guarded royal treasury. The mark of the griffon is imbedded in the center of each bar.

1683.name: Attohwa Ginseng
1683.desc: This medicinal root only grows in the arid soil of the Attohwa Chasm. Its rarity makes it quite expensive.

1684.name: Gold Key
1684.desc: An elaborately designed solid gold key.

1685.name: Warding Oil
1685.desc: Members of the Quadav patrol use this pungent oil in their lamps. It is said that the strong odor the oil emits when lit repels even the most terrible of beasts.

1686.name: Soiled Letter
1686.desc: This grimy letter has been sealed with red wax.

1687.name: Recoll. of Fear
1687.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1688.name: Recoll. of Pain
1688.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1689.name: Recoll. of Guilt
1689.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1690.name: Hippogryph Tf.
1690.desc: This feather grows on the tail of a hippogryph.

1691.name: Giant Scale
1691.desc: This faintly glowing scale is thought to have been taken from the body of a colossal wyrm.

1692.name: Carmine Chip
1692.desc: This translucent carmine chip is shaped like a tiny hexagon. A strange light is emitted from its sparkling center.

1693.name: Cyan Chip
1693.desc: This translucent cyan chip is shaped like a tiny hexagon. A strange light is emitted from its sparkling center.

1694.name: Gray Chip
1694.desc: This translucent gray chip is shaped like a tiny hexagon. A strange light is emitted from its sparkling center.

1695.name: Habaneros
1695.desc: Molten hot peppers famous for being more "stimulating" than the renowned blazing peppers.

1696.name: Magicked Steel
1696.desc: This special steel has been forged with a magical flame.

1698.name: Extra-Fine File
1698.desc: It is said Demons use these files to sharpen their horns and claws.

1699.name: Scarlet Linen
1699.desc: Cloth woven from linen and bloodthread.

1700.name: Bloodthread
1700.desc: This scarlet thread is produced by a variety of diremite. Much like a spider's web, it is extremely light, but unbelievably strong.

1701.name: Fine Linen Cloth
1701.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this linen is twice as thin as that of a normal square.

1702.name: Smooth Velvet
1702.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this velvet is extremely soft and smooth.

1703.name: Kunwu Ore
1703.desc: This ore contains traces of the metal kunwu iron.

1704.name: Kunwu Iron
1704.desc: A chunk of kunwu iron, smelted using a traditional Far Eastern method.

1705.name: Kunwu Sheet
1705.desc: Kunwu iron forged into a sheet.

1706.name: Nyumomo Doll
1706.desc: The main character from the ridiculously popular "Nyumomo, the Twirling Taru" comics. There is not a girl in Jeuno who has not begged her father to buy her one of these dolls.

1707.name: Eastern Paper
1707.desc: This high-quality paper has been made with advanced methods known only to those living in the Eastern empire.

1708.name: Counterfeit Gil
1708.desc: It is rumored the Goblins make these replicas of real gil. However, because they cannot duplicate the magic imprint cast by the Vana'diel Treasury, most shopkeeps can easily spot one.

1709.name: Naphille Pochette
1709.desc: Naphille is known around Jeuno for being on the cutting edge of fashion trends. Anyone who's who in the duchy can be seen carrying one of her outrageously expensive pochettes.

1710.name: Fernan's Diaries
1710.desc: This biography portrays a day in the life of Jeuno's most popular actor, Fernan. From witty quotes to true-to-life drawings, there is enough material in this book to make any fan drool.

1711.name: Molybden. Ingot
1711.desc: An ingot of smelted molybdenum ore.

1712.name: Cashmere Wool
1712.desc: This lustrous wool is found on a rare variety of sheep bred only in Near Eastern nations.

1714.name: Cashmere Cloth
1714.desc: Luxurious cloth woven from the highest quality cashmere thread. It retains heat better than any of the more common wools.

1715.name: Vivified Coral
1715.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this coral has begun to grow again.

1716.name: Smth. Btl. Jaw
1716.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this beetle jaw creates almost no friction.

1717.name: Photoanima
1717.desc: This fully materialized quasispirit emits the auras of all eight elements.

1718.name: M-bugard Tusk
1718.desc: The twisted tusk of a megalobugard.

1719.name: Harajnite Shell
1719.desc: This hard shell protects the harajnite from predators.

1720.name: Teal Memosphere
1720.desc: The crystallized remains of one of the Empty. It emits a soft blue glow.

1721.name: Beryl Memosphere
1721.desc: The crystallized remains of one of the Empty. It emits a soft green glow.

1722.name: Indigo Memosphere
1722.desc: The crystallized remains of one of the Empty. It emits a soft indigo glow.

1723.name: White Memosphere
1723.desc: The crystallized remains of one of the Empty. It emits a soft white glow.

1724.name: Soulflayer Robe
1724.desc: A long silk robe once worn by a soulflayer. Cannot be equipped by people.

1725.name: Snow Lily
1725.desc: This rare type of high-altitude lily blooms even in the harshest of conditions.

1726.name: Egret Fishing Rod
1726.desc: The Fishermen's Guild no longer carries this high-quality fishing rod. Unfortunately, this one is broken.

1727.name: Gar. Teak Lumber
1727.desc: A processed piece of lumber made from a rare tree only found on the southern island of Garhada.

1728.name: Ordrynite
1728.desc: This legendary ore is rumored to harbor an unimaginable amount of energy. When held up to the light, it glows brightly.

1729.name: Moblin Hotrok
1729.desc: When rubbed, this smooth stone becomes unbearably hot. Essential for cooking and heating in the areas of Movalpolos where the use of flammable materials is restricted.

1730.name: Polar Flowerpot
1730.desc: This pot is specially designed to slow the growth of the polar fir.

1731.name: Knightsbells
1731.desc: The knights of early San d'Oria used to attach these tiny bells to their chocobo saddles.

1732.name: Rimed Sapling
1732.desc: This tiny tree has been covered in a beautiful layer of ice.

1733.name: Ice Bauble
1733.desc: Ice carved into a perfect sphere.

1734.name: Blue Bandeau
1734.desc: A sparkling blue band of velvet.

1735.name: Khue Pie
1735.desc: A meat pie made by the world-famous ranger Khue Ronchab. While delicious when hot, its flavor is comparable to a sweaty mocassin when cool.

1736.name: Mehiruru Pop
1736.desc: A tasty frozen snack invented by the black mage Mehiruru. Unfortunately, it melts at a very low temperature.

1737.name: Jade Bauble
1737.desc: Jade carved into a perfect sphere.

1738.name: Shakudo Ingot
1738.desc: This variety of blood-red copper is found in the Far East.

1739.name: Balloon Cloth
1739.desc: This sturdy variety of cotton cloth was once used in Bastok when building hot-air balloons.

1741.name: H.Q. Eft Skin
1741.desc: This eft skin is in perfect condition.

1742.name: Kiddie Present
1742.desc: The quickest way to a child's heart.

1743.name: Kiddie Present
1743.desc: The quickest way to a child's heart.

1744.name: Kiddie Present
1744.desc: The quickest way to a child's heart.

1745.name: Kiddie Present
1745.desc: The quickest way to a child's heart.

1746.name: Thankful Letter
1746.desc: This letter, written by a child, is addressed to a "smilebringer."

1747.name: Grateful Letter
1747.desc: This letter, written by a child, is addressed to a "smilebringer."

1748.name: Joyful Letter
1748.desc: This letter, written by a child, is addressed to a "smilebringer."

1749.name: Ebisu Card
1749.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a ranger reeling in a pugil.

1750.name: Bishamon Card
1750.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a fierce warrior clad in Far Eastern armor.

1751.name: Hotei Card
1751.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a plump monk wearing fancy Far Eastern garments.

1752.name: Benten Card
1752.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a voluptuous bard.

1753.name: Daikoku Card
1753.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a hefty white mage carrying a massive hammer.

1754.name: Jurojin Card
1754.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is an aged beastmaster and his pet sheep.

1755.name: Fukurokuju Card
1755.desc: A trading card sold by the Tenshodo. On one side is a long-faced summoner carrying a staff.

1756.name: Cerebrator Rem.
1756.desc: Ethereal residue from the body of a cerebrator.

1757.name: Coveter Remnant
1757.desc: Ethereal residue from the body of a coveter.

1758.name: Satiator Remnant
1758.desc: Ethereal residue from the body of a satiator.

1759.name: Rec. of Suffering
1759.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1760.name: Rec. of Animosity
1760.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1761.name: Rec. of Anxiety
1761.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1763.name: Viridian Urushi
1763.desc: This variety of blue-green urushi is widely used in the Far East.

1764.name: Kejusu Satin
1764.desc: Made from a blend of wool and cotton, this Far Eastern cloth is extremely smooth and lustrous.

1765.name: Habu Skin
1765.desc: This molt comes from the deadly habu snake found in the Far East.

1767.name: Eltoro Leather
1767.desc: A worn square of leather made from the hide of a legendary bull.

1768.name: Rheiyoh Leather
1768.desc: A square of leather made from the skin of a variety of deer found in countries to the south.

1769.name: Galateia
1769.desc: Bleached white, this durable cloth is used in garments popular in the Far West.

1770.name: Oversized Fang
1770.desc: The incisor of a very large creature.

1772.name: Super Cermet
1772.desc: Alchemy is used to synthesize these chunks of extra-super-hard material.

1773.name: Dark Scales
1773.desc: These scales are fashioned from pure darksteel.

1774.name: Red Gravel
1774.desc: These marble-sized reddish-brown rocks crumble quite easily.

1775.name: Brimsand
1775.desc: A powerful type of firesand enhanced with anima.

1776.name: Spaghetti
1776.desc: Long, thick strands of pasta made from Konschtat semolina.

1777.name: Flaxen Pouch
1777.desc: Jakaka gave you this bag and told you to travel to the top of Parradamo Tor. Are you supposed to collect something...?

1778.name: Parradamo Stones
1778.desc: Jakaka's flaxen pouch is filled with tiny pumice pebbles gathered from the top of Parradamo Tor.

1779.name: Cotton Pouch
1779.desc: Zebada gave you this bag to collect ice from a certain subterranean spring.

1780.name: Chamnaet Ice
1780.desc: Zebada's cotton pouch is filled with ice taken from Chamnaet Spring.

1781.name: Sylvan Stone
1781.desc: A beautiful green stone said to smell like the forest.

1782.name: Florid Stone
1782.desc: A beautiful red stone said to smell like stale blood.

1783.name: Luminian Tissue
1783.desc: A mass of tasteless, odorless, transparent Luminian cells.

1784.name: Phuabo Organ
1784.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as a phuabo. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the body.

1785.name: Xzomit Organ
1785.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as a xzomit. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the body.

1786.name: Aern Organ
1786.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as an aern. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the body.

1787.name: Hpemde Organ
1787.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as a hpemde. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the body.

1788.name: Yovra Organ
1788.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as a yovra. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the body.

1789.name: Chocopass
1789.desc: This "free ride" ticket is part of a campaign to give novice adventurers a taste of chocobo riding.

1790.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1791.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.
