1812.desc: A fully suspended memory from one of the Empty.

1813.name: Vrml. Lacquer
1813.desc: This variety of stunning red paint originates in the Far East.

1814.name: Ethrl. Vermilion
1814.desc: Vermilion lacquer enhanced with anima to give it more luster.

1815.name: Sanctified Lbr.
1815.desc: Ancient lumber enhanced with anima to make it more durable.

1817.name: Cactus Arm
1817.desc: The arm of a cactus native to the Altepa Desert.

1818.name: Euvhi Organ
1818.desc: An organ taken from the body of a Luminian being known as a euvhi. It still seems to function, even though it has been separated from the entity.

1819.name: Luminion Chip
1819.desc: A smooth piece of remarkably lightweight tissue taken from the body of a Luminion.

1820.name: T.M.: Fortitude
1820.desc: Battle tactics for adventuring fellows are written on this parchment. This scroll details the core principles of defense.

1821.name: Atst. of Invuln.
1821.desc: This piece of papyrus certifies that you have mastered the shield.

1822.name: Supernal Fragment
1822.desc: A fragment from a forbidden shield.

1823.name: Shrimp Lantern
1823.desc: A tin-plated toy that resembles a shrimp. When placed in water, lighting the candle on its back will propel it.

1824.name: Haunted Muleta
1824.desc: This brilliant red cape used in ram fighting seems to be possessed with an unruly spirit.

1825.name: Antlion Trap
1825.desc: Originally designed by the Antica, this powerful trap, made from an antlion jaw, is extremely effective in capturing all types of antlions.

1826.name: Opal Silk
1826.desc: The color of this strange thread changes depending on the angle from which one views it.

1827.name: Premium Bag
1827.desc: No expense was spared in making this luxurious handbag. However, while its appearance may be appealing, its lack of storage space makes it one of the least practical bags on the market.

1828.name: Red Grs. Thread
1828.desc: Thread spun from red moko grass fiber.

1829.name: Red Grass Cloth
1829.desc: Cloth woven from red grass fiber thread.

1830.name: Lugworm Sand
1830.desc: Nutritionally enriched sand perfect for raising plump, juicy lugworms.

1831.name: Worm Mulch
1831.desc: Nutritionally enriched soil perfect for raising plump, juicy little worms.

1832.name: Bkn. Hume Rod
1832.desc: This Hume fishing rod is broken and unusable.

1833.name: Bkn. Halcyon Rod
1833.desc: This halcyon fishing rod is broken and unusable.

1834.name: Ardent Jadeite
1834.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this jadeite radiates a strangely inimical energy.

1835.name: Vivified Mythril
1835.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this mythril has been given a life force of its own.

1836.name: Marble
1836.desc: A beautifully streaked slab of limestone.

1837.name: Saber Shoot
1837.desc: This sturdy yet supple branch gets its name from its sharp tip. The slight scent of blood can also be detected on it...

1838.name: Bal Shell
1838.desc: A beautifully decorated shell widely used to make accessories.

1839.name: T.M.: Might
1839.desc: Battle tactics for adventuring fellows are written on this parchment. This scroll details the core principles of offense.

1840.name: Semolina
1840.desc: Coarsely ground wheat processed in the Konschtat Highlands.

1841.name: Unicorn Horn
1841.desc: A horn from the mythical unicorn.

1842.name: Cloud Evoker
1842.desc: A stone once used in Far Eastern rainbringing rituals.

1843.name: Spectral Crimson
1843.desc: This provokingly red cloth reeks of the underworld.

1844.name: Spect. Goldenrod
1844.desc: This subtle yellow cloth may harbor something otherworldly.

1845.name: Red Moko Grass
1845.desc: An uncommon variety of moko grass. Fiber taken from its stem is used as a material for red grass thread.

1846.name: Pumimi Doll
1846.desc: Nyumomo's triplet sister from the outrageously popular "Nyumomo the Twirling Tarutaru" comics. Quiet and reserved, but secretly dreams of becoming a world-famous goldsmith.

1847.name: Fifth Virtue
1847.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1848.name: Fourth Virtue
1848.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1849.name: Sixth Virtue
1849.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1850.name: First Virtue
1850.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1851.name: Deed of Placidity
1851.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1852.name: H.Q. Phuabo Org.
1852.desc: A well-preserved organ taken from the body of a phuabo. Thousands of sparkling particles continue to emit from the glowing tissue.

1853.name: Second Virtue
1853.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1854.name: Deed of Moder.
1854.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1855.name: H.Q. Xzomit Organ
1855.desc: A well-preserved organ taken from the body of a xzomit. Thousands of sparkling particles continue to emit from the glowing tissue.

1856.name: Third Virtue
1856.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1857.name: Cordial Invite
1857.desc: An invitation to a secret meeting. The seals of San d'Oria, Bastok, and Windurst are stamped on the back.

1858.name: Moblumin Ingot
1858.desc: An ingot of the Moblins' lightweight aluminum alloy.

1860.name: Moblin Putty
1860.desc: An adhesive putty made using the Moblins' traditional recipe.

1861.name: Moblin Sheepskin
1861.desc: Beautiful curls of fluffy wool adorn this tough skin.

1862.name: M. Sheep Lth.
1862.desc: Thin but tough Moblin sheep leather.

1863.name: M. Sheep Wool
1863.desc: Curly wool harvested from Moblin sheep.

1864.name: H.Q. Antlion Jaw
1864.desc: This antlion jaw is in perfect condition.

1865.name: Orc Cutting
1865.desc: A leather cutting used when making an Orc helm.

1866.name: Quadav Parts
1866.desc: A set of parts used when making a Quadav barbut.

1867.name: Yagudo Cutting
1867.desc: A cloth cutting used when making a Yagudo headdress.

1868.name: Goblin Cutting
1868.desc: A leather cutting used when making a Goblin coif.

1869.name: Lamia Skin
1869.desc: An expensive, silky skin shed by a Lamia.

1870.name: Deed of Sensib.
1870.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

1871.name: H.Q. Hpemde Org.
1871.desc: A well-preserved organ taken from the body of a hpemde. Thousands of sparkling particles continue to emit from the glowing tissue.

1872.name: Ghrah M Chip
1872.desc: A smooth piece of tissue taken from a Luminion known as a ghrah. A strange light is emitted from its sparkling center.

1873.name: Brigand's Chart
1873.desc: This glass bottle contains a chart drawn by a brigand. There is a mark on Khoonta Dunes, on the Buburimu Peninsula.

1874.name: Pirate's Chart
1874.desc: This glass bottle contains a chart drawn by a pirate. There is a mark on Whitebone Sands, in the Valkurm Dunes.

1875.name: Anct. Beastcoin
1875.desc: A gold coin minted by the Quadav long ago. Far more ornate than modern currency, it consists of nearly pure gold, and a diamond fitted into its center.

1876.name: T.M.: Endurance
1876.desc: Battle tactics for adventuring fellows are written on this parchment. This scroll details the core principles of healing magic.

1877.name: Fomor Codex
1877.desc: This aged book is said to contain the tragic history of the Fomors. However, you cannot read the cryptic characters that fill the pages.

1878.name: Air Tank
1878.desc: This tank made from brass sheets is mainly used to store gasses.

1879.name: Vivio Platinum
1879.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this platinum ingot has regenerative power.

1880.name: Clustered Tar
1880.desc: A thick, black liquid produced by cluster-type monsters.

1881.name: Shield Bug
1881.desc: When viewed from above, this beetle-like insect resembles a small shield. However, beware when handling one, as they release a noxious odor if agitated.

1882.name: Flaxseed Oil
1882.desc: A yellow oil produced by pressing the seeds of the flax flower.

1883.name: Shell Powder
1883.desc: Seashells ground into a fine, white powder.

1884.name: Zinc Oxide
1884.desc: A white powder formed by applying extreme heat to zinc ore.

1885.name: Zincite
1885.desc: This natural mineral forms deep below Vana'diel's surface.

1886.name: Sieglinde Putty
1886.desc: An adhesive putty invented by the alchemist Sieglinde. It is often used to fill cracks and prevent leaking of water and air.

1888.name: Silica
1888.desc: A brown-colored sand made up primarily of tiny quartz particles.

1890.name: River Foliage
1890.desc: A select variety of freshwater plants, freeze-dried for storage. Can be revived by placing in water.

1891.name: Sea Foliage
1891.desc: A select variety of saltwater plants, freeze-dried for storage. Can be revived by placing in water.

1892.name: Desalinator
1892.desc: When applied to normal water, this special blend of chemicals developed by the Alchemists' Guild adjusts the solution's pH to match that of water found in lakes and streams.

1893.name: Salinator
1893.desc: When applied to normal water, this special blend of chemicals developed by the Alchemists' Guild adjusts the solution's salinity and pH to match that of water found in the ocean.

1894.name: Goldfish Set
1894.desc: This set contains all you need to keep your pet goldfish happy.

1895.name: Freshwater Set
1895.desc: This set contains all you need to keep your freshwater-dwelling pets happy.

1896.name: Saltwater Set
1896.desc: This set contains all you need to keep your saltwater-dwelling pets happy.

1897.name: Hopipi Doll
1897.desc: Nyumomo's triplet sister from the ludicrously popular "Nyumomo the Twirling Tarutaru" comics. Loves casting Firaga and making mischief.

1898.name: Fresh Blood
1898.desc: The stench of this blood is unbearable.

1899.name: H.Q. Euvhi Organ
1899.desc: A well-preserved organ taken from the body of a euvhi. Thousands of sparkling particles continue to emit from the glowing tissue.

1900.name: H.Q. Aern Organ
1900.desc: A well-preserved organ taken from the body of an aern. Thousands of sparkling particles continue to emit from the glowing tissue.

1902.desc: A suspended memory of a Lumorian.

2156.desc: This top-quality tea is a specialty of Aht Urhgan.

2158.name: Hydra Fang
2158.desc: An extremely long and poisonous hydra fang.

2159.name: Qutrub Bandage
2159.desc: One of the bandages that cover the qutrubs' entire bodies. It is soaked in embalming fluid and an assortment of other unappetizing liquids.

2160.name: Troll Pauldron
2160.desc: A bronze shoulderpiece from the armor of a Troll cannoneer. It is too heavy for a person to equip.

2161.name: Troll Vambrace
2161.desc: A bronze armpiece from the armor of a Troll hoplite. It is too heavy for a person to equip.

2162.name: Mamool Ja Helmet
2162.desc: An iron helmet formerly worn by a Mamool Ja fighter. Due to its odd shape, only Mamool Ja can wear it.

2163.name: Imp Wing
2163.desc: This tiny wing continues to flap desperately, even after being severed from the imp's body.

2164.name: Peph. Hive Chip
2164.desc: Part of a hive formed by hardened pephredo secretions.

2165.name: Qutrub Gorget
2165.desc: This gorget once decorated the neck of a Qutrub. It is too heavy for a person to equip.

2166.name: Marid Hair

2167.name: Lamian Armlet
2167.desc: This armlet once decorated a Lamia's wrist. It would not fit on a person.

2168.name: Cerberus Claw

2169.name: Cerberus Hide
2169.desc: The hide of the great beast Cerberus. All who gaze upon it are filled with a sense of dread.

2170.name: Cerber. Leather

2171.name: Colibri Beak

2172.name: Hydra Scale

2173.name: Wam. Cocoon
2173.desc: A cocoon made by a wamoura larva. Used as an ingredient for making wamoura silk.

2175.name: Flan Meat

2317.desc: This large chocobo egg is covered by a thick shell. It is a bit warm to the touch.

2319.name: Chocobo Egg

2321.name: Ryacoco Doll
2321.desc: Nyumomo's cousin from the outrageously popular "Nyumomo the Twirling Tarutaru" comics. Twin sister of Myucoco. Short-tempered, but loves to fish.

2330.desc: A beautiful sash said to have been a part of the legendary archer Yoichi's suit of armor.

2331.name: Blue M. Testimony
2331.desc: This papyrus scroll reads: "The bearer of this document has  proven excellence in the ways of  the blue mage."

2332.name: Cor. Testimony
2332.desc: This papyrus scroll reads: "The bearer of this document has  proven excellence in the ways of  the corsair."

2333.name: Pup. Testimony
2333.desc: This papyrus scroll reads: "The bearer of this document has  proven excellence in the ways of  the puppetmaster."

2335.name: Soulfl. Tentacle
2335.desc: A tentacle cut from the body of a soulflayer. It still twitches occasionally.

2346.name: Tornado Salad
2346.desc: A super deluxe salad created especially for chocobos.

2348.desc: An ice-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Enhances magic accuracy

2379.desc: All-natural dye used to color a chocobo's feathers yellow.

2380.name: Red Chocobo Dye
2380.desc: All-natural dye used to color a chocobo's feathers red.

2381.name: Blue Chocobo Dye
2381.desc: All-natural dye used to color a chocobo's feathers blue.

2382.name: Grn. Chocobo Dye
2382.desc: All-natural dye used to color a chocobo's feathers green.

2383.name: Blk. Chocobo Dye
2383.desc: All-natural dye used to color a chocobo's feathers black.

2384.name: Smoky Flask
2384.desc: A cerulean gas swirls within this stopped flask. A label on it reads "Danger! Extremely Flammable."

2385.name: Moldy Buckler
2385.desc: A shield once worn by Gigas champion Alkyoneus, originally taken from one of his past victims. It has seen much wear and cannot be equipped.

2386.name: Hoary Battle Horn
2386.desc: The horn of a colossal wyrm carved into a battle horn by Gigas champion Pallas. It is too large to sound.

2387.name: Flickering Lantern
2387.desc: A gilt lantern treasured by the fiend Sozu Rogberry. The eerie flame burning inside emits a surreal radiance.

2388.name: Diorite
2388.desc: Supposedly a piece broken from the legendary beast Ullikummi. Flecks of mica embedded in the stone sparkle when held to the light.

2389.name: Speed Apple
2389.desc: A ripe faerie apple dipped in rich honey. Use it during a chocobo race to temporarily increase the speed of your steed.

2390.name: Stamina Apple
2390.desc: A ripe faerie apple dipped in creamy yogurt. Use it during a chocobo race to temporarily increase the stamina of your steed.

2391.name: Shadow Apple
2391.desc: A ripe faerie apple dipped in aromatic coffee. Use it during a chocobo race to temporarily increase the evasion of your steed.

2392.name: Pepper Biscuit
2392.desc: A biscuit laced with a ridiculous amount of black pepper, guaranteed to cause anyone who consumes it to have a sneezing fit.  When used during a chocobo race...

2393.name: Fire Biscuit
2393.desc: A biscuit laced with a ridiculous number of hot peppers, guaranteed to set on fire the mouth of anyone who consumes it.  When used during a chocobo race...

2394.name: Gysahl Bomb
2394.desc: A prank firework that emits a thick gas smelling remarkably similar to gysahl greens.  When used during a chocobo race...

2395.name: Spore Bomb
2395.desc: A prank firework that releases a thick gas containing the spores of danceshrooms.  When used during a chocobo race...

2396.name: Fairwth. Fetish
2396.desc: A doll used in Far Eastern countries to implore the spirits of nature for fair weather.  When offered during a chocobo race...

2397.name: Foulwth. Frog
2397.desc: A frog used in Far Eastern countries to implore the spirits of nature for rain.  When offered during a chocobo race...

2398.name: Chocobo Taping
2398.desc: Supports a chocobo's limbs, allowing for optimum muscle management.  When equipped during a race, increases a chocobo's strength.

2399.name: Chocobo Blnk.
2399.desc: Blocks a chocobo's side vision, allowing the bird to concentrate on the track ahead.  When equipped during a race, increases a chocobo's endurance.

2400.name: Shadow Roll
2400.desc: Blocks a chocobo's view of the ground directly below it, allowing the bird to concentrate on the track ahead.  When equipped during a race, increases a chocobo's discernment.

2401.name: Chocobo Hood
2401.desc: Covers a chocobo's ears, thus blocking out surrounding noise and allowing the bird to concentrate on the track.  When equipped during a race, increases a chocobo's receptivity.

2402.name: CRA Racing Form
2402.desc: A document distributed by the VCS authorizing the participation of a chocobo in a CRA-sanctioned race.

2403.name: R Chocotrain: STR
2403.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of race chocobo strength conditioning.

2404.name: R Chocotrain: END
2404.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of race chocobo endurance conditioning.

2405.name: R Chocotrain: DSC
2405.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of race chocobo discernment conditioning.

2406.name: R Chocotrain: RCP
2406.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of race chocobo receptivity conditioning.

2407.name: R Chocotrain
2407.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of basic race chocobo conditioning.

2408.name: Flocon-de-mer
2408.desc: This flaky coral fragment is a beautiful milky white color.

2409.name: Flame Holder
2409.desc: A fire-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Converts the energy of a  　Fire Maneuver into attack  　power during weapon skills  　However, this adds burden to Fire Maneuvers

2410.name: Ice Maker
2410.desc: An ice-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Converts the energy of an Ice  　Maneuver into magic attack power  　when casting magic spells  　However, this adds burden to Ice 　Maneuvers

2411.name: Replicator
2411.desc: A wind-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・When HP is low, converts all  　Wind Maneuvers into copy images  　However, this adds burden to Wind Maneuvers

2412.name: Hammermill
2412.desc: An earth-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases the damage  　inflicted by Shield Bash  ・May also induce "Slow" effect

2413.name: Coiler
2413.desc: A thunder-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Occasionally grants the automaton  　bonus melee attacks

2414.name: Steam Jacket
2414.desc: A water-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Reduces damage taken from  　successive attacks of the same type

2415.name: Turbo Charger II
2415.desc: A wind-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Adds "Haste" effect

2416.name: Vivi-valve II
2416.desc: A light-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases "Cure" potency

2417.name: Aht Urhgan Brass
2417.desc: This ore contains traces of the metal Aht Urhgan brass.

2418.name: A.U. Brass Ingot
2418.desc: An ingot of refined Aht Urhgan brass.

2419.name: A.U Brass Sheet
2419.desc: Aht Urhgan brass forged into a sheet.

2420.name: Luminous Shell
2420.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this crab shell emits a powerful electric charge.

2421.name: Spirit Shell
2421.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the hardness of this turtle shell has more than doubled.

2422.name: Mtl. Mesh Sheet
2422.desc: This mythril sheet has been transformed to a net-like mesh by the power of anima.

2423.name: Frigid Skin
2423.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the temperature of this raptor skin has been pushed well below freezing.

2424.name: Incomb. Wool
2424.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this wool is incredibly fire-resistant.

2425.name: Humid. Velvet
2425.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, this velvet is capable of retaining a large amount of moisture.

2426.name: Wivre Horn
2426.desc: The massive horn of a wivre.

2427.name: Wivre Maul
2427.desc: The tip of a wivre tail. Often called a "maul" due to its striking resemblance to the popular melee weapon.

2428.name: Wivre Hide
2428.desc: The thick hide of a wivre. It is said that not even the sharpest of blades can penetrate this adamantine skin.

2429.name: P.Abjuration: Hd.
2429.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   phantasmal pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying a cursed helm.

2430.name: P.Abjuration: Bd.
2430.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   phantasmal pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying a cursed breastplate.

2431.name: P.Abjuration: Hn.
2431.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   phantasmal pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed gauntlets.

2432.name: P.Abjuration: Lg.
2432.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   phantasmal pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed cuishes.

2433.name: P.Abjuration: Ft.
2433.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   phantasmal pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed sabatons.

2434.name: H.Abjuration: Hd.
2434.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   hadean pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying a cursed hat.

2435.name: H.Abjuration: Bd.
2435.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   hadean pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying a cursed coat.

2436.name: H.Abjuration: Hn.
2436.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   hadean pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed cuffs.

2437.name: H.Abjuration: Lg.
2437.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   hadean pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed trews.

2438.name: H.Abjuration: Ft.
2438.desc: The inscription reads:  "In accordance with the ancient   hadean pact, may the bane be   lifted from thy forbidden artifact." This prayer is necessary in purifying cursed clogs.

2439.name: Cursed Helm
2439.desc: Gold ornaments decorate this darksteel helm. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon it, the helm cannot be equipped.

2440.name: Cursed Helm -1
2440.desc: Gold ornaments decorate this luxurious darksteel helm. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon it, the helm cannot be equipped.

2441.name: C. Breastplate
2441.desc: Gold ornaments decorate this darksteel breastplate. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon it, the breastplate cannot be equipped.

2442.name: C. Breastplate -1
2442.desc: Gold ornaments decorate this luxurious darksteel breastplate. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon it, the breastplate cannot be equipped.

2443.name: Cursed Gauntlets
2443.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these darksteel gauntlets. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the gauntlets cannot be equipped.

2444.name: C. Gauntlets -1
2444.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these luxurious darksteel gauntlets. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the gauntlets cannot be equipped.

2445.name: Cursed Cuishes
2445.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these darksteel cuishes. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the cuishes cannot be equipped.

2446.name: Cursed Cuishes -1
2446.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these luxurious darksteel cuishes. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the cuishes cannot be equipped.

2447.name: Cursed Sabatons
2447.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these darksteel sabatons. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the sabatons cannot be equipped.

2448.name: C. Sabatons -1
2448.desc: Gold ornaments decorate these luxurious darksteel sabatons. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the sabatons cannot be equipped.

2449.name: Cursed Hat
2449.desc: This hat has been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon it, the hat cannot be equipped.

2450.name: Cursed Hat -1
2450.desc: This luxurious hat has been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon it, the hat cannot be equipped.

2451.name: Cursed Coat
2451.desc: This coat has been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon it, the coat cannot be equipped.

2452.name: Cursed Coat -1
2452.desc: This luxurious coat has been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon it, the coat cannot be equipped.

2453.name: Cursed Cuffs
2453.desc: These cuffs have been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the cuffs cannot be equipped.

2454.name: Cursed Cuffs -1
2454.desc: These luxurious cuffs have been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the cuffs cannot be equipped.

2455.name: Cursed Trews
2455.desc: These trews have been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the trews cannot be equipped.

2456.name: Cursed Trews -1
2456.desc: These luxurious trews have been woven of the finest platinum silk. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the trews cannot be equipped.

2457.name: Cursed Clogs
2457.desc: These clogs have been decorated with the finest platinum silk. However, due to a curse that has been placed upon them, the clogs cannot be equipped.

2458.name: Cursed Clogs -1
2458.desc: These luxurious clogs have been decorated with the finest platinum silk. However, due to a terrible curse that has been placed upon them, the clogs cannot be equipped.

2459.name: Minium
2459.desc: A variety of powdered red lead.

2460.name: F. Glass Sheet
2460.desc: A high quality sheet of extremely pliable glass.

2461.name: Rusty Medal
2461.desc: An old Republican Iron Medal. The inscription on the back reads: "863 C.E. Xarcabard."

2462.name: Frayed Arrow
2462.desc: A red string has been wrapped around the shaft of this worn arrow.

2463.name: Colorful Hair
2463.desc: This tuft consists of hair of several colors and textures.

2464.name: Carbon Dioxide
2464.desc: Colorless and odorless carbon dioxide gas.

2465.name: Rubber Cap
2465.desc: This inner cap is made of an extremely resilient rubber.

2466.name: Rubber Harness
2466.desc: This inner harness is made of an extremely resilient rubber.

2467.name: Rubber Gloves
2467.desc: These inner gloves are made of an extremely resilient rubber.

2468.name: Rubber Chausses
2468.desc: These inner chausses are made of an extremely resilient rubber.

2469.name: Rubber Soles
2469.desc: These inner soles are made of an extremely resilient rubber.

2470.name: Nethereye Chain
2470.desc: Chain made from metal forged in the Underworld.

2471.name: Netherfield Chain
2471.desc: Chain made from metal forged in the Underworld.

2472.name: Netherspirit Chain
2472.desc: Chain made from metal forged in the Underworld.

2473.name: Nethercant Chain
2473.desc: Chain made from metal forged in the Underworld.

2474.name: Netherpact Chain
2474.desc: Chain made from metal forged in the Underworld.

2475.name: Kaolin
2475.desc: High quality clay used in making ceramics.

2476.name: Platinum Silk
2476.desc: Smooth silk wrapped around a fine thread of pure platinum.

2477.name: Soul Plate
2477.desc: The image of a monster has been superimposed on this glass plate.

2478.name: Soul Reflector
2478.desc: Reflects the image imposed on a soul plate, calling forth the recorded monster.

2479.name: Chocobet Ticket
2479.desc: This CRA-issued ticket is used for selecting the order in which chocobos will place in races. Winning tickets should be taken to the nearest CRA outlet.

2480.name: Gauger Plate
2480.desc: The fearsome image of a fiend's soul has been burned on this plate with a Soulgauger SGR-1.

2481.name: Comp. Certificate
2481.desc: A document distributed certifying the participation of your chocobo in a CRA-sanctioned race. Necessary for collecting any reward money.

2482.name: Chocotrain: STR
2482.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of chocobo strength conditioning.

2483.name: Chocotrain: END
2483.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of chocobo endurance conditioning.

2484.name: Chocotrain: DSC
2484.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of chocobo discernment conditioning.

2485.name: Chocotrain: RCP
2485.desc: This VCS-issued token can be exchanged for 1 (one) session of chocobo receptivity conditioning.

2486.name: Chocobuck Slip
2486.desc: Exchange this ticket for chocobucks at any VCS Chocobuck Exchange Depot.

2487.name: M. Camp Entry
2487.desc: A form permitting entry to a top- secret mercenary training camp. The Salaheem's Sentinels logo can be seen when held to a light.

2488.name: Alexandrite
2488.desc: A rare and precious gem, the color of which shifts between various hues of green and red depending on the light.

2489.name: W. Areuhat
2489.desc: A full-color transcription of the famous Near Eastern literary classic. Tells the story of a bold young swordswoman and her adventures while seeking out the wyrms of Vana'diel.

2490.name: Forbidden Key
2490.desc: A key from a forgotten time, plucked from a dark chasm of time and space.

2491.name: Leujaoam Log
2491.desc: A blank Assault log given to you by Paparoon. It contains enough room for even the most ambitious of Leujaoam Sanctum exploits to be committed to page in scrupulous detail.

2492.name: Mamool Ja Journal
2492.desc: A blank Assault journal given to you by Paparoon. It contains enough room for even the most ambitious of Mamool Ja Training Grounds exploits to be committed to page in scrupulous detail.

2493.name: Lebros Chronicle
2493.desc: A blank Assault chronicle given to you by Paparoon. It contains enough room for even the most ambitious of Lebros Cavern exploits to be committed to page in scrupulous detail.

2494.name: Periqia Diary
2494.desc: A blank Assault diary given to you by Paparoon. It contains enough room for even the most ambitious of Periqia exploits to be committed to page in scrupulous detail.

2495.name: Ilrusi Ledger
2495.desc: A blank Assault ledger given to you by Paparoon. It contains enough room for even the most ambitious of Ilrusi Atoll exploits to be committed to page in scrupulous detail.

2496.name: Anc. Beast Horn
2496.desc: The gnarled horn of a prehistoric beast, long since extinct.

2497.name: Kukulkan's Skin
2497.desc: The exceptionally pliant and durable skin of Kukulkan. Ideal as a shock-absorbent lining for armor.

2498.name: Briareus's Sash
2498.desc: A prized possession of the legendary Gigas, Briareus, unable to be severed by even the sharpest blade.

2499.name: Regurg. Wing
2499.desc: This partially digested wing retrieved from a glavoid clearly did not agree with the giant worm's stomach.

2500.name: Official Reflector
2500.desc: This soul reflector has been approved for use in all Pankration events. The outline of a monster has been branded onto the back of the reflector.

2501.name: B. Puppet Turban
2501.desc: This turban is for use on Stormwaker-type automatons. Enhances black magic skills.

2502.name: W. Puppet Turban
2502.desc: This turban is for use on Stormwaker-type automatons. Enhances white magic skills.

2503.name: Almond
2503.desc: Seeds of the almond tree found in the Near East.

2504.name: H. Qdv. Chestplate
2504.desc: A durable chestplate worn by the Quadav during battle. It is far too bulky for people to wear.

2505.name: H. Qdv. Backplate
2505.desc: A durable shell worn by the Quadav during battle. It is far too heavy for people to wear.

2506.name: Ladybug Wing
2506.desc: This hard, shell-like wing bears a unique red and black pattern.

2507.name: Lycopodium Flower
2507.desc: This bright red flower has been plucked from the head of a lycopodium.

2509.name: Slug Eye
2509.desc: The remarkably elastic eye stalk of a slug.

2510.name: Orc Helmet
2510.desc: A Goblin-made helmet worn by Orcs. It is far too bulky for people to wear.

2511.name: Orc Pauldron
2511.desc: A Goblin-made shoulderpiece from the armor of an Orc. It is far too heavy for people to wear.

2512.name: Lynx Hide
2512.desc: The colorful hide of a lynx.

2513.name: Rafflesia Vine
2513.desc: A vine cut from a carnivorous rafflesia. The poisonous thorns aid the plant in capturing prey.

2514.name: Rafflesia Petal
2514.desc: The fleshy petal of a carnivorous rafflesia. The hook at its tip is used to render prey immobile.

2515.name: Sandworm Fang
2515.desc: A serrated tooth taken from the maw of a sandworm.

2516.name: Sandworm Shell
2516.desc: A chitinous plate that protects the body of a sandworm.

2517.name: FES Gobbie Key
2517.desc: This slightly rusted key, found in a locked chest on a long-sunken ship, can be used to open the special dial of the Goblin mystery box.

2518.name: Smilodon Hide
2518.desc: The striped hide of a smilodon.

2519.name: Yagudo Osode
2519.desc: A pauldron worn by the Yagudo. Its awkward design makes it impossible for people to wear.

2520.name: Yagudo Kote
2520.desc: A gauntlet worn by the Yagudo. Its awkward design makes it impossible for people to wear.

2521.name: Gnole Claw
2521.desc: The feculent claw of a gnole. These talons are said to harbor a score of infectious diseases.

2522.name: Gnat Wing
2522.desc: The light, yet pliable wing of a giant gnat.

2523.name: Peiste Skin
2523.desc: The elastic skin of a peiste.

2524.name: Peiste Stinger
2524.desc: A poisonous stinger found on the head of a peiste.

2525.name: Bottled Pixie
2525.desc: Did you remember to put holes in the lid?

2526.name: Bkn. MMM Rod
2526.desc: Clearly of questionable durability from the start, this Moblin Maze Mongers fishing rod is now broken and unusable.

2527.name: Red Oven Mitt
2527.desc: Protects little Hume hands from hot pots and pans.

2528.name: Plume d'Or
2528.desc: An attractive brooch comprised of a gold feather and a single pearl.

2531.name: Obsidian
2531.desc: This shiny, black volcanic glass is often used in making weapons.

2532.name: Teak Log
2532.desc: A rough-cut log of teak timber.

2534.name: Jacaranda Log
2534.desc: A rough-cut log of jacaranda timber.

2537.name: Cilice
2537.desc: Cloth woven from wool and dhalmel hair.

2539.name: Dhalmel Hair
2539.desc: The shiny, silken hair of a dhalmel.

2540.name: Magellan Card
2540.desc: A card created by the moogles for the Starlight Celebration. When opened, you can see twinkling stars.

2541.name: Andromeda Card
2541.desc: A card created by the moogles for the Starlight Celebration. When opened, you can see twinkling stars.

2542.name: Goblin Mess Tin
2542.desc: An all-purpose trencher forged from darksteel. 7 out of 10 Goblins don't leave home without one.

2543.name: Goblin Weel
2543.desc: A willow wicker basket used by Goblins to catch fish and eels.

2544.name: Smilodon Collar
2544.desc: An large, leather collar studded with sharp spikes.

2545.name: Lynx Collar
2545.desc: A heavy collar made of gold.

2546.name: Blk. Bolt Heads
2546.desc: The striking tips of black bolts.

2547.name: Obsid. Arrowhd.
2547.desc: The striking tips of obsidian arrows.

2548.name: Gnat Fletchings
2548.desc: Gnat wings that are fitted to the nock end of arrow shafts. They add to the directional stability of arrows in flight.

2549.name: Djinn Ash
2549.desc: The burnt remains of a djinn.

2550.name: Vellum
2550.desc: High-quality parchment made from sheepskin. This particular piece features a decorative border.

2551.name: Electrum Chain
2551.desc: This chain is made of electrum links.

2552.name: Campaign Voucher
2552.desc: A form of Op Credit endowing the holder with the right to participate in Campaign Ops. Issued by the Allied Forces high command.

2553.name: Sanjaku-Tenugui
2553.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Monomi."

2554.name: Asphodel
2554.desc: Known for its pungent aroma, this yellow flower is known throughout San d'Oria as the "Undying Lily."

2555.name: Soshi
2555.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Aisha."

2556.name: Dnc. Testimony
2556.desc: This papyrus scroll reads: "The bearer of this document has  proven excellence in the ways of  the dancer."

2557.name: Sch. Testimony
2557.desc: This papyrus scroll reads: "The bearer of this document has  proven excellence in the ways of  the scholar."

2558.name: Yagudo Glue
2558.desc: A fine grained glue mixed by the Yagudo, used in the repair of pottery.

2559.name: Bonanza Marble
2559.desc: A Mog Bonanza marble inscribed with the numbers which you have selected. Issued by the Mog House Management Union.

2560.name: Fiend Plate
2560.desc: An image captured while engaged in surveillance of the Beastman Confederate has been superimposed onto this glass plate.

2562.name: 108-Knot Quipu
2562.desc: A knotted rope used by the Yagudo to deliver orders. Due to the complexity and number of these knots, the encoded information is indecipherable.

2563.name: Karugo Clay
2563.desc: An extremely high-quality, fine-grained clay originating in the vicinity of Fort Karugo-Narugo.

2564.name: Peiste Pellets
2564.desc: A container of questionably produced feed pellets. The label simply reads, "Peiste." A pungent, fish-like odor emanates from within.

2565.name: Gnole Pellets
2565.desc: A container of questionably produced feed pellets. The label simply reads, "Gnole." The familiar scent of blood emanates from within.

2566.name: Gnat Pellets
2566.desc: A container of questionably produced feed pellets. The label simply reads, "Gnat." The putrid stench of rot and decay emanates from within.

2567.name: Dorter Key
2567.desc: A key to the former living quarters of low-ranking Yagudo initiates, converted to a prison after the outbreak of war.

2568.name: Lthe. Consomme
2568.desc: A light and refreshing vegetable soup that draws on the harmonizing effects of eastern ginger for its aroma and flavor. Standard chocobo fodder.

2569.name: Lethe Potage
2569.desc: A thick and hearty vegetable soup that draws on the harmonizing effects of eastern ginger for its aroma and flavor. Standard chocobo fodder.

2570.name: Pelt of Dawon
2570.desc: The pelt of the legendary tigress Dawon, soft to the touch and beautifully patterned in fiery orange and black.

2571.name: Balrahn's Eyepatch
2571.desc: An eyepatch worn by the celebrated hero of legend, Balrahn. It is regarded as a sacred relic throughout the empire of Aht Urhgan.

2572.name: Pandemonium Key
2572.desc: An elaborately fashioned and ornate key used to gain passage through the Pandemonium Gate.

2573.name: Monkey Wine
2573.desc: A known favorite of the notorious hydra, Tinnin. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the three-headed beast out of hiding.

2574.name: Ferrite
2574.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Armed Gears. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the robotic monstrosity out of hiding.

2575.name: Sheep Botfly
2575.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Mamool Ja, Gotoh Zha the Redolent. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the fiend out of hiding.

2576.name: Olzhiryan Cactus
2576.desc: A known favorite of the notorious wivre, Dea. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the hulking beast out of hiding.

2577.name: Senorita Pamama
2577.desc: A known favorite of the notorious marid, Iriz Ima. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the pugnacious pachyderm out of hiding.

2578.name: Oily Blood
2578.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Lividroot Amooshah. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the dreaded malboro matron out of hiding.

2579.name: Samariri Corpsehair
2579.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Poroggo, Iriri Samariri. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the nefarious amphibian out of hiding.

2580.name: Hellcage Butterfly
2580.desc: A known favorite of the notorious puk, Vulpangue. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the plague-ridden pest out of hiding.

2581.name: Floral Nectar
2581.desc: A known favorite of the notorious colibri, Chamrosh. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the winged menace out of hiding.

2582.name: Rodent Cheese
2582.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Qiqirn, Cheese Hoarder Gigiroon. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the cheesy hedonist out of hiding.

2583.name: Buffalo Corpse
2583.desc: A known favorite of the notorious cerberus, Sarameya. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the cantankerous canine out of hiding.

2584.name: Pure Blood
2584.desc: A known favorite of the notorious vampyr, Nosferatu. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the perpetually bloodlusting undead out of hiding.

2585.name: Vinegar Pie
2585.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Troll, Khromasoul Bhurborlor. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the gigantic oaf out of hiding.

2586.name: Rock Juice
2586.desc: A known favorite of the notorious wamoura, Achamoth. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the maneating moth out of hiding.

2587.name: Raw Buffalo
2587.desc: A known favorite of the notorious dragon, Anantaboga. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the ferocious reptilian out of hiding.

2588.name: Bone Charcoal
2588.desc: A known favorite of the notorious bomb, Reacton. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the enraged explosive out of hiding.

2589.name: Granulated Sugar
2589.desc: A known favorite of the notorious flan, Dextrose. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the gelatinous blob out of hiding.

2590.name: Shadeleaf
2590.desc: A known favorite of the notorious wamoura larva, Brass Borer. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the terrifying termite out of hiding.

2591.name: Pectin
2591.desc: A known favorite of the notorious slime, Claret. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the blubbery mass out of hiding.

2592.name: Cog Lubricant
2592.desc: A known favorite of the notorious automaton, Ob. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the mechanical terror out of hiding.

2593.name: Singed Buffalo
2593.desc: A known favorite of the notorious khimaira, Tyger. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the mythic monster out of hiding.

2594.name: Exorcism Treatise
2594.desc: A known favorite of the notorious soulflayer, Mahjlaef the Paintorn. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the merciless warlock out of hiding.

2595.name: Myrrh
2595.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Experimental Lamia. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the serpentine witch out of hiding.

2596.name: Rose Scampi
2596.desc: A known favorite of the notorious orobon, Nuhn. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the foul fish out of hiding.

2597.name: Opalus Gem
2597.desc: A known favorite of the notorious acrolith, Wulgaru. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the arcane atrocity out of hiding.

2598.name: M. No. 11 Molting
2598.desc: A known favorite of the notorious qutrub, Zareehkl the Jubilant. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the grotesque horror out of hiding.

2599.name: Mint Drop
2599.desc: A known favorite of the notorious imp, Verdelet. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the evil sorcerer out of hiding.

2600.name: Golden Teeth
2600.desc: A known favorite of the notorious skeleton, Velionis. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the bony ghoul out of hiding.

2601.name: Greenling
2601.desc: A known favorite of the notorious Lil' Apkallu. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the murderous avian out of hiding.

2602.name: Spoilt Blood
2602.desc: A known favorite of the notorious chigoe, Chigre. Allegedly irresistible enough to draw the bounding bloodsucker out of hiding.

2603.name: Coin of Birth
2603.desc: This aged bronze coin bears a peculiar glyph on one side, and the image of a sprouting seedling on the other.

2604.name: Coin of Adv.
2604.desc: This aged bronze coin bears a peculiar glyph on one side, and the image of a rising sun on the other.

2605.name: Coin of Glory
2605.desc: This aged bronze coin bears a peculiar glyph on one side, and the image of a rose in full bloom on the other.

2606.name: Coin of Decay
2606.desc: This aged bronze coin bears a peculiar glyph on one side, and the image of a withered leaf on the other.

2607.name: Coin of Ruin
2607.desc: This aged bronze coin bears a peculiar glyph on one side, and the image of a skull on the other.

2608.name: Plat. Chestnut
2608.desc: An aromatic chestnut with a deep brown husk that grows in abundance on the lush grasslands of the La Theine Plateau. Rich in nutrients and quite tasty when roasted.

2609.name: Tinnin's Fang
2609.desc: The lethally toxic fang of the hydra, Tinnin. It continues to drip poison from the venom ducts within.

2610.name: A. Gears' Fragment
2610.desc: A mechanical fragment taken from the disassembled remains of the menacing Armed Gears. It appears to be a generator designed for providing large quantities of power.

2611.name: G. Zha's Necklace
2611.desc: Neckware of the Mamool Ja, Gotoh Zha the Redolent. Forever imbued with the indelible scent of its owner, it can be equipped by none other.

2612.name: Dea's Horn
2612.desc: Taken from the nasal protrusion of the massive wivre Dea, this imposing horn is perfectly adapted for impaling unwary targets.

2613.name: Iriz Ima's Hide
2613.desc: The thick, tough hide of Iriz Ima. Its durability is a testament to its capacity to protect the marid against most forms of predation.

2614.name: Amoosh.'s Tendril
2614.desc: A wreathing, vinelike tendril amputated from the malboro Lividroot Amooshah. The meandering of the vine makes it seem to continue to move, even in death.

2615.name: Iriri Samariri's Hat
2615.desc: The perfectly form-fit hat of Poroggo  Iriri Samariri. Tailor-made to an amphibious head, it cannot and will not adorn the typical adventurer noggin.

2616.name: Vulpangue's Wing
2616.desc: A wing clipping taken from the puk, Vulpangue. Its lightness and thinness belie both its strength and durability.

2617.name: Chamrosh's Beak
2617.desc: The razor-sharp beak of the colibri Chamrosh, capable of easily tearing apart the flesh of most any creature.

2618.name: Gigiroon's Cape
2618.desc: The cape and cowl of Qiqirn, Cheese Hoarder Gigiroon. Fit to the unique form of a Qiqirn body, it cannot be donned by people, and reeks of cheese.

2619.name: Sarameya's Hide
2619.desc: The tough, near-impenetrable hide of Sarameya, one of the few known extant members of the cerberus family.

2620.name: Nosferatu's Claw
2620.desc: Plunged into its victims and then licked clean of the dripping blood, this clawed appendage of the vampyr Nosferatu still possesses a tangible eeriness.

2621.name: Bblr.'s Vambrace
2621.desc: The solid and well-worn plate armguard once donned by the Troll, Khromasoul Bhurborlor. As with all troll armor, it is far too cumbersome to be worn by even the strongest of people.

2622.name: Acham.'s Antenna
2622.desc: An antenna taken from the fallen wamoura, Achamoth. This tentacular appendage is adept at detecting even invisible and silent prey.

2623.name: Anantaboga's Heart
2623.desc: The enormous heart of the dragon, Anantaboga. It is still saturated through and through with foul reptilian blood.

2624.name: Reacton's Ashes
2624.desc: The smoldering remains of the bomb, Reacton. Despite the passage of time, the cinders retain their immense heat.

2625.name: Dextrose's Blubber
2625.desc: The blubberous flesh of the flan, Dextrose. It jiggles incessantly, even when at rest.

2626.name: B. Borer's Cocoon
2626.desc: A cutting taken from the elaborate cocoon spun by the larval wamoura, Brass Borer.

2627.name: Claret Globule
2627.desc: A tiny blob of gelatinous flesh hacked from the body of the slime, Claret.

2628.name: Ob's Arm
2628.desc: A technologically advanced automaton arm, salvaged from the dismantled remains of Ob.

2629.name: Tyger's Tail
2629.desc: Cleaved from the ferocious khimaira, Tyger, this once lethal tail is wielded in both whipping and stabbing motions.

2630.name: Mahjlaef's Staff
2630.desc: Too great in power and size to be wielded by people, this wicked staff of the soulflayer Mahjlaef the Paintorn is no doubt responsible for the deaths of countless adventurers.

2631.name: Ex. Lamia Armband
2631.desc: Decorative and protective armware of Experimental Lamia. It inexplicably resists being equipped on the arm of any other.

2632.name: Nuhn's Esca
2632.desc: The bioluminescent, bulbous sac of the oboron, Nuhn. The light is used to attract unsuspecting prey in the dark depths of cold waters.

2633.name: Wulgaru's Head
2633.desc: The severed head of the acrolith, Wulgaru. It was once animated by a most malicious arcane presence.

2634.name: Zrkl.'s Neckpiece
2634.desc: Necklace formerly worn by the qutrub, Zareehkl the Jubilant. It is not suited to dress the neck of any other individual.

2635.name: Verdelet's Wing
2635.desc: The detached, fleshy wing of the imp, Verdelet. Either by some latent magic, or simply remaining nerves, it twitches periodically.

2636.name: Velionis's Bone
2636.desc: A shard of blackened bone taken from the remains of the wicked skeleton, Velionis.

2637.name: Lil' Apkallu's Egg
2637.desc: A shiny egg laid by Lil' Apkallu. The pale green hue of the eggshell is an unmistakable sign.

2638.name: Chigre
2638.desc: The complete and well-preserved corpse of arguably the rarest member of the chigoe family, Chigre.

2639.name: Hardened Bone
2639.desc: This bone of indeterminate origin is hard enough to pass for solid rock. It would be highly difficult to craft.

2640.name: Murex Spicule
2640.desc: A peculiar spiny protrusion removed from the curious creature known as a murex.

2641.name: Amb. Pseudopod
2641.desc: A mass of some unidentifiable organic substance removed from the curious creature known as an amoeban.

2642.name: Kabenro
2642.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Myoshu."

2643.name: Jinko
2643.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Yurin."

2644.name: Ryuno
2644.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Kakka."

2645.name: Eastern Ginger
2645.desc: An eastern variety of ginger valued for its sharp, refreshing aroma. Believed in the East to possess divine powers of protection, lending to its common usage as a design motif in many coats of arms.

2646.name: Meta. Balsam
2646.desc: A fragrant oil rumored to possess aromas so potent they can alter the very personalities of those who smell them.

2647.name: Meta. Perfume
2647.desc: A fragrant perfume rumored to possess aromas so potent they can alter the very personalities of those who smell them.

2648.name: Ant. Arrowhd.
2648.desc: The striking tips of antlion arrows.

2649.name: Colibri Fltchg.
2649.desc: Colibri feathers that are fitted to the nock end of arrow shafts. They add to the directional stability of arrows in flight.

2650.name: Drk. Bolt Heads
2650.desc: The striking tips of darkling bolts.

2651.name: Fsn. Bolt Heads
2651.desc: The striking tips of fusion bolts.

2652.name: Gigas Helm
2652.desc: The huge headwear of a full-grown Gigas. Incapable of being equipped by any other race, the helmet is designed as much for inducing fear in the enemy as protecting the head of its wearer.

2653.name: Gigas Gauntlets
2653.desc: The massive gauntlets worn by armored Gigas. Far too large or heavy to be worn by people, the lack of skill in craftsmanship is compensated for by sheer enormity and ruggedness.

2654.name: Dark Ixion Horn
2654.desc: The twisted and contorted horn wrested from the head of Dark Ixion. It emits an alluring yet lackluster shine of foreboding blackness.

2655.name: Dark Ixion Tail
2655.desc: The plush, full tail of Dark Ixion. Despite the ominous aura of evil still surrounding the fine hairs, their innate beauty is undeniable.

2656.name: Luminian Thread
2656.desc: A radiant silken thread spun of the finest luminian filaments.

2657.name: Flameshun Cloth
2657.desc: A fire-resistant cloth that has been infused with liquid mythril for enhanced flame retardancy.

2658.name: Silkworm Thread
2658.desc: An exquisite silk specimen produced only by crawlers living in mountainous regions. The especially fine quality is attributed to high altitudes.

2659.name: Canvas Toile
2659.desc: A sturdy watch of cotton canvas known predominantly for its use in the construction of sails for ocean-faring vessels.

2660.name: Pantin Wire
2660.desc: Durable, flexible wiring used in the manipulation of marionettes and various other puppets.

2661.name: Corduroy Cloth
2661.desc: A high-quality swatch of ridged velvet, the cords of which run parallel to one another in a vertical pattern.

2662.name: Mirage Keffiyeh -1
2662.desc: A mirage keffiyeh. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2663.name: Mirage Jubbah -1
2663.desc: A mirage jubbah. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2664.name: Mrg. Bazubands -1
2664.desc: A pair of mirage bazubands. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2665.name: Mirage Shalwar -1
2665.desc: A pair of mirage shalwar. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2666.name: Mirage Charuqs -1
2666.desc: A pair of mirage charuqs. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2667.name: Comm. Tricorne -1
2667.desc: A commodore tricorne. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2668.name: Comm. Frac -1
2668.desc: A commodore frac. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2669.name: Comm. Gants -1
2669.desc: A pair of commodore gants. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2670.name: Comm. Trews -1
2670.desc: A pair of commodore trews. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2671.name: Comm. Bottes -1
2671.desc: A pair of commodore bottes. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2672.name: Pantin Taj -1
2672.desc: A pantin taj. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2673.name: Pantin Tobe -1
2673.desc: A pantin tobe. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2674.name: Ptn. Dastanas -1
2674.desc: A pair of pantin dastanas. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2675.name: Ptn. Churidars -1
2675.desc: A pair of pantin churidars. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2676.name: Ptn. Babouches -1
2676.desc: A pair of pantin babouches. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2677.name: Bovine Brand Card
2677.desc: Officially issued by the M.H.M.U. ranch publisher's press, this card is used to keep track of how many bovine brands you have acquired.

2678.name: Maple Strongbox
2678.desc: A solidly constructed maple lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases funds.

2679.name: Magn. Strongbox
2679.desc: A solidly constructed magnolia lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases resources.

2680.name: Beech Strongbox
2680.desc: A solidly constructed beech lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases supplies.

2681.name: Evrgrn. Strongbox
2681.desc: A solidly constructed evergreen lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases reconnaissance.

2682.name: Holly Strongbox
2682.desc: A solidly constructed holly lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases skill.

2683.name: Oak Strongbox
2683.desc: A solidly constructed oak lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases production.

2684.name: Elm Strongbox
2684.desc: A solidly constructed elm lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases morale.

2685.name: Willow Strongbox
2685.desc: A solidly constructed willow lockbox used by Orcs for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases prosperity.

2686.name: Bronze Letterbox
2686.desc: A heavily reinforced bronze letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases funds.

2687.name: Brass Letterbox
2687.desc: A heavily reinforced brass letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases resources.

2688.name: Shakudo Letterbox
2688.desc: A heavily reinforced shakudo letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases supplies.

2689.name: Paktong Letterbox
2689.desc: A heavily reinforced paktong letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases reconnaissance.

2690.name: Pig Iron Letterbox
2690.desc: A heavily reinforced pig iron letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases skill.

2691.name: Iron Letterbox
2691.desc: A heavily reinforced iron letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases production.

2692.name: Cst. Irn. Letterbox
2692.desc: A heavily reinforced cast iron letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases morale.

2693.name: Wrt. Irn. Letterbox
2693.desc: A heavily reinforced wrought iron letterbox used by Quadav for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases prosperity.

2694.name: Bam. Grass Basket
2694.desc: A tightly woven basket of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases funds.

2695.name: Bam. Med. Basket
2695.desc: A tightly woven basket of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases resources.

2696.name: Bamboo Bugcage
2696.desc: A tightly woven cage of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases supplies.

2697.name: Bam. Flwr. Basket
2697.desc: A tightly woven basket of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases reconnaissance.

2698.name: Bamboo Birdcage
2698.desc: A tightly woven cage of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases skill.

2699.name: Bam. Char. Basket
2699.desc: A tightly woven basket of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases production.

2700.name: Bam. Tea Basket
2700.desc: A tightly woven basket of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases morale.

2701.name: Bam. Snakecage
2701.desc: A tightly woven cage of bamboo strips used by Yagudo for the safekeeping of valuables. Increases prosperity.

2702.name: Buckwheat Flour
2702.desc: A relatively dark flour made by grinding the triangular seeds of the buckwheat plant.

2703.name: Khrom. Leather
2703.desc: A piece of leather made from the hide of a karakul. Khroma has been incorporated into the tanning process, resulting in the distinctive appearance and properties.

2704.name: Oil-Soaked Clth.
2704.desc: A swatch of cloth soaked in oil to enhance its resistance to water.

2705.name: Foulard
2705.desc: A light fabric made of silk which gives off a beautiful, brilliant sheen.

2706.name: Blue Text. Dye
2706.desc: An all-natural, blue dye used in the coloring of cloth.

2707.name: Green Text. Dye
2707.desc: An all-natural, green dye used in the coloring of cloth.

2708.name: Yellow Txt. Dye
2708.desc: An all-natural, yellow dye used in the coloring of cloth.

2709.name: White Text. Dye
2709.desc: An all-natural, white dye used in the coloring of cloth.

2710.name: Soba Noodles
2710.desc: Thin, round, elongated noodles made from buckwheat flour.

2711.name: Khroma Nugget
2711.desc: A small chunk of khroma. Cursory examination reveals that it is not without impurities.

2712.name: Red Textile Dye
2712.desc: An all-natural, red dye used in the coloring of cloth.

2713.name: Dyer's Woad
2713.desc: A tall-growing grass which blooms marvelous flowers of bright yellow.

2714.name: Filet Lace
2714.desc: An exquisite, meticulously stitched piece of lace with a fishnet pattern.

2715.name: Gold Stud
2715.desc: A decorative gold button embossed with a simple design.

2716.name: Brilliantine
2716.desc: A lustrous variety of tweed used as lining in coats and various other types of apparel.

2717.name: Electrum Stud
2717.desc: A decorative electrum button embossed with a simple design.

2718.name: Etoile Tiara -1
2718.desc: An etoile tiara. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2719.name: Etoile Casaque -1
2719.desc: An etoile casaque. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2720.name: Etoile Bangles -1
2720.desc: A pair of etoile bangles. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2721.name: Etoile Tights -1
2721.desc: A pair of etoile tights. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2722.name: Etoile Toe Shoes -1
2722.desc: A pair of etoile toe shoes. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2723.name: Argute M.board -1
2723.desc: An argute mortarboard. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2724.name: Argute Gown -1
2724.desc: An argute gown. It is badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2725.name: Argute Bracers -1
2725.desc: A pair of argute bracers. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2726.name: Argute Pants -1
2726.desc: A pair of argute pants. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2727.name: Argute Loafers -1
2727.desc: A pair of argute loafers. They are badly damaged and can no longer be worn.

2728.name: Cernunnos Bulb
2728.desc: The bulb of the mobile plant known as the Cernunnos. Though currently in stasis, it can grow with the proper nutrients.

2729.name: Hydrangea
2729.desc: An exotic flower that grows only in the Far East, with leafy petals that change hue from deep blue to a rich reddish-violet.

2730.name: Mandragora Cap
2730.desc: A cork cap branded with the face of a mandragora. A Buffalo Bonanza Ranch exclusive. Collect them all!

2731.name: Korrigan Cap
2731.desc: A cork cap branded with the face of a korrigan. A Buffalo Bonanza Ranch exclusive. Collect them all!

2732.name: Lycopodium Cap
2732.desc: A cork cap branded with the face of a lycopodium. A Buffalo Bonanza Ranch exclusive. Collect them all!

2733.name: S. Smil. Leather
2733.desc: Enhanced by the power of anima, the toughness of this piece of smilodon leather is unworldly.

2734.name: MMM Member's Cd.
2734.desc: A crumpled square of parchment haphazardly scrawled with a mishmash of unintelligible glyphs. Apparently used by the MMM Revitalization Team to monitor clients' stress levels.

2735.name: Dk. Ixion Ferrule
2735.desc: A ferrule fashioned from the gnarled horn of Dark Ixion. Long after being separated from its master, it continues to pulse and throb with an otherworldly energy.

2736.name: Kupon A-Lum
2736.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from notorious monsters in Lumoria.

2737.name: Silky Suede
2737.desc: The tough hide of a buffalo has been meticulously napped to create this silky-smooth fabric.

2738.name: Cassiopeia Card
2738.desc: A card created by the moogles for the Starlight Celebration. When opened, you can see twinkling stars.

2739.name: Perseus Card
2739.desc: A card created by the moogles for the Starlight Celebration. When opened, you can see twinkling stars.

2740.name: Seedspall Lux
2740.desc: A shard of the Seed Crystal recovered from Jugner Forest. It blazes with the blinding radiance of a white-hot sun.

2741.name: Seedspall Luna
2741.desc: A shard of the Seed Crystal recovered from the Pashhow Marshlands. It shines with the soft luminescence of an autumn moon.

2742.name: Seedspall Astrum
2742.desc: A shard of the Seed Crystal recovered from the Meriphataud Mountains. It twinkles with the glittering splendor of a sea of stars.

2743.name: Oxblood Orb
2743.desc: An exquisite orb fashioned from the extremely rare variety of blood-red coral known as oxblood.

2744.name: Angel Skin Orb
2744.desc: An exquisite orb fashioned from the extremely rare variety of pinkish coral known as angel skin.

2745.name: Kupon A-DBcd
2745.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for relic equipment from Dynamis - Beaucedine.

2746.name: Kupon A-DXar
2746.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for equipment from Dynamis - Xarcabard.

2747.name: Gargouille Horn
2747.desc: A gargouille's horn, its edge as sharp as tempered steel.

2748.name: Garglle. Shank
2748.desc: A shank of gargouille flesh, hardened by time into something resembling solid rock.

2749.name: Gargouille Eye
2749.desc: The lustrous, crystalline eye of a gargouille.

2750.name: Amphiptere Hide
2750.desc: The glossy, scaly hide of an amphiptere.

2751.name: Amph. Leather
2751.desc: The rough hide of an amphiptere has been processed into a smooth and sturdy square of leather.

2752.name: Amph. Whisker
2752.desc: The bristly, whip-like whisker of an amphiptere.

2753.name: Amphiptere Claw
2753.desc: This razor-sharp claw is still specked with the blood of the amphiptere's unfortunate prey.

2754.name: Ruszor Fang
2754.desc: The massive fang of a ruszor, sharp enough to slice through blocks of solid ice.

2755.name: Ruszor Hide
2755.desc: The ruszor's hide is surprisingly soft and silky to the touch.

2756.name: Ruszor Leather
2756.desc: This sturdy leather made from the hide of a ruszor is particularly resistant to frigid temperatures.

2757.name: Orc. Armor Plate
2757.desc: A metal plate that appears to have fallen off of an Orcish warmachine.

2758.name: Quadav Backscale
2758.desc: A bronze scale donned as added back protection by Quadav warriors.

2759.name: Yagudo Caulk
2759.desc: A traditional adhesive and filling material invented by the Yagudo.

2760.name: Ratstail Explosive
2760.desc: An explosive containing drab, mousy-gray gunpowder, manufactured in the traditional Goblin style.

2761.name: Feyweald Log
2761.desc: A rough-cut log from a tree of the Feyweald, the pixie forest of legend.

2762.name: Feywld. Lumber
2762.desc: Processed lumber from a tree of the Feyweald, the pixie forest of legend.

2763.name: Swamp Ore
2763.desc: This slimy ore, found often in marshlands, contains the metal iron.

2764.name: Dweomer Steel
2764.desc: Smelted by fey magic, this steel ingot is warm to the touch.

2765.name: Fool's Gold Ore
2765.desc: This ore contains bronze, iron, and sulfur. Its luster pales in comparison to genuine gold.

2766.name: Oberon's Gold
2766.desc: An ingot of the purest gold, smelted by the fey magic of Oberon, King of Faeries.

2767.name: Ob. Gold Sheet
2767.desc: A gleaming sheet of the purest gold, smelted by the fey magic of Oberon, King of Faeries.

2768.name: Revertant: Sing.
2768.desc: An acrid-smelling concoction used by synergists to repair defects in various materials. Small in quantity, it appears to be of a very high concentration.

2769.name: Buggane Horn
2769.desc: The pitch-black horn of a buggane, a fearsome ogre summoned by pixies to exact revenge on invaders who threatened their forest home. It glows with an eerie energy.

2770.name: White Butterfly
2770.desc: A magnificent white butterfly with gossamer wings that glisten in the sunlight.

2771.name: Bell Cricket
2771.desc: A colorful species of jumping bug known for its peaceful chirping song.

2772.name: Glowfly
2772.desc: A small, jet-black insect of the beetle family that emits an ethereal yellowish glow from its abdomen.

2773.name: Fine Parchment
2773.desc: Scribes the world over swear by this fine quality sheepskin parchment for the hours of smudge-free writing pleasure it offers.

2774.name: Enchanted Ink
2774.desc: Literally fill your words with magic with this rare variety of enchanted ink.

2775.name: Padded Box
2775.desc: An empty box lined with shock-absorbent material, designed for the transportation of fragile objects. Includes wrapping paper.

2776.name: Pumice Stone
2776.desc: This pumice stone is used to rub away the layer of sheen from animal hide.

2777.name: Magicked Blood
2777.desc: The dark magic infused within this portion of blood causes it to swish about fitfully within its vial.

2778.name: Inferior Cocoon
2778.desc: A low-grade cocoon of unknown origin, unsuitable for commercial use.

2779.name: Enfeeb. Kit: Poison
2779.desc: The enfeeblement kit requested by Doctor Shantotto.

2780.name: Enfeeb. Kit: Blind
2780.desc: The enfeeblement kit requested by Doctor Shantotto.

2781.name: Enfeeb. Kit: Sleep
2781.desc: The enfeeblement kit requested by Doctor Shantotto.

2782.name: Enfeeb. Kit: Silence
2782.desc: The enfeeblement kit requested by Doctor Shantotto.

2783.name: Evolith
2783.desc: An ordinary stone with no distinguishing features evident to the naked eye.

2784.name: Fire Fewell
2784.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2785.name: Ice Fewell
2785.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2786.name: Wind Fewell
2786.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2787.name: Earth Fewell
2787.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2788.name: Lightning Fewell
2788.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2789.name: Water Fewell
2789.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2790.name: Light Fewell
2790.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2791.name: Dark Fewell
2791.desc: A synergy staple, this substance yields potent elemental power when burned.

2792.name: Catalytic Oil
2792.desc: Developed by the Bastokan Alchemists' Guild, this oil can be added to various substances to facilitate combustion.

2793.name: Flamestone
2793.desc: A brilliant red stone that harbors the elemental power of fire.

2794.name: Froststone
2794.desc: A deep blue stone that harbors the elemental power of ice.

2795.name: Galestone
2795.desc: A vivid green stone that harbors the elemental power of wind.

2796.name: Tremorstone
2796.desc: A rich brown stone that harbors the elemental power of earth.

2797.name: Stormstone
2797.desc: A bright violet stone that harbors the elemental power of lightning.

2798.name: Tidestone
2798.desc: A translucent stone that harbors the elemental power of water.

2799.name: Astral Matter
2799.desc: This otherworldly substance seems to warp any light that passes through it.

2800.name: Revertant
2800.desc: An acrid-smelling concoction used by synergists to repair defects in various materials.

2801.name: Kupon AW-Pan
2801.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for items from Pandemonium Warden.

2802.name: Kupon AW-Abs
2802.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from Absolute Virtue.

2803.name: Mordant 15
2803.desc: An acidic substance used by synergists to etch patterns in various materials. Purity: 15 (Usable with items Lv.15 or below)

2804.name: Mordant 30
2804.desc: An acidic substance used by synergists to etch patterns in various materials. Purity: 30 (Usable with items Lv.30 or below)

2805.name: Mordant 45
2805.desc: An acidic substance used by synergists to etch patterns in various materials. Purity: 45 (Usable with items Lv.45 or below)

2806.name: Mordant 60
2806.desc: An acidic substance used by synergists to etch patterns in various materials. Purity: 60 (Usable with items Lv.60 or below)

2807.name: Mordant 75
2807.desc: An acidic substance used by synergists to etch patterns in various materials. Purity: 75 (Usable with items Lv.75 or below)

2808.name: Ebon Pigment
2808.desc: A powerful colorant used by synergists to infuse materials with rich ebony tones.

2809.name: Furia Pigment
2809.desc: A powerful colorant used by synergists to infuse materials with fiery red hues.

2810.name: Ebur Pigment
2810.desc: A powerful colorant used by synergists to infuse materials with the pure white of ivory.

2811.name: Aptant: Primus
2811.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "source."

2812.name: Aptant: Aecus
2812.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "equality."

2813.name: Aptant: Durus
2813.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "durable."

2814.name: Aptant: Bellum
2814.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "war."

2815.name: Aptant: Arkhe
2815.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "beginning."

2816.name: Aptant: Tachus
2816.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "quick."

2817.name: Aptant: Pera
2817.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "far."

2818.name: Aptant: Ishis
2818.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "strength."

2819.name: Aptant: Fyrst
2819.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "first."

2820.name: Aptant: Geoc
2820.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "help."

2821.name: Aptant: Haelan
2821.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "cure."

2822.name: Aptant: Secan
2822.desc: A burnished metallic token bearing an ancient glyph for "attack."

2823.name: Iridium
2823.desc: A rare precious metal that is exceptionally durable and heat-resistant.

2824.name: Shagreen
2824.desc: Unusually durable leather of indeterminate animal origin. Curious bumps dot the surface.

2825.name: Cambric
2825.desc: An elegant woven fabric that sparkles brilliantly under light.

2826.name: Mandra Scale
2826.desc: A charming brass scale in the shape of a mandragora's leaf crown.

2827.name: Rgd. Gld. Thread
2827.desc: This gold thread is sturdy enough to support the weight of even the burliest bugbear.

2828.name: Herensugue Skin
2828.desc: The supple yet resilient skin of Herensugue.

2829.name: Radiant Velvet
2829.desc: This square of velvet is so brilliantly colored as to seem to radiate light.

2830.name: Bastet Fang
2830.desc: The saber-like cuspid of Bastet, sharp enough to pierce even the toughest-skinned prey.

2831.name: Yel. Brass Chain
2831.desc: Of higher purity than ordinary brass, this chain shines a brilliant yellow.

2832.name: Samwell's Shank
2832.desc: The giant leg bone of Slumbering Samwell. If only he could have stayed awake, it might still be attached to his body.

2833.name: Thunder Coral
2833.desc: A piece of coral with sharp, jagged branches that resemble bolts of lightning.

2834.name: Immortal Molt
2834.desc: The molt of a highlander lizard, shed in a climactic battle with another of its kind. There can be only one!

2835.name: Dahu Hair
2835.desc: Hair from the mane of a dahu. No matter how hard you try to straighten it, it always reverts to a twisted, gnarled state.

2836.name: Woolly Pelage
2836.desc: A pelage from a giant ram so thick and fluffy it could keep an adventurer warm on even the coldest nights.

2837.name: Ratatoskr Pelt
2837.desc: The pelt of the elusive Ratatoskr, sought after by many a hunter.

2838.name: Ephemeral Cloth
2838.desc: A piece of cloth so delicate and thin it seems about to fade from existence at any moment.

2839.name: Coquecigr. Skin
2839.desc: The absurdly grotesque skin of the fabled coquecigrue.

2840.name: Imperial Topaz
2840.desc: A resplendent yellow topaz of peerless cut and clarity.

2841.name: Qdv. Silv. Ingot
2841.desc: An ingot of silver refined using Quadav smelting techniques.

2842.name: Flawed Garnet
2842.desc: A garnet so murky and rough that it resembles little more than a misshapen lump of red stone.

2843.name: Lineadach
2843.desc: A particularly elegant square of linen woven by a Fomor.

2844.name: Penumbral Brass
2844.desc: An ingot of brass with oddly patterned streaks of black that appear to shift like shadows.

2845.name: Aramid Fiber
2845.desc: An unnatural-seeming fiber that is curiously resilient given its thin and supple nature.

2846.name: Hefty Oak Lbr.
2846.desc: An exceptionally heavy and sturdy-looking piece of oak lumber.

2847.name: Blue Jasper
2847.desc: A jasper of brilliant blue, so rarely seen that many consider it but a myth.

2848.name: Grimy Brz. Sheet
2848.desc: This bronze sheet is so filthy with dirt and grime that it turns the stomach even to touch it.

2849.name: Likho Talon
2849.desc: The sharp, dagger-like talon of a likho.

2850.name: Sahagin Gold
2850.desc: An ingot smelted from alluvial gold retrieved by the Sahagin from an ancient river bed.

2851.name: Bukktooth
2851.desc: The massive and sturdy molar of a rambukk, worn down from chewing large quantities of cud.

2852.name: Tenebrium
2852.desc: A shadowy black ingot smelted by demons in their netherworldly forge.

2853.name: Malebolge Mand.
2853.desc: A hellishly warped mandrel said to have the power to twist its possessor's soul.

2854.name: Stately Crab Sh.
2854.desc: This massive shell from a rare species of land crab is so hard it feels like metal to the touch.

2855.name: Mahogany Htwd.
2855.desc: The darkest and hardest wood from the center of an ancient mahogany tree.

2856.name: Ensang. Cloth
2856.desc: A bloodstained cloth that belonged to a ghost.

2857.name: T. Tama-hagane
2857.desc: A lump of the purest steel, wrought in a manner taught in a land far to the east.

2858.name: Wolfram Steel
2858.desc: A highly durable steel, refined through methods long since forgotten.

2859.name: Cobalt Ore
2859.desc: Ore containing the lustrous metal cobalt. When processed with other metals, it produces a brilliant blue hue.

2860.name: Plumbago
2860.desc: Metallic in appearance and slick to the touch, this mineral is used to harness and channel the power of lightning.

2861.name: Timeworn Talisman
2861.desc: The years have not been kind to this Ephramadian talisman. An image of the Dark Rider embossed on its surface is barely distinguishable.

2862.name: Kingdom Waystone
2862.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2863.name: Repub. Waystone
2863.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2864.name: Feder. Waystone
2864.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2865.name: Duchy Waystone
2865.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2866.name: Selbina Waystone
2866.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2867.name: Mhaura Waystone
2867.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2868.name: Rabao Waystone
2868.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2869.name: Kazham Waystone
2869.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2870.name: Norg Waystone
2870.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2871.name: Safehd. Waystone
2871.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2872.name: Empire Waystone
2872.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2873.name: Nashm. Waystone
2873.desc: This otherwise nondescript gray stone glows with a faint blue light that feels somehow familiar...

2874.name: Lambent Scale
2874.desc: This delicate scale from the wing of a pixie glows with a soothing light. It is said to have curative powers.

2875.name: Ethereal Squama
2875.desc: A delicate scale from the wing of a pixie, so thin and tenuous as to be almost imperceptible.

2876.name: Muculent Ingot
2876.desc: This ingot of pure gold is coated in a viscous, slimy substance that clings tenaciously to its surface.

2877.name: Befouled Silver
2877.desc: Years spent in the possession of baleful monsters have lent an ill-boding glint to this once-pure precious metal.

2878.name: Scarlet Kadife
2878.desc: Luxurious and smooth to the touch, this fabric is such a deep red as to make one wonder if it were dyed with blood.

2879.name: Igneous Barnacle
2879.desc: Jagged to the touch, this barnacle from the shell of the massive Krabkatoa is coated with a layer of hardened magma.
