2880.name: Kidney Stone
2880.desc: A tawny crystalline stone recovered from the body of the antlion Orcus, quite beautiful despite its unpleasant origin.

2881.name: Eye of Verthandi
2881.desc: A brilliant, flawless gem of otherworldly beauty that was a treasured possession of Verthandi.

2882.name: Baby Blobdingnag
2882.desc: A smallish globule of red slime split off from Blobdingnag. If left alone, will it grow to the size of its parent...?

2883.name: Ruthven's Nail
2883.desc: The dagger-like fingernail of the fearsome vampyr Lord Ruthven, still specked with the blood of one of his unfortunate victims.

2884.name: Krabkatoa Shell
2884.desc: The massive, still-smoldering shell of the gargantuan Krabkatoa, as solid and durable as a slab of volcanic rock.

2885.name: Orcus Mandible
2885.desc: The imposing mandible of the mammoth antlion Orcus, suited to instantaneously crushing the life out of unwitting prey.

2886.name: Mildewy Ingot
2886.desc: The excess mold growth on this shabby-looking ingot makes it impossible to discern what sort of metal it originally was.

2887.name: Decayed Ingot
2887.desc: A decaying ingot of some indeterminate metal that feels as if it is about to crumble to dust in your hand.

2888.name: Sanguinet
2888.desc: A translucent mass of tissue found protruding from the body of certain vorageans. Though slightly cracked, it retains a vivid scarlet hue.

2889.name: Limule Pincer
2889.desc: The spiny pincer of a limule.

2890.name: Clionid Wing
2890.desc: The delicate, translucent wing of a clionid.

2891.name: Dried Chigoe
2891.desc: The withered husk of a dead chigoe.

2892.name: R. Mutton Chop
2892.desc: This bloody slab of ram meat exudes an overpowering raw odor.

2893.name: G. Blk. Tiger Fang
2893.desc: The oversized fang of a gargantuan species of black tiger.

2894.name: Trophy Shield
2894.desc: A shield claimed by a Gigas off the body of a particularly renowned adventurer it slew.

2895.name: Oversized Sock
2895.desc: This oversized sock of a Gigas reeks of blood and sweat. Far too large (and rancid) to be worn except by Gigas.

2896.name: Massive Armband
2896.desc: A weighted armband used by Gigas in building their hulking muscles. Far too heavy to be worn except by Gigas.

2897.name: Tr. Insect Wing
2897.desc: An insect wing so transparent as to be almost invisible to the eye.

2898.name: Piceous Scale
2898.desc: A pitch-black scale that seems to smother the light around it.

2899.name: Winter Puk Egg
2899.desc: Puk eggs laid in winter, such as this one, are said to be particularly rich in nutrients.

2900.name: Bug-eaten Hat
2900.desc: This hole-ridden hat of a Poroggo appears to have provided a meal for many an insect. Only Poroggos can equip it.

2901.name: Giant Agaricus
2901.desc: A particularly large and earthy agaricus mushroom.

2902.name: Filthy Gnole Claw
2902.desc: This massive gnole claw is caked with dirt and grime.

2903.name: Eft Blood
2903.desc: Blood from an eft, said to be so vile that even leeches steer clear of it.

2904.name: G. Slug Eyestalk
2904.desc: The long, slimy eyestalk of a giant slug.

2905.name: Moonglow Cloth
2905.desc: A gossamer astral fabric that glows only under the light of the moon. Far too delicate to use in crafting.

2906.name: Rotting Eyeball
2906.desc: An eyeball from a treant that has begun to decompose.

2908.name: Oblivispore
2908.desc: A poisonous fungus said to wipe clean the memory of any who eats it. Not suitable for consumption.

2909.name: Snakeskin Moss
2909.desc: A curious mottled moss that had been growing from the back of a goobbue.

2910.name: Arm. Dragonhorn
2910.desc: The massive horn of a wivre, of fearsome size and sharpness.

2911.name: Ripped Eft Skin
2911.desc: A skin that appears to have been savagely ripped from the body of the eft it once belonged to.

2912.name: Gt. Bugard Tusk
2912.desc: The particularly gargantuan tusk of a bugard.

2913.name: Clouded Lens
2913.desc: A murky lens from the eye of an Ahriman.

2914.name: Murmuring Glob
2914.desc: A small globule rumored to be the soul of a deep sea creature. Place it by your ear, and you can hear the sound of a murmuring stream...

2917.name: Bl. Hecteye
2917.desc: The bloodshot eyeball of a hecteyes.

2918.name: Baleful Skull
2918.desc: The cursed skull of a corpselight.

2919.name: Bloody Fang
2919.desc: The fang of a bat, flecked with fresh blood.

2920.name: Alkaline Humus
2920.desc: The strong alkaline concentration of this humus renders it highly unsuitable for gardening.

2921.name: V. Scorp. Stinger
2921.desc: Still dripping with potent venom, this scorpion stinger must be handled with care.

2922.name: Eft Egg
2922.desc: In spite of (or perhaps because of?) the slight sulphurous odor it exudes, eft eggs such as this one are considered a delicacy.

2923.name: Ctrice. Tailmeat
2923.desc: Meat from the tail of a cockatrice. Stringy and flavorless, it is quite unpopular among culinarians.

2924.name: Shk. Whisker
2924.desc: This whisker cut from the face of a coeurl pulsates with energy in your hand.

2925.name: Resilient Mane
2925.desc: A particularly pliant and durable manticore mane.

2926.name: Moaning Vestige
2926.desc: A fragment of some unidentifiable organic substance that emits a faint moaning sound.

2927.name: Glavoid Shell
2927.desc: The massive shell of a glavoid.

2928.name: 2Lf. Chloris Bud
2928.desc: An oversized flower from the crown of a chloris.

2929.name: Helm of Briareus
2929.desc: An imposing helm that once guarded the head of the legendary Gigas Briareus. Only wearable by Gigas.

2930.name: Carabosse's Gem
2930.desc: A small yet tasteful gem that belonged to the evil pixie queen Carabosse.

2931.name: Fistl. Discharge
2931.desc: A thick, foul-smelling liquid expelled from a fistule.

2932.name: Kukulkan's Fang
2932.desc: The massive fang of Kukulkan, capable of impaling a knight clean through his armor.

2933.name: Chukwa's Egg
2933.desc: Laid by Chukwa, this egg is several orders of magnitude larger than a typical adamantoise egg.

2934.name: Manan. Necklet
2934.desc: The oversized necklet of a manananggal. An incantation of warding prevents any but its original owner from equipping it.

2935.name: Marvin's Pelage
2935.desc: The ratty, flea-ridden pelage of Mangy-tailed Marvin.

2936.name: Chasmic Stinger
2936.desc: A dagger-like stinger harvested from the body of a chasmic hornet.

2937.name: Raskovnik Vine
2937.desc: The giant vine of a raskovnik.

2938.name: Bakka's Wing
2938.desc: A wing clipped from the imp lord Bakka.

2939.name: Cuelebre's Horn
2939.desc: The fearsome horn of the legendary serpent Cuelebre.

2940.name: Mictlan. Habit
2940.desc: The dark robes worn by the necromancer Mictlantecuhtli while performing his dark rituals. Not wearable by living beings.

2941.name: Keesha Pamama
2941.desc: A pamama purloined from the possession of the fruit-fancying Keesha Poppo.

2942.name: Mikey's Nugget
2942.desc: A hefty silver nugget removed from the stomach of Megamaw Mikey. Likely the cause of a mega-serious case of indigestion.

2943.name: Balaur Skull
2943.desc: The ghastly skull of a balaur wyvern.

2944.name: Lieje Lantern
2944.desc: Once carried by a Tonberry Lieje, this lantern still burns with an ominous, hateful light.

2945.name: Shriveled Wing
2945.desc: The wing of a clionid, now shriveled from excess exposure to the air.

2946.name: Tarnished Pincer
2946.desc: The pincer of a limule, now tarnished from excess exposure to the air.

2947.name: Exorcised Skull
2947.desc: This corpselight skull has been cleansed of the curse that once resided in it.

2948.name: Acidic Humus
2948.desc: Mildly acidic in composition, this humus is well suited for gardening.

2949.name: Quiv. Eft Egg
2949.desc: This eft egg seems about to hatch at any moment...

2950.name: Smooth Whisker
2950.desc: Drained of electricity, this coeurl whisker is soft and smooth to the touch.

2951.name: Malachite
2951.desc: This spectacular green-colored mineral shimmers brilliantly even in dim light.

2952.name: M. Counteragent
2952.desc: A gaseous substance that acts to temporarily disperse miasmas.

2953.name: Viscous Spittle
2953.desc: A slimy substance expectorated by a rare species of arachnid. Can be used as an adhesive.

2954.name: Powder Casket
2954.desc: A small casket of gunpowder. Dampened from time spent underwater, it is likely unusable in its current state.

2955.name: Kindred's Crest
2955.desc: An elaborate crest belonging to the uppermost nobility of the Kindred.

2956.name: H. Kindred Crest
2956.desc: Surreal, nightmarish patterns adorn this crest, symbolizing a Kindred house of the purest lineage.

2957.name: S. Kindred Crest
2957.desc: The demonically frightful patterns etched into this crest skillfully convey the power possessed by the unholiest of Kindred houses.

2958.name: Kupon W-E85
2958.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for level 85 empyrean weapons.

2959.name: Kupon A-RJob
2959.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for job-specific Dynamis accessories.

2960.name: Water Lily
2960.desc: The delicate, light purple blossom of a water lily.

2961.name: Sanguine Spike
2961.desc: The fearsome spike of a peiste, dripping with both venom and the blood of the creature's prey.

2962.name: Itzpapa. Scale
2962.desc: A glossy black scale resembling obsidian, harvested from the wing of Itzpapalotl.

2963.name: Ulhuadshi's Fang
2963.desc: A massive fang extracted from the mouth of the giant sandworm Ulhuadshi.

2964.name: Sobek's Skin
2964.desc: The skin of the gargantuan bugard Sobek, as rugged as the face of a mountain.

2965.name: Cirein. Lantern
2965.desc: The eerie light stalk of Cirein-croin, employed by this fearsome deep sea hunter to lure prey of all sizes.

2966.name: Bukhis's Wing
2966.desc: The massive wing of the gargantuan taurus known as Bukhis.

2967.name: Sedna's Tusk
2967.desc: The overgrown tusk of the ruszor Sedna, so sharp and sturdy that it can cut through glaciers.

2968.name: Daybreak Soul
2968.desc: The remnant of a soul lost in the dying world of Abyssea. It glows faintly only in the early morning hours.

2969.name: Twilight Soul
2969.desc: The remnant of a soul lost in the dying world of Abyssea. It glows faintly only in the wee hours of the night.

2970.name: Mokujin
2970.desc: A ninjutsu tool used in casting "Migawari."

2971.name: Inoshishinofuda
2971.desc: A master ninjutsu tool that may be used to cast any elemental ninjutsu.

2972.name: Shikanofuda
2972.desc: A master ninjutsu tool that may be used to cast any self-enhancing ninjutsu.

2973.name: Chonofuda
2973.desc: A master ninjutsu tool that may be used to cast any foe-enfeebling ninjutsu.

2974.name: Trump Card
2974.desc: A special technique has been used to imbue this card with the energies of all elements.

2975.name: Manigordo Tusk
2975.desc: The keen-edged, oversized cuspid of a manigordo.

2976.name: Badlands Salt
2976.desc: A flavorful salt found in the shell of a desert-dwelling crab.

2977.name: Punch Bug
2977.desc: A rare and remarkable insect that, despite its minuscule size, is capable of boring holes in the hardest rocks and shells.

2978.name: MMM Gorget
2978.desc: A gorget of pedestrian craftsmanship, engraved with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2979.name: MMM Gorget +1
2979.desc: This magnificent gorget has been forged by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. It is emblazoned with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2980.name: MMM Cuffs
2980.desc: Cuffs of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stitched on one wrist.

2981.name: MMM Cuffs +1
2981.desc: These magnificent cuffs have been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin clothiers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been embroidered into one wrist.

2982.name: MMM Collar
2982.desc: A collar of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stitched on one side.

2983.name: MMM Collar +1
2983.desc: This magnificent collar has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin clothiers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been embroidered into one side.

2984.name: MMM Ham
2984.desc: A fatty, heavily salted and highly unappetizing ham. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem carelessly carved into the flesh is barely recognizable.

2985.name: MMM Ham +1
2985.desc: A mouthwatering ham cured by the finest Moblin chefs in Movalpolos. It is stamped with a highly stylized variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2986.name: MMM Helm
2986.desc: A helm of pedestrian craftsmanship, engraved with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2987.name: MMM Helm +1
2987.desc: This magnificent helm has been forged by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. It is emblazoned with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2988.name: MMM Table
2988.desc: A round table of pedestrian craftsmanship, engraved with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2989.name: MMM Table +1
2989.desc: This magnificent table has been crafted by the finest carpenters in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been engraved in the center.

2990.name: MMM Box
2990.desc: A nondescript box stamped with a crude, barely recognizable Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

2991.name: MMM Box +1
2991.desc: This fashionable box has been crafted by the finest carpenters in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been engraved on its lid.

2992.desc: A nugget of some indeterminate metal. Its exact properties are uncertain.

3015.name: Bone Handicraft
3015.desc: A bizarre handicraft created from animal bones of indeterminate origin. Its exact properties are uncertain.

3016.desc: A powdery foodstuff of unknown origin, the specific properties of which are uncertain.

3017.name: Crmb. Bonemeal

3024.name: MMM Sword
3024.desc: A sword of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem engraved on its hilt.

3025.name: MMM Axe
3025.desc: An axe of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem engraved on its hilt.

3026.name: MMM Katana
3026.desc: A katana of pedestrian craftsmanship. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been crudely engraved on its hilt.

3027.name: MMM Sword +1
3027.desc: This magnificent sword has been forged by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been engraved on the hilt.

3028.name: MMM Axe +1
3028.desc: This magnificent axe has been forged by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been engraved on the hilt.

3029.name: MMM Katana +1
3029.desc: This magnificent katana has been forged by the finest Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. The hilt is engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3030.name: MMM Staff
3030.desc: A staff of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem carved in its base.

3031.name: MMM Shield
3031.desc: A shield of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem engraved on its face.

3032.name: MMM Coffer
3032.desc: A coffer of pedestrian craftsmanship. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been crudely engraved on its lid.

3033.name: MMM Staff +1
3033.desc: This magnificent staff has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin woodcarvers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been carved in its base.

3034.name: MMM Shield +1
3034.desc: This magnificent shield has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem is emblazoned on its face.

3035.name: MMM Coffer +1
3035.desc: This magnificent coffer has been forged by the finest Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. The lid is engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3036.name: MMM Ring
3036.desc: A ring of pedestrian craftsmanship, engraved with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3037.name: MMM Bangle
3037.desc: Bangles of pedestrian craftsmanship, engraved with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3038.name: MMM Crown
3038.desc: A crown of pedestrian craftsmanship. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been crudely engraved in the front.

3039.name: MMM Ring +1
3039.desc: This exquisite ring has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin jewelers in Movalpolos. It is engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3040.name: MMM Bangle +1
3040.desc: These exquisite bangles have been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin jewelers in Movalpolos. They are engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3041.name: MMM Crown +1
3041.desc: A magnificent crown fit for only the most regal of Moblin kings. Forged by the finest Moblin smiths in Movalpolos, it bears an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3042.name: MMM Robe
3042.desc: A robe of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stitched into one sleeve.

3043.name: MMM Hose
3043.desc: Hose of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stitched into one ankle.

3044.name: MMM Coat
3044.desc: A coat of pedestrian craftsmanship, bearing a crudely stitched Moblin Maze Mongers emblem on one sleeve.

3045.name: MMM Robe +1
3045.desc: This fine robe has been sewn by the most skilled Moblin clothiers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been embroidered into one sleeve.

3046.name: MMM Hose +1
3046.desc: This fine hose has been sewn by the most skilled Moblin clothiers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been embroidered by the ankle.

3047.name: MMM Coat +1
3047.desc: This magnificent coat has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin clothiers in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been embroidered into the breast pocket.

3048.name: MMM Belt
3048.desc: A belt of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stamped alongside the buckle.

3049.name: MMM Boots
3049.desc: Boots of pedestrian craftsmanship, with a crude Moblin Maze Mongers emblem stamped on the side.

3050.name: MMM Jerkin
3050.desc: A jerkin of pedestrian craftsmanship, bearing a crudely stitched Moblin Maze Mongers emblem on one sleeve.

3051.name: MMM Belt +1
3051.desc: This fine belt has been crafted by the most skilled Moblin cobblers in Movalpolos. The belt buckle has been fashioned into an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3052.name: MMM Boots +1
3052.desc: These fine boots have been crafted by the most skilled Moblin cobblers in Movalpolos. The fasteners have been fashioned into elaborate variants of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3053.name: MMM Jerkin +1
3053.desc: This magnificent jerkin has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin leathercrafters in Movalpolos. An elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem adorns one sleeve.

3054.name: MMM Earring
3054.desc: An earring of pedestrian craftsmanship, crudely engraved with the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3055.name: MMM Knife
3055.desc: A knife of pedestrian craftsmanship, with the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem crudely engraved on its hilt.

3056.name: MMM Baghnakhs
3056.desc: Baghnakhs of pedestrian craftsmanship. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem has been crudely engraved on its hilt.

3057.name: MMM Earring +1
3057.desc: This exquisite earring has been fashioned by the most skilled Moblin jewelers in Movalpolos. It is engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3058.name: MMM Knife +1
3058.desc: This striking knife has been forged by the most skilled Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. The hilt is engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3059.name: MMM Baghnakhs +1
3059.desc: These magnificent baghnakhs have been forged by the finest Moblin smiths in Movalpolos. The hilts are engraved with an elaborate variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3060.name: MMM Potion
3060.desc: An unremarkable potion. A simple Moblin Maze Mongers emblem adorns the label.

3061.name: MMM Ether
3061.desc: An unremarkable ether. A simple Moblin Maze Mongers emblem adorns the label.

3062.name: MMM Elixir
3062.desc: A murky elixir in a dusty, scratched bottle. The tattered label bears a crudely scribbled variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3063.name: MMM Potion +1
3063.desc: A potion in an ornate bottle. The gaudy label features a stately portrait of Goldagrik alongside the familiar Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3064.name: MMM Ether +1
3064.desc: An ether in an ornate bottle. The gaudy label features a stately portrait of Goldagrik alongside the familiar Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3065.name: MMM Elixir +1
3065.desc: This elixir has been prepared by the finest Moblin alchemists in Movalpolos. An elaborate label bearing a stylized variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem adorns the elegantly curved vial.

3066.name: MMM Soup
3066.desc: A bowl of thin, watered-down soup. A crudely drawn Moblin Maze Mongers emblem is imprinted at the base.

3067.name: MMM Pie
3067.desc: A stale and lightly burnt pie with a hard crust, decorated with a nearly indistinguishable Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3068.name: MMM Bread
3068.desc: A rock-hard, crusty, and decidedly unappetizing loaf of bread. The Moblin Maze Mongers emblem carelessly impressed into the crust is barely recognizable.

3069.name: MMM Soup +1
3069.desc: Prepared by the finest Moblin chefs in Movalpolos, this hearty soup gives off a rich aroma of exotic spices.

3070.name: MMM Pie +1
3070.desc: Prepared by the finest Moblin bakers in Movalpolos, this tasty pie features a filling of fresh fruit and a scrumptious cinnamon crust.

3071.name: MMM Bread +1
3071.desc: A loaf of mouthwatering bread prepared by the finest Moblin bakers in Movalpolos. The crust is skillfully decorated with a stylized variant of the Moblin Maze Mongers emblem.

3072.name: Withered Cocoon
3072.desc: The withered cocoon of a wamoura.

3073.name: Eruca Egg
3073.desc: The oversized egg of an eruca.

3074.name: Blanched Silver
3074.desc: A small lump of silver that has lost some of its lustre.

3075.name: Undying Ooze
3075.desc: This repulsive gunk continues to bubble and squirm long after being separated from the doomed that excreted it.

3076.name: Bone Chips
3076.desc: Cracked and chipped bones of various size, shape, and origin.

3077.name: Wailing Rags
3077.desc: When you rest your ear on these tattered garments, you can hear a tortured scream in the distance…

3078.name: Cracked Drgscale.
3078.desc: A massive force must have been required to cause this sturdy scale of a dragon to crack.

3079.name: Gory Pincer
3079.desc: This scorpion pincer is caked with the blood of its prey.

3080.name: Ext. Eyestalk
3080.desc: The fully extended eyestalk of a slug, doomed never to return to its retracted state.

3081.name: Coeurl Round
3081.desc: This round cut of thigh meat from a coeurl is laced with fat and gristle.

3082.name: Withered Bud
3082.desc: Separated from the mandragora it sprouted from, it looks about to crumble into dust at any moment.

3083.name: Mngl. Ck. Skin
3083.desc: The cockatrice this skin was taken from clearly did not go down without a fight…

3084.name: Great Root
3084.desc: The thick root of an ancient treant.

3085.name: Bewitching Tusk
3085.desc: The ensorcelled tusk of a bugard.

3086.name: Molt Scraps
3086.desc: Scraps from the shedded molt of a lizard.

3087.name: Apkallu Down
3087.desc: These underfeathers of an apkallu are soft and warm to the touch.

3088.name: Avian Remex
3088.desc: A large feather that allows birds to control their flight path in strong wind.

3089.name: Orbn. Cheekmeat
3089.desc: The rare cheekmeat of an orobon, considered a delicacy.

3090.name: Spheroid Plate
3090.desc: A plate of some indeterminate metal, retrieved from a deactivated spheroid.

3091.name: Hd. Raptor Skin
3091.desc: The frozen, hardened skin of a raptor.

3092.name: Mocking Beak
3092.desc: The beak of a colibri said to have the ability to mimic adventurers' voices.

3093.name: H.Q. Crab Meat
3093.desc: This piece of land crab meat is of perfect quality. So perfect, in fact, that it would be unthinkable to eat it.

3094.name: H.Q. Rock Salt
3094.desc: A chunk of rock salt of the highest grade and quality.

3095.name: Worm-Eaten Bud
3095.desc: This hole-ridden mandragora bud has clearly served as a feast for many a famished worm.

3096.name: Spotted Flyfrond
3096.desc: An attractive spotted pattern adorns this giant flytrap leaf.

3097.name: Blk. Rabbit Tail
3097.desc: The tail of a black rabbit, said to serve as a warding talisman.

3098.name: Gnr. Taurus Horn
3098.desc: The balefully gnarled horn of a taurus.

3099.name: Gargouille Stone
3099.desc: A hunk of flesh from a gargouille that has hardened to the point that it is indistinguishable from actual stone.

3100.name: Moonbeam Clam
3100.desc: A rare clam said to open its shell only when the light of the moon shines upon it.

3101.name: Dented Skull
3101.desc: The skull of a corpselight that seems to have caught a hefty blow to its head.

3102.name: Stiff. Tentacle
3102.desc: This tentacle has stiffened after the death of the soulflayer it was once attached to.

3103.name: Black Whisker
3103.desc: Other than its jet-black color, this feline whisker is entirely unremarkable.

3104.name: Opaque Wing
3104.desc: The murky-colored wing of some unnatural beast.

3105.name: Fortune Wing
3105.desc: This red wing of a ladybug is said to bring good luck to he who finds it.

3106.name: Djinn Ashes
3106.desc: The smoldering ashes of a now-extinguished djinn.

3107.name: Shockshroom
3107.desc: A poisonous mushroom said to send a paralyzing shock through the body if consumed.

3108.name: Crwl. Floatstone
3108.desc: A small stone removed from the stomach of a crawler. Can be used as pumice.

3109.name: H.Q. Rabbit Hide
3109.desc: The particularly sturdy hide of a rabbit.

3110.name: Rvg. Seal: Hd.
3110.desc: A musty slip of parchment, worn and faded with the years. Faintly visible is the image of a piece of armor, annotated with elaborately scribed glyphs of a long-lost tongue.

3111.desc: A musty slip of parchment, worn and faded with the years. Faintly visible is the image of a piece of armor, annotated with elaborately scribed glyphs of a long-lost tongue.

3210.name: Vision Stone
3210.desc: A peculiar translucent stone that refracts light, making objects seen through it appear bright and clear.

3211.name: Vision Coin
3211.desc: A palm-sized coin of a curious transparent metal.

3212.name: Vision Jewel
3212.desc: A shimmering jewel of perfect cut and clarity.

3213.name: Vision Card
3213.desc: A timeworn card bearing an exquisitely illustrated depiction of a far-seeing eye.

3214.name: Ardor Stone
3214.desc: Holding this small crimson stone in your hand, you feel a gentle warmth well up from within you.

3215.name: Ardor Coin
3215.desc: A palm-sized coin embossed with the mark of a burning heart.

3216.name: Ardor Jewel
3216.desc: The red hues of this jewel blaze and surge like the flames of passion.

3217.name: Ardor Card
3217.desc: A timeworn card bearing an exquisite illustration of a raging flame.

3218.name: Wieldance Stone
3218.desc: Taking this stone into hand, you can feel your arm move more freely.

3219.name: Wieldance Coin
3219.desc: A palm-sized coin emblazoned with the mark of a hand gripping a blade.

3220.name: Wieldance Jewel
3220.desc: The amber hues of this jewel swish and swirl unconstrained.

3221.name: Wieldance Card
3221.desc: A timeworn card bearing an exquisite illustration of a whirling sword.

3222.name: Balance Stone
3222.desc: A sturdy stone that, when placed on the ground, can curiously not be moved from either side.

3223.name: Balance Coin
3223.desc: A peculiar coin that, when flipped, will always alternate heads and tails.

3224.name: Balance Jewel
3224.desc: A beautiful translucent jewel of perfectly symmetrical cut.

3225.name: Balance Card
3225.desc: A timeworn card bearing an exquisitely illustrated depiction of a weighing scale.

3226.name: Voyage Stone
3226.desc: Taking this stone into hand, you can feel the wind blowing at your back.

3227.name: Voyage Coin
3227.desc: A palm-sized coin embossed with the mark of a ship with billowing sails.

3228.name: Voyage Jewel
3228.desc: The blue hues of this jewel ebb and flow like the ocean tides.

3229.name: Voyage Card
3229.desc: A timeworn card bearing an exquisitely illustrated depiction of a striding man.

3230.name: Tablilla Mercury
3230.desc: A shimmering liquid metal extracted from the doll Tablilla.

3231.name: Sharabha Hide
3231.desc: The rugged hide of the manticore Sharabha.

3232.name: Tiger King Hide
3232.desc: The regally colored hide of the fell tiger Ansherekh.

3233.name: Vadleany Fluid
3233.desc: A pungent liquid extracted from a slain vadleany, its role of driving away would-be foes unfulfilled.

3234.name: Oasis Water
3234.desc: A precious bottle of pure water from a desert oasis, carried by the fearsome Bugul Noz.

3235.name: Sandy Shard
3235.desc: Desert sand clings to the surface of this shattered doll fragment.

3236.name: Smoldering Arm
3236.desc: This arm of a bomb still steams and smolders despite having been long since severed from its master.

3237.name: Sand-caked Fang
3237.desc: This oversized manticore fang is caked with such a thick layer of sand that its original shape and color is impossible to discern.

3239.name: Puppet's Blood
3239.desc: The blood of an unnaturally bred puk, said to impel anyone who drinks it to dance uncontrollably.

3241.name: Ladybird Leaf
3241.desc: A broad, thick-veined leaf that had been stuck to the back of an unaware ladybug.

3243.name: Sabulous Clay
3243.desc: A variety of desert clay with a high sand content.

3244.name: Giant Mistletoe
3244.desc: A parasitic plant so tenacious that it is known to strangle the life out of even the mightiest oaks.

3245.name: Bevel Gear
3245.desc: A massive, jagged-toothed gear of bronze that powered the mighty Koghatu.

3246.name: Snow God Core
3246.desc: This condensed soul of the fearsome Upas-Kamuy burns with a chill blue flame.

3247.name: Sisyphus Fragment
3247.desc: A fragment of debris claimed off the shattered body of the golem Sisyphus.

3248.name: Rimed Wing
3248.desc: The frost-covered wing of the notorious imp Chillwing Hwitti.

3249.name: Audumbla Hide
3249.desc: The rough hide of the bovine audumbla.

3250.name: Helical Gear
3250.desc: A curved metallic gear that once powered a mighty acrolith.

3251.name: Gear Fluid
3251.desc: A peculiar, oil-like substance that will not freeze in even the coldest temperatures.

3252.name: Gelid Arm
3252.desc: The arm of a snoll, so stingingly cold that holding it in one's bare hand will inflict an almost instant case of frostbite.

3254.name: Ice Wyvern Scale
3254.desc: This wyvern scale is as chill as ice to the touch.

3255.name: Whiteworm Clay
3255.desc: A rich clay-like substance excreted from the body of an entozoon.

3256.name: Imp Sentry's Horn
3256.desc: A compact horn employed by imp sentries to warn their masters of potential interlopers.

3257.name: Benumbed Eye
3257.desc: This clouded eye of a benumbed vodoriga stares at you with a soulless, empty gaze.

3258.name: H.Q. Buffalo Horn
3258.desc: A particularly large and sturdy buffalo horn.

3259.name: H.Q. B. Tiger Hide
3259.desc: The particularly rugged and sleek hide of a black tiger.

3260.name: Teekess. Fragment
3260.desc: The shattered fragment of the Teekesselchen.

3261.name: Minaruja Skull
3261.desc: The abnormally hard skull of the wyvern Minaruja.

3262.name: Jaculus Wing
3262.desc: The almost-weightless yet highly durable wing of the peapuk Jaculus.

3263.name: Naiad's Lock
3263.desc: A wispy lock of hair from the pixie Lorelei, whose haunting song is said to have beckoned many an adventurer to an untimely demise.

3264.name: Goblin Gunpowder
3264.desc: A highly explosive gunpowder devised by the mad Goblin bombardier Burstrox Powderpate.

3265.name: Bubbling Oil
3265.desc: Extracted from a magic pot, the noxious fumes of this thick oil induce nausea with a single whiff.

3266.name: Darkflame Arm
3266.desc: Severed from an unfortunate djinn, this arm blazes with a black, heatless flame.

3267.name: Pursuer's Wing
3267.desc: The wing of the ajattara wyvern, said to chase away evil spirits of the forest.

3268.name: H.Q. Wivre Hide
3268.desc: The particularly thick and durable skin of a wivre.

3269.name: Decaying Molar
3269.desc: Eaten away from within by hungry grubs, this molar of a skeleton smells even worse than it looks.

3270.name: H.Q. Pugil Scale
3270.desc: This large pugil scale shimmers brilliantly under light.

3271.name: Fay Teardrop
3271.desc: A teardrop-shaped clear gemstone claimed from a slain pixie.

3272.name: Unseelie Eye
3272.desc: A dark jewel claimed from a slain unseelie.

3273.name: Goblin Rope
3273.desc: An unsightly but sturdy rope of Goblin make.

3274.name: Goblin Oil
3274.desc: This malodorous oil is employed by Goblins to prevent their armor from rusting any more than it already has.

3275.name: Genbu Scrap
3275.desc: A blemished metal fragment from a shattered seal of Genbu.

3276.name: Suzaku Scrap
3276.desc: A blemished metal fragment from a shattered seal of Suzaku.

3277.name: Seiryu Scrap
3277.desc: A blemished metal fragment from a shattered seal of Seiryu.

3278.name: Byakko Scrap
3278.desc: A blemished metal fragment from a shattered seal of Byakko.

3279.name: Neptunal Tatter
3279.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent neptunal abjuration.

3280.name: Martial Tatter
3280.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent martial abjuration.

3281.name: Earthen Tatter
3281.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent earthen abjuration.

3282.name: Dryadic Tatter
3282.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent dryadic abjuration.

3283.name: Aquarian Tatter
3283.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent aquarian abjuration.

3284.name: Wyrmal Tatter
3284.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent wyrmal abjuration.

3285.name: Phant. Tatter
3285.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent phantasmal abjuration.

3286.name: Hadean Tatter
3286.desc: A shred of parchment from a rent hadean abjuration.

3287.name: Orthrus's Claw
3287.desc: The claw of Orthrus, long-lost brother of Cerberus, has carried many a would-be hero to the beast's dreadful maw.

3288.name: Dragua's Scale
3288.desc: A scale from the wyrm Dragua, said to be as impenetrable as the hardest metal.

3289.name: Apademak Horn
3289.desc: The massive, gnarled horn of the warbringer Apademak inspires fear and awe.

3290.name: Isgebind's Heart
3290.desc: The frozen heart of the dragon Isgebind, as cold to the touch as chill fetters of ice.

3291.name: Alfard's Fang
3291.desc: The massive fang of Alfard drips with lethal poison.

3292.name: Azdaja's Horn
3292.desc: The horrifically twisted horn of Azdaja, embodiment of hatred and destruction.

3293.name: Iron Plate
3293.desc: A piece wrenched from the plate armor of an iron giant.

3294.name: Colorless Soul
3294.desc: This elemental soul, drained of all its power, has faded to a milky white.

3295.name: Incrscnt. Shade
3295.desc: This faded wisp is all that remains of a wandering soul lost in Abyssea. It glows wistfully with the arrival of dusk.

3296.name: Decrscnt. Shade
3296.desc: This faded wisp is all that remains of a wandering soul lost in Abyssea. It glows mournfully with the break of dawn.

3297.name: Flame Geode
3297.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with red crystals of many shades and hues.

3298.name: Snow Geode
3298.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with clear crystals of many shades and hues.

3299.name: Breeze Geode
3299.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with green crystals of many shades and hues.

3300.name: Soil Geode
3300.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with yellow crystals of many shades and hues.

3301.name: Thunder Geode
3301.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with purple crystals of many shades and hues.

3302.name: Aqua Geode
3302.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with blue crystals of many shades and hues.

3303.name: Light Geode
3303.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with bright crystals of many shades and hues.

3304.name: Shadow Geode
3304.desc: A hollow rock lined along the inside with dark crystals of many shades and hues.

3305.name: Ruszor Arrowhd.
3305.desc: The striking tips of ruszor arrows.

3306.name: D. A. Bolt Heads
3306.desc: The striking tips of dark adaman bolts.

3307.name: Heat Capacitor
3307.desc: A fire-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Adds Fire Maneuver burden  　to restore TP

3308.name: Power Cooler
3308.desc: An ice-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Reduces MP cost

3309.name: Barrage Turbine
3309.desc: A wind-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Adds Wind Maneuver burden to  　occasionally grant a Barrage effect

3310.name: Barrier Module II
3310.desc: An earth-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases chance of blocking  　with shield  ・Earth Maneuver: Adds Shield Mastery  　and shortens Shield Bash recast time

3311.name: Galvanizer
3311.desc: A thunder-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases chance of countering

3312.name: Percolator
3312.desc: A water-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases skill gain rate

3313.name: Vivi-Valve
3313.desc: A light-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases "Cure" potency

3315.name: Ominous Skull
3315.desc: Oddly well preserved, the hollow eyes of this skull gaze at you with a baleful expression.

3316.name: Flame Gem
3316.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of fire.

3317.name: Snow Gem
3317.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of ice.

3318.name: Breeze Gem
3318.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of wind.

3319.name: Soil Gem
3319.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of earth.

3320.name: Thunder Gem
3320.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of lightning.

3321.name: Aqua Gem
3321.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of water.

3322.name: Light Gem
3322.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of light.

3323.name: Shadow Gem
3323.desc: A brilliant jewel that harbors the elemental power of darkness.

3324.name: Blksmth. Emblem
3324.desc: The familiar emblem of the Blacksmiths' Guild.

3325.name: Gldsmth. Emblem
3325.desc: The familiar emblem of the Goldsmiths' Guild.

3326.name: Bonewrk. Emblem
3326.desc: The familiar emblem of the Boneworkers' Guild.

3327.name: Weavers' Emblem
3327.desc: The familiar emblem of the Weavers' Guild.

3328.name: Culinary Emblem
3328.desc: The familiar emblem of the Culinarians' Guild.

3329.name: Tanners' Emblem
3329.desc: The familiar emblem of the Tanners' Guild.

3330.name: Fishermn. Emblem
3330.desc: The familiar emblem of the Fishermen's Guild.

3445.desc: Armor worn by the hellish warrior Hahava. It emits a scarcely audible, unceasing shriek.

3446.name: Kholomo. Hide
3446.desc: The callused, bulky hide of Kholomodumo, the massive beast said to have hungered for the flesh of all living beings.

3449.name: Celaeno's Cloth

3451.name: Comet Fragment
3451.desc: A stone said to have come crashing down from the heavens in a fiery fury. Covered in a fine celestial silt, it exudes a faint trace of an unknown gas.

3452.name: Puny Planet Kit
3452.desc: This kit contains multifarious parts, with which a unique celestial globe can be constructed and customized in an almost limitless number of ways.

3453.name: Cosmic Designs
3453.desc: Synthesizing these celestial globe instructions with a puny planet kit and a comet fragment should provide for some rather resplendent results.

3544.desc: A delicate gossamer fabric hailing from a land far to the west. Named for a beautiful maiden of myth who lovingly weaved a fabric over the course of years while faithfully awaiting her husband's return from war.

3545.desc: A silken fabric woven by a method said to have originated in a lost kingdom far to the east.

3546.name: Staghorn Coral
3546.desc: Sturdy and smooth with multiple branches, this rare form of silvery-white coral calls to mind the antlers of a mighty stag.

3547.name: Sealord Skin
3547.desc: The smooth skin of a pteraketos, the gargantuan rulers of the ocean depths.

3548.name: Sealord Leather
3548.desc: A square of smooth leather made from the skin of a pteraketos. Highly resistant to water and lightning.

3549.name: Belladonna Sap
3549.desc: A viscous liquid extracted from the stem of a belladonna, its sweet smell conceals a highly lethal poison. Said to take on remarkable medicinal properties if processed correctly.

3550.name: Wyrdstrand
3550.desc: An unusual thread said to resemble the mythical thread of fate.

3552.name: Squamous Hide
3552.desc: The thick, scaly hide of a vicious predatory lizard.

3553.name: B. Table Blueprint
3553.desc: A set of instructions for assembling a banquet table. Requires two sets of materials, one of wood and one of fabric.

3554.name: B. Table Fabric
3554.desc: A set of fabric used in assembling a banquet table.

3555.name: B. Table Wood
3555.desc: A set of wooden pieces used in assembling a banquet table.

3556.name: A. Fiend. Comp.
3556.desc: Pages 26 through 30 of a bestiary penned by a military scholar.

3557.name: Athena Orb
3557.desc: The pink and red whorls of energy that dance and swirl within this orb call to mind an elegant shower of flower petals.

3558.name: Abdhaljs's Honor
3558.desc: This brilliantly glowing orb pulsates to the touch. In a flowing runic script, it bears the message: "To you, who outbraved my greatest challenge, I present the greatest reward in my power to offer."

3640.desc: Furnishing: A sublime chilled dessert of frozen cream topped with a tangy rolanberry sauce. Prepared with special preservatives to prevent melting.

3641.desc: Furnishing: A decorative helmet display employed in the traditional martial festivals of the Far East.

3642.name: Katana-kazari
3642.desc: Furnishing: A decorative katana display employed in the traditional martial festivals of the Far East.

3643.name: Carillon Vermeil
3643.desc: Furnishing: A wind chime of traditional San d'Orian design. Rings with a clear, vibrant tone.

3644.name: Aeolsglocke
3644.desc: Furnishing: A wind chime of traditional Bastokan design. Rings with a deep, sonorous tone.

3645.name: Leafbell
3645.desc: Furnishing: A wind chime of traditional Windurstian design. Rings with a gentle, soothing tone.

3646.name: Mandra. Pricket
3646.desc: Furnishing: A seasonal candleholder modeled after a mandragora. Said to ward away evil spirits.

3647.name: Spook-a-Swirl
3647.desc: Furnishing: A San d'Orian Harvest Festival decoration that is at once both eerie and charming.

3648.name: Choc. Grumpkin
3648.desc: Furnishing: A Bastokan Harvest Festival decoration that is at once both eerie and charming.

3649.name: Harvest Horror
3649.desc: Furnishing: A Windurstian Harvest Festival decoration that is at once both eerie and charming.

3650.name: Prinseggstarta
3650.desc: Furnishing: While positively del-egg-table, egg-cess consumption of this egg-stravagant conf-egg-tion of rich marzipan glazed with rich egg cream will have your stomach too eggs-hausted to go egg hunting.

3651.name: Harvest Pastry
3651.desc: Furnishing: This cake, made with ripe pumpkin and surrounded by chocolate cookies, celebrates the Harvest Festival in the most caloric way possible.

3652.name: Memorial Cake
3652.desc: Furnishing: An elaborately decorated confection fit to be served at a feast for the ages.

3653.name: Banquet Table
3653.desc: Furnishing: A large and sturdy table suitable for the most lavish of celebrations.

3654.name: Tender Bouquet
3654.desc: Furnishing: Delicately placed into a lovingly crafted vase, this vibrant bouquet of roses is romantic enough to make even the most ornery of codgers recall the many breathless moments spent with their sweetheart.

3655.name: Treasury Table
3655.desc: Furnishing: A sturdy, oversized iron table with numerous lockable compartments, used at many a guild to safely store profits.

3656.name: Treasury Stand
3656.desc: Furnishing: A sturdy, compact iron table with numerous lockable compartments, used at many a guild to safely store profits.

3657.name: Sundries Table
3657.desc: Furnishing: A large, many-shelved iron table ideal for organizing assorted guild wares.

3658.name: Sundries Stand
3658.desc: Furnishing: A compact iron table with a shelf ideal for organizing assorted guild wares.

3659.name: Supply Table
3659.desc: Furnishing: A simple yet practical wooden table with compartments for storing crafting materials.

3660.name: Supply Stand
3660.desc: Furnishing: A simple yet practical wooden stand with compartments for storing crafting materials.

3661.name: Stone Hearth
3661.desc: Furnishing: A spartan stone fireplace employed by blacksmiths in firing their wares.

3662.name: Display Blades
3662.desc: Furnishing: A display case exhibiting an assortment of decorative blades forged by the Blacksmiths' Guild.

3663.name: Bonecraft Tools

3664.name: Shell Assortment
3664.desc: Furnishing: A colorful assortment of seashells used by apprentice boneworkers in practicing simple shellwork.

3665.name: Spinning Wheel
3665.desc: Furnishing: This traditional spinning wheel emphasizes function over form.

3666.name: Fine Raiments
3666.desc: Furnishing: A set of exquisitely tailored garments suitable for the most special of occasions.

3667.name: Brass Crock
3667.desc: Furnishing: A slow-cook pot coveted by culinarians for its ability to produce savory soups and stews.

3669.name: Courier Bags
3669.desc: Furnishing: Lightweight leather bags suitable for carrying parcels with a minimum of added encumbrance.

3670.name: Net and Lure
3670.desc: Furnishing: These rugged nets and colorful glass lures would be a welcome addition to any angler's tackle box.

3671.name: Stockfish
3671.desc: Furnishing: A variety of cured fish that would make for a tasty and nutritious addition to any adventurer's ration pack.

3672.name: Carpenter's Kit
3672.desc: Furnishing: No carpenter worth his sawdust would be caught without this set of essential woodworking tools.

3673.name: Chocobo Carvings

3674.name: Alembic

3675.name: Flask Set

3681.desc: Furnishing: Inlaid with a cartographic design, this table was built thanks to techniques from a civilization that history has all but completely forgotten.
