3748.name: Leafkin Bed
3748.desc: Furnishing: The deep green hues of this Adoulin-inspired piece lend an aura of majesty to any abode.

3749.name: Chemistry Set
3749.desc: Furnishing: A set of experimental chemical compounds of unknown causticity. Handle with care.

3750.name: Qiqirn Sack
3750.desc: Furnishing: Filled to the brim with jingly in hopes of a prosperous new year.

3751.name: Besigiled Table
3751.desc: Furnishing: This round table has been engraved with black, white, and red arcane symbols.

3752.name: Colibri Bed
3752.desc: Furnishing: A sleeping device imbued with the essence of countless avian-counting whirlygigs.

3753.name: Blueblade Fell
3753.desc: Furnishing: A masterfully depicted still life of Blueblade Fell in Lufaise Meadows.

3754.name: Kagami Mochi
3754.desc: Furnishing: A decorative piece containing a Far East-style celebratory rice cake at its center.

3755.name: Prismatic Chest
3755.desc: Furnishing: A shining coffer whose scintillating glimmer lends a spot of cheer to any dreary living room.

3756.name: Sachertorte
3756.desc: Furnishing: A piece of delectable chocolate cake.

3840.name: Pulse Cell: Mx
3840.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a mextli harness.

3841.name: Pulse Cell: An
3841.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge an anhur robe.

3842.name: Pulse Cell: Fa
3842.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a suit of fazheluo radiant mail.

3843.name: Pulse Cell: To
3843.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a Toci's harness.

3844.name: Pulse Cell: He
3844.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a Heka's kalasiris.

3845.name: Pulse Cell: Mk
3845.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a mekira meikogai.

3846.name: Pulse Cell: Bo
3846.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a borealis.

3847.name: Pulse Cell: As
3847.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge an Asteria.

3848.name: Pulse Cell: Ep
3848.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge an ephemeron.

3849.name: Pulse Cell: Mu
3849.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a murasamemaru.

3850.name: Pulse Cell: Co
3850.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a coruscanti.

3851.name: Pulse Cell: De
3851.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge a delphinius.

3852.name: Pulse Cell: Ay
3852.desc: This pulsating cell houses the energy required to charge an aytanri.

3853.name: Phase Displacer
3853.desc: A curious substance developed by Jeunoan researchers, said to have a debilitating effect on Voidwalkers.

3854.name: Crepuscular Chip
3854.desc: A six-sided fragment of indeterminate matter as black as midnight. Thin strands of energy pulsate from within.

3855.name: Niveous Chip
3855.desc: A six-sided fragment of indeterminate matter as white as a snowdrift. Thin strands of energy pulsate from within.

3856.name: Valkyrie's Tear
3856.desc: A flawless, multifaceted gemstone said to be the solidified tear of a fallen valkyrie.

3857.name: A.Omega Eye
3857.desc: This piece of tissue appears to be from Arch-Omega's eye.

3858.name: A.Omega Heart
3858.desc: This piece of tissue appears to be from Arch-Omega's heart.

3860.name: A.Omega Hind Leg
3860.desc: A segment of Arch-Omega's hind leg.

3862.name: A.Ultima Cerebrum
3862.desc: This piece of tissue appears to be from Arch-Ultima's cerebrum.

3863.name: A.Ultima Heart
3863.desc: This piece of tissue appears to be from Arch-Ultima's heart.

3867.name: Valkyrie's Wing
3867.desc: Soft to the touch yet regal to behold, this wing once carried a brave valkyrie forth to a battle from which she never returned.

3868.name: Valkyrie's Soul
3868.desc: A large and brilliantly transparent gemstone said to be the concentrated soul of a fallen valkyrie.

3869.name: Simian Horn
3869.desc: An adamantine horn painstakingly carved from a fallen yztarg.

3870.name: Simian Mane
3870.desc: The soft and silky mane of a defeated yztarg.

3872.name: Meeble Claw
3872.desc: Still surprisingly sharp, this Meeble claw segment has been used to dig up ponzes upon ponzes of burrow worms.

3873.name: Burrow Worm
3873.desc: A "tasty" worm sure to satiate a famished Meeble.

3875.name: Diligence Grimoire
3875.desc: Manifold findings made by the Meeble Burrows Exploration Committee are inscribed in this supernatural tome.

3876.name: Avidity Grimoire
3876.desc: Manifold findings made by the Meeble Burrows Exploration Committee are inscribed in this supernatural tome.

3877.name: Gallantry Grimoire
3877.desc: Manifold findings made by the Meeble Burrows Exploration Committee are inscribed in this supernatural tome.

3878.name: Militance Grimoire
3878.desc: Manifold findings made by the Meeble Burrows Exploration Committee are inscribed in this supernatural tome.

3879.name: Sanctity Grimoire
3879.desc: Manifold findings made by the Meeble Burrows Exploration Committee are inscribed in this supernatural tome.

3880.name: Bloodshed Plans
3880.desc: Designs diagramming the construction of each piece of an armor series in astonishing detail.

3881.name: Umbrage Plans
3881.desc: Designs diagramming the construction of each piece of an armor series in astonishing detail.

3882.name: Ritualistic Plans
3882.desc: Designs diagramming the construction of each piece of an armor series in astonishing detail.

3883.name: Tutelary Plans
3883.desc: Designs diagramming the construction of each piece of an armor series in astonishing detail.

3884.name: Primacy Plans
3884.desc: Designs diagramming the construction of each piece of an armor series in astonishing detail.

3885.name: Melodious Plans
3885.desc: A parchment detailing complex designs for an orchestrion that requires two different sets of parts to construct.

3886.name: Timbre Case Kit
3886.desc: A kit containing parts used in the construction of an orchestrion's exterior.

3887.name: Musichinery Kit
3887.desc: A kit containing parts used in the construction of an orchestrion's inner machinery.

3888.name: S. Palimpsest
3888.desc: An arcane sheet of parchment inscribed with eldritch emblems that pulse with a magical glow.

3889.name: O. Palimpsest
3889.desc: An arcane sheet of parchment inscribed with eldritch emblems that pulse with a magical glow.

3890.name: A. Palimpsest
3890.desc: An arcane sheet of parchment inscribed with eldritch emblems that pulse with a magical glow.

3891.name: I. Palimpsest
3891.desc: An arcane sheet of parchment inscribed with eldritch emblems that pulse with a magical glow.

3892.name: Fulgent Ore
3892.desc: This rounded fragment of rock glistens brightly thanks to the miniscule jewels interspersed throughout high concentrations of iron.

3893.name: Daimonic Mandible
3893.desc: The stench of death and decay constantly waft up from this from this mandible, formerly attached to an apian beast.

3894.name: Daimonic Casing
3894.desc: An exoskeleton from an apian beast that lurks deep underground with the alleged power to protect its bearer from curses of all kinds.

3895.name: Rala Visage I
3895.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3896.name: Rala Visage II
3896.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3897.name: Rala Visage III
3897.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3898.name: Rala Visage IV
3898.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3899.name: Rala Visage V
3899.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3900.name: Faithful's Torso I
3900.desc: A torso segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3901.name: Faithful's Torso II
3901.desc: A torso segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3902.name: Faithfl. Torso III
3902.desc: A torso segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3903.name: Faithfl. Torso IV
3903.desc: A torso segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3904.name: Faithful's Torso V
3904.desc: A torso segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3905.name: Faithful's Legs I
3905.desc: A bottom-half segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka. The outline of a creature resembling a twitherym has been chiseled into its slightly worn surface.

3906.name: Faithful's Legs II
3906.desc: A bottom-half segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka. The outline of a creature resembling a twitherym has been chiseled into its slightly worn surface.

3907.name: Faithful's Legs III
3907.desc: A bottom-half segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka. The outline of a creature resembling a twitherym has been chiseled into its slightly worn surface.

3908.name: Faithful's Legs IV
3908.desc: A bottom-half segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka. The outline of a creature resembling a twitherym has been chiseled into its slightly worn surface.

3909.name: Faithful's Legs V
3909.desc: A bottom-half egment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka. The outline of a creature resembling a twitherym has been chiseled into its slightly worn surface.

3910.name: Cirdas Visage I
3910.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3911.name: Cirdas Visage II
3911.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3912.name: Cirdas Visage III
3912.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3913.name: Cirdas Visage IV
3913.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3914.name: Cirdas Visage V
3914.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

3915.name: Saffron Blossom
3915.desc: This flower blooms a shade of purple almost as vibrant as the spice that derives from it is potent.

3916.name: Saffron
3916.desc: A flavorful spice derived from the pistil of a saffron blossom.

3917.name: Leafkin Frond
3917.desc: This leafkin body part is surprisingly strong, considering it is light enough to float on the slightest of breezes.

3918.name: Midrium Ore
3918.desc: This ore contains the metal midrium.

3920.name: Vanadium Ore
3920.desc: This ore contains the metal vanadium.

3922.name: Rhodium Ore
3922.desc: This ore contains the metal rhodium.

3924.name: Scholar Stone
3924.desc: A chunk of rock that contains resplendent azure jewels.

3926.name: Urunday Log
3926.desc: A rough-cut log of urunday timber.

3928.name: Velkk Necklace
3928.desc: A necklace fashioned from bone and worn by the Velkk.  It is too ominous an accessory to be worn even by adventurers.

3929.name: Velkk Mask
3929.desc: A mask crafted from leather and worn by the Velkk.  It is too ominous an accessory to be worn even by adventurers.

3930.name: Twitherym Wing
3930.desc: Despite its near-invisible size, this twitherym wing is a beauty to behold.

3931.name: Twitherym Scale
3931.desc: A single scale painstakingly removed from a tiny twitherym wing. It shines so faintly that it is almost imperceptible.

3932.name: Chapuli Horn
3932.desc: A chapuli horn so razor-sharp that it can slice fingers if handled improperly.

3933.name: Craklaw Pincer
3933.desc: A nearly indestructible pincer ripped from the arm of a craklaw.

3934.name: Matamata Shell
3934.desc: A hollow shell that once served as a suit of armor for a ferocious matamata. It has taken its fair share of beatings, but is none the worse for the wear.

3935.name: Umbril Ooze
3935.desc: A jar of viscous liquid obtained from a slain umbril.

3936.name: Acuex Poison
3936.desc: A jar of viscous liquid obtained from a slain acuex.

3937.name: Panopt Tears
3937.desc: A bottle of brackish tears collected from a culled panopt.

3938.name: Mantid Foreleg
3938.desc: A foreleg from a mantid that is as sharp as any blade.

3939.name: Mantid Carapace
3939.desc: A sturdy exoskeleton that once kept a vicious mantid safe from the elements and enemies.

3940.name: Carrier Crab Crpc.
3940.desc: A rigid and rocky shell that once housed a carrier crab.

3941.name: Chapuli Wing
3941.desc: This wing torn from the back of a chapuli is as light as a feather and flexible enough to survive tempestuous gales.

3942.name: Loc. Elutriator
3942.desc: An interesting specimen of creature that resembles a chapuli. Though normally associated with death and destruction, this type of beast possesses a certain organ which aids in flushing out poisons.

3943.name: Chapuli Fltchg.
3943.desc: Chapuli wings that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

3944.name: Chapuli Arrowhd.
3944.desc: The striking tips of chapuli wing arrows.

3945.name: Mdr. Bolt Heads
3945.desc: The striking tips of midrium bolts.

3946.name: Mantid Arrowhd.
3946.desc: The striking tips of mantid foreleg arrows.

3947.name: Dmc. Bolt Heads
3947.desc: The striking tips of damascus bolts.

3948.name: Tulfaire Fltchg.
3948.desc: Tulfaire feathers that are fitted  to the nock end of arrow shafts.  They add to the directional stability  of arrows in flight.

3949.name: Tit. Bolt Heads
3949.desc: The striking tips of titanium bolts.

3950.name: Pulchridopt Wing
3950.desc: The wing of a black twitherym that flutters within the deepest recesses of otherworldly skirmishes. Its breathtaking beauty often causes pioneers to forget themselves and stare into its velvet sheen for minutes on end.

3951.name: Wailing Stone
3951.desc: Cold to the touch, this adumbral stone emits faint, ghoulish screams that pioneers liken to a convocation of the damned.

3952.name: Wailing Stone +1
3952.desc: Cold to the touch, this adumbral stone emits faint, ghoulish screams that pioneers liken to a convocation of the damned.

3953.name: Wailing Stone +2
3953.desc: Cold to the touch, this adumbral stone emits faint, ghoulish screams that pioneers liken to a convocation of the damned.

3954.name: Ghastly Stone
3954.desc: Cold to the touch, this pallid stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

3955.name: Ghastly Stone +1
3955.desc: Cold to the touch, this pallid stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

3956.name: Ghastly Stone +2
3956.desc: Cold to the touch, this pallid stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

3957.name: Coalition Grease
3957.desc: Developed by the Inventors' Coalition, this sorcerous lubricant brings out the true power of certain pieces of equipment.

3958.name: Yahse Humus
3958.desc: This mineral-rich soil taken from the Yahse Hunting Grounds is as nutritious as it is natural.

3959.name: D. Oro. Steak
3959.desc: Meat sliced efficiently from the body of one of the Zoldeff River's tastiest inhabitants--the Delectable Orobon.

3960.name: Celadon Yggrete
3960.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral green glow.

3961.name: Zaffre Yggrete
3961.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral blue glow.

3962.name: Alizarin Yggrete
3962.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral red glow.

3965.name: Adoulinian Kelp
3965.desc: A type of seaweed that is often harvested along the shores of the Ulbukan continent.

3966.name: Soul Spectacles
3966.desc: Glasses used in the process of measuring soul pyres.

3967.name: Kupon A-DeII
3967.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from bosses in Delve.

3968.name: Kupon A-De
3968.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from notorious monsters in Delve.

3969.name: Kupon A-Sal
3969.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for Salvge +1 armor.

3970.name: Kupon A-Nyz
3970.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU).Tradeable for armor from Nyzul Isle Uncharted Survey.

3971.name: Kupon I-S5
3971.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for rank V simulacra segments.

3972.name: Kupon I-S2
3972.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for rank II simulacra segments.

3973.name: Kupon I-Orche
3973.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for orchestrion sheet music.

3974.name: Airlixir
3974.desc: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3975.name: Airlixir +1
3975.desc: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3976.name: Airlixir +2
3976.desc: This mystical and magical solution may be as intangible as the air itself, but it still draws out immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

3977.name: Gabbrath Horn
3977.desc: The mafic horn of a culled gabbrath that remains doused in the blood of the countless pioneers unfortunate enough to be impaled upon it.

3978.name: Rockfin Fin
3978.desc: A rigid dorsal fin with a surprisingly clean and soft shape. It once belonged to a rockfin before the beast was fished up from the depths of the unknown.

3979.name: Rockfin Tooth
3979.desc: Sharper than the most finely whetted blade, this rockfin tooth can slice through an unwary pioneer's fingers in an instant.

3980.name: Bztavian Stinger
3980.desc: Once attached to the abdomen of an enraged bztavian, this stinger would make even the winds retreat in fear when thrust.

3981.name: Bztavian Wing
3981.desc: This wing ripped forcefully from the back of a bztavian would cause roaring gales to erupt whenever flapped.

3982.name: Arborscent Seed
3982.desc: That this seed will produce vegetation is known. Where the seed came from, however, is not.

3983.name: Arborfruit Seed
3983.desc: That this seed will spring forth fruit is known. Where the seed came from, however, is not.

3984.name: G. Seed Pouch
3984.desc: A pouch containing the seeds of a golden yellow flower.

3985.name: M. Beetle Saliva
3985.desc: Viscous, noxious drool scraped from the mouth of a massive insect that slobbers uncontrollably. Slathering it on mineral veins attracts antlions.

3986.name: Ch. Tree Sap
3986.desc: A fragrant fluid that releases an odor many insects crave. Placing it in an arboreal grove makes it possible to harvest beetles occasionally.

3988.name: Garden Worm
3988.desc: A long and lithe slimy slitherer that enriches the soil wherever it tunnels. Increases the yield of crops from a furrow.

3989.name: Grove Humus
3989.desc: This humus apparently has no official origin, as scholars have yet to ascertain its native habitat. Reduces the length of a furrow's grow season.

3990.name: Coalition Humus
3990.desc: This spectacular example of humus was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally produces strange and wondrous harvests.

3991.name: Grove Worm
3991.desc: A long and lithe slimy slitherer that enriches the soil wherever it tunnels. Increases the bounty obtained from a grove.

3992.name: Grove Mulch
3992.desc: This specialized mulch works even in the unforgiving Ulbukan soil, keeping the trees growing straight and strong. Occasionally increases the number of times one can log a grove.

3993.name: Grove Cuttings
3993.desc: Cuttings from trees nestled in an unspecified forest somewhere in Vana'diel. Enables lumber to be more easily procured from groves.

3994.name: Entisyrup
3994.desc: This thick and viscous nectar emits an aroma so enticing that many pioneers begin to slaver simply at the thought of tasting it. Allows insects to be more easily procured from groves.

3995.name: Coalit. Fertilizer
3995.desc: This spectacular example of fertilizer was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous finds to flourish in a grove.

3996.name: Stone Serum
3996.desc: A solution that magically softens the surface of mineral veins and primes them for mining. Increases the yield of a vein when mined.

3997.name: Vein Serum
3997.desc: A solution that magically stiffens the surface of mineral veins, which keeps them from degrading quickly. Occasionally increases the number of times a vein can be mined.

3998.name: Auserum
3998.desc: A solution that transmogrifies the surface of mineral veins, completely changing their composition. Allows for precious metals to be more easily procured while mining.

3999.name: Smelting Serum
3999.desc: A solution that transmogrifies the surface of mineral veins, completely changing their composition. Allows for metals to be more easily procured while mining.

4000.name: Coalition Serum
4000.desc: This spectacular example of a serum was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous finds to flourish when mining.

4001.name: Sardine Sphere
4001.desc: A ball of ground sardine mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4002.name: Bass Sphere
4002.desc: A ball of ground bass mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4003.name: Shrimp Sphere
4003.desc: A ball of ground shrimp mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4004.name: Baitball
4004.desc: A ball of ground mincemeat mixed with grains specifically added to attract fish inhabiting freshwater lakes and ponds.

4005.name: Super Baitball
4005.desc: This spectacular example of rolled bait was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous catches when fishing in freshwater.

4006.name: Sardine Chum
4006.desc: A sack of mangled sardine mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4007.name: Bass Chum
4007.desc: A sack of mangled bass mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4008.name: Shrimp Chum
4008.desc: A sack of mangled shrimp mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4009.name: Mincemeat Chum
4009.desc: A sack of mincemeat slapped together and mixed with grains specifically added to attract saltwater fish.

4010.name: Coalition Chum
4010.desc: This spectacular example of chum was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Occasionally allows for strange and wondrous catches when fishing in saltwater.

4011.name: Grow-M-Good
4011.desc: This spectacular example of a catalytic enzyme was released upon Ulbuka by the Inventors' Coalition after months of careful research, which have paid off in spades. Makes crops harvestable in almost no time.

4012.name: Waktza Rostrum
4012.desc: Though this beak was already torn violently from the head of a conquered waktza, lightning still crackles and darts around it, temporarily paralyzing any who dare to touch its dagger-blade surface.

4013.name: Waktza Crest
4013.desc: Once crowning the head of a proud waktza, this delicately soft feather belies the ferocity of its former master. Due to its difficulty to obtain, many fortunate enough to get their hands upon one employ it as a good luck charm.

4014.name: Yggdreant Bole
4014.desc: This sturdy trunk hacked free of a yggdreant after a flurry of agonizing attempts puts the fear of the forest into each and every pioneer who dares steal even a glimpse at it.

4015.name: Yggdreant Root
4015.desc: This knotted and gnarled root, chopped from the base of a fallen yggdreant, is rumored to bestow everlasting life upon those who boil it in their tea.

4017.name: Mand. Sprout
4017.desc: The verdant, two-leafed shoot that emerges from the heads of all mandragoras. Contemporary researchers proffer that its leaves capture sunlight and convert it into a sort of nourishment.

4018.name: Guatambu Log
4018.desc: A rough-cut log of guatambu timber.

4020.name: Titanium Ore
4020.desc: This ore contains the metal titanium.

4023.name: Snowsteel Ore
4023.desc: This ore contains the precious metal snowsteel.

4026.name: Akaso
4026.desc: A grass common throughout the Ulbukan continent. Fiber taken from its stem is used as a material for akaso thread.

4028.name: Akaso Cloth
4028.desc: Cloth woven from akaso fiber thread.

4029.name: Runeweave
4029.desc: This jet-black cloth contains the faintest remains of runic sorcery woven into its fabric.

4030.name: Sekishitsu
4030.desc: Originating from a country in the Far East, this blood red and pitch black coating, made from the sap of the lacquer tree, is often used on wooden crafts.

4031.name: Snap. Secretion
4031.desc: The digestive juices that rest inside a snapweed's mouth and trap whatever prey is unfortunate enough to wander inside.

4032.name: Snap. Tendril
4032.desc: The still-twitching and vine-like limb of a snapweed.

4033.name: Verdigris St.
4033.desc: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4034.name: Verdigris St. +1
4034.desc: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4035.name: Verdigris St. +2
4035.desc: Cold to the touch, this sickly green stone is as ghoulishly powerful as it is frigid.

4036.name: Lebondopt Wing
4036.desc: The wing of a black twitherym that flutters within the deepest recesses of otherworldly skirmishes. Its inconceivably dark color often reminds pioneers how pathetically miniscule they are in the grand scheme of things.

4037.name: Yorcia Visage I
4037.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4038.name: Yorcia Visage II
4038.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4039.name: Yorcia Visage III
4039.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4040.name: Yorcia Visage IV
4040.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4041.name: Yorcia Visage V
4041.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

4042.name: Acuex Ore
4042.desc: This ore developed incalculable veins of enigmatic sorcerous minerals while gestating in the stomach of an acuex.

4043.name: Lavarion
4043.desc: A resplendent jewel streaked with slowly snaking lines of magma.

4044.name: Atramenterrane
4044.desc: Its inky slick appearance and metallic lustre are but two clues to the subterranean powers this rock harbors, such as mitigating the forces of water.

4045.name: Cyclone Cotton
4045.desc: Forcefully shorn from its stem by tempestuous winds that churn with the power of the heavens, this bulb of cotton came from locales unknown.

4046.name: Corroded Ore
4046.desc: Dyed a bleeding red hue from various chemical reactions, this craggy and porous mass of minerals has an appearance so miserable that not even a miner could commiserate with it.

4047.name: Rdt. Silk Thread
4047.desc: An extremely strong and incredibly durable thread of silk harvested from erucas that slink about the crevasses of Yorcia Weald.

4048.name: Dragoneye
4048.desc: Speckled with bumps around its eyes, this fish can be found in the rivers and lakes of the Ulbukan continent's archipelago. Many marine life maniacs, including the Green Thumb Moogle, covet this precious fish.

4049.name: Rusted Coin
4049.desc: An ancient piece of currency from the east, hauled in from the depths of the ocean and coated with a heavy layer of rust. Coin collectors like the Green Thumb Moogle may find this of interest.

4050.name: Fat Burrow Worm
4050.desc: So rotund that it would hardly fit in a Meeble's mouth, this rare specimen of burrow worm is prized by not only the burrow diggers themselves, but by the Green Thumb Moogle as well.

4051.name: Alloy Tonic
4051.desc: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4052.name: Alloy Tonic +1
4052.desc: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4053.name: Alloy Tonic +2
4053.desc: This mystical solution is inexplicably absorbed by myriad metals, which seems to be how it draws out the immense powers that lay dormant within certain pieces of equipment.

4054.name: Beetle Spirit
4054.desc: The crystallized soul of a beetle as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4055.name: Antlion Spirit
4055.desc: The crystallized soul of an antlion as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4056.name: Pachy. Spirit
4056.desc: The crystallized soul of a pachypodium as black as midnight. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

4057.name: Gelid Aggregate
4057.desc: A frigid crystalline mass, stones of this type occasionally contain pellucid gems that create dazzling displays of light.

4058.name: Bismuth Ore
4058.desc: Dizzying numbers of veins containing the mineral bismuth crisscross the interior of this ore.

4059.name: Pluton
4059.desc: This naturally annealed conglomeration was formed from magma that once coursed through deep subterranean channels.

4060.name: Beitetsu
4060.desc: This somber hunk of ore contains an extremely rare type of iron that rumors say will revolutionize the smithing process.

4061.name: Riftborn Boulder
4061.desc: A shiny, jet-black gemstone wrested from the abyssal depths of an unexplored crevasse.

4062.name: Microcosmic Orb
4062.desc: A gem so solid that dropping it from a fair height supposedly leaves fissures in the immediate area.

4063.name: Macrocosmic Orb
4063.desc: A gem so solid that dropping it from a fair height supposedly leaves fissures in the earth for malms.

4064.name: Rem's Tale Ch.1
4064.desc: The first chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. The kingdom is fraught with chaos, as a long-ruling king collapses. His death engenders a terrible power struggle, from which Rem miraculously emerges the victor.

4065.name: Rem's Tale Ch.2
4065.desc: The second chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the leader of a neighboring empire takes advantage of Rem's inexperience and invades his homeland.

4066.name: Rem's Tale Ch.3
4066.desc: The third chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, Rem's kingdom is unceremoniously routed by the invaders and annexed to their territory.

4067.name: Rem's Tale Ch.4
4067.desc: The fourth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, Rem is stripped of his crown and imprisoned within his own palace's oubliette.

4068.name: Rem's Tale Ch.5
4068.desc: The fifth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the protagonist struggles to maintain his sanity while locked in what is ostensibly a granite-lined hole for a full decade.

4069.name: Rem's Tale Ch.6
4069.desc: The sixth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, a rowdy band of vagabonds who long for the good old days of Rem's rule slips into the oubliette under the cover of darkness and saves him in his eleventh year of imprisonment.

4070.name: Rem's Tale Ch.7
4070.desc: The seventh chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, news of Rem's rescue rings throughout the land and galvanizes the masses to stand up in resistance to the neighboring tyrant.

4071.name: Rem's Tale Ch.8
4071.desc: The eighth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the hefty number of resistance fighters rise up against their oppressive foreign king.

4072.name: Rem's Tale Ch.9
4072.desc: The ninth chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the resistance movement succeeds against all odds. The well-trained phalanxes of infantry from the neighboring kingdom lie battered and defeated outside the castle gates.

4073.name: Rem's Tale Ch.10
4073.desc: The final chapter of ten in the saga of an ordinary man named Rem. In it, the crown is returned to Rem thirteen years after the whole ordeal began. Everyone lives happily ever after under his fair and just rule.

4074.name: Thought Crystal
4074.desc: The crystallized remains of suspended thought memories, they contain a faint trace of life.

4075.name: Hope Crystal
4075.desc: The crystallized remains of suspended hope memories, they contain a faint trace of life.

4076.name: Fulfillment Crystal
4076.desc: The crystallized remains of suspended fulfillment memories, they contain a faint trace of life.

4077.name: Moonbow Steel
4077.desc: This tama-hagane was refined in Reisenjima and shines with seven colors when struck by the moon's light. The technique for refining it has been lost to eternity.

4078.name: Moonbow Cloth
4078.desc: This fabric was woven in Reisenjima and shines with seven colors when struck by the moon's light. The technique for weaving it has been lost to eternity.

4079.name: Moonbow Leather
4079.desc: This leather was made in Reisenjima and shines with seven colors when struck by the moon's light. The technique for making it has been lost to eternity.

4080.name: Moonbow Urushi
4080.desc: This paint was mixed in Reisenjima and shines with seven colors when struck by the moon's light. The technique for mixing it has been lost to eternity.

4081.name: Moonbow Stone
4081.desc: This gem was polished in Reisenjima and shines with seven colors when struck by the moon's light. The technique for polishing it has been lost to eternity.

4082.name: Moonlight Coral
4082.desc: This coral from Reisenjima gives off a warm and soft light.

4083.name: Ber. Arrowheads
4083.desc: The striking tips of beryllium arrows.

4084.name: Mixed Fletchings
4084.desc: A mixture of porxie wings and chapuli wings that are fitted to the nock end of arrow shafts. They add to the directional stability of arrows in flight.

4085.name: Ber. Bolt Heads
4085.desc: The striking tips of beryllium bolts.

4086.name: Lustreless Scale
4086.desc: A blackened scale that has long lost its lustre.

4087.name: Lustreless Hide
4087.desc: A blackened hide that has long lost its lustre.

4088.name: Lustreless Wing
4088.desc: A blackened wing that has long lost its lustre.

4089.name: Bonanza Pearl
4089.desc: A Mog Bonanza pearl inscribed with the numbers which you have selected. Issued by the Mog House Management Union.

4090.name: Dream Fl. Petal
4090.desc: A redolent flower petal. Essential oil extraced from it can be used to enhance one's dreams.

4091.name: BB Cowbell A.
4091.desc: A perfect replica of the cowbells used for Vana'diel's most famous buffaloes. Collect all three types and bring them to Jeuno for a special reward.

4092.name: BB Cowbell B.
4092.desc: A perfect replica of the cowbells used for Vana'diel's most famous buffaloes. Collect all three types and bring them to Jeuno for a special reward.

4093.name: BB Cowbell G.
4093.desc: A perfect replica of the cowbells used for Vana'diel's most famous buffaloes. Collect all three types and bring them to Jeuno for a special reward.

4094.name: Goblin Stew 880
4094.desc: The epitome of soups everywhere, this stew was cooked in the year 880. The longer it ages, the better it tastes, but most Goblins cannot bear to leave it sitting for more than a few minutes before their appetites get the best of them.

4281.name: Red Hot Cracker
4281.desc: Before eating one of these spicy crackers, be sure to find a bottle of distilled water to put out the fire in your mouth. "Beast Killer"+12 "Resist Sleep"+12 HP recovered while healing +9

4282.desc: People praise the pleasant properties of pipin' hot popotoes. HP+25 VIT+3 HP recovered while healing +1

4283.name: Choco-delight
4283.desc: The smooth, rich, and creamy flavor of this beverage puts regular chocomilk to shame. MP recovered while healing +5

4284.name: Tender Navarin

5109.name: Frayed Sack (A1)
5109.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of a refined stone that resembles a lush forest.

5110.name: Frayed Sack (A2)
5110.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of a refined stone that resembles a lush forest.

5111.name: Frayed Sack (M1)
5111.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of a refined stone that resembles a decomposing corpse.

5112.name: Frayed Sack (M2)
5112.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of a refined stone that resembles a decomposing corpse.

5113.name: Cracked Nut
5113.desc: An indehiscent fruit with a fractured shell.

5114.name: Moist Rolanberry
5114.desc: An especially tart rolanberry smothered in insect saliva to preserve its freshness.

5115.name: R. Moko Grass
5115.desc: Moko grass that has been left in sorry shape after being devoured by voracious insects.

5116.name: Cavorting Worm
5116.desc: A worm that wiggles and flourishes so joyfully that it almost seems to be smiling.

5117.name: Levigated Rock
5117.desc: Every granule of this strange powder clings to one's body with almost palpable desperation.

5118.name: Little Lugworm
5118.desc: A rather small example of toads' favorite treat.

5119.name: Training Manual
5119.desc: A tome bursting at the spine with drills as numerous as they are rigorous.

5120.name: Titanic Sawfish
5120.desc: Feared by sailors and pirates alike, this giant sea monster has been known to tear apart ships with its razor-sharp teeth.

5121.name: Moorish Idol
5121.desc: This tropical fish makes its home among the coral of the Maliyakaleya Reef. The Tarutaru love to keep them as pets.

5122.name: Bibiki Slug
5122.desc: This echinoderm is found all over Bibiki Bay. While edible, you will not find any gracing the menu of a four-star restaurant.

5123.name: Jacknife
5123.desc: This bivalve shellfish lives in the sands of Bibiki Bay. It scares away predators by shooting out of its shell like a rocket.

5124.name: Tropical Clam
5124.desc: This bivalve shellfish lives in the sands of Bibiki Bay. Recently, excess gathering has led to a decline in their numbers.

5125.name: Phanauet Newt
5125.desc: This green amphibian is found only in the Phanauet Channel.

5126.name: Muddy Siredon
5126.desc: A tiny newt is found only in the Phanauet Channel. It is easily recognizable by its large outer gills.

5127.name: Gugrusaurus
5127.desc: A mythical reptile said to roam the seven seas.

5128.name: Cone Calamary
5128.desc: This variety of squid is known for its cone-shaped body.

5129.name: Lik
5129.desc: A fabled freshwater fish, legendary for its enormous length.

5130.name: Tavnazian Goby
5130.desc: A freshwater fish found in the rivers of the Tavnazian Archipelago. It is easily recognized by its orange and silver stripes.

5131.name: Vongola Clam
5131.desc: This bivalve shellfish found in the waters around Purgonorgo Isle is known for its exquisite flavor.

5132.name: Gurnard
5132.desc: This saltwater fish lives in the Gugru Blue. It can fly in the air, but only for a short time.

5133.name: Pterygotus
5133.desc: This giant arthropod is also known as a "sea scorpion."

5134.name: Mola Mola
5134.desc: Legends say that this enormous saltwater fish has rescued shipwreck victims at sea.

5135.name: Rhinochimera
5135.desc: This enormous saltwater fish has a swordlike tusk growing from its head.

5136.name: Istavrit
5136.desc: This saltwater fish inhabits the reefs of the Arrapago Islands. Its fins are extremely poisonous.

5137.name: Turnabaligi
5137.desc: This enormous freshwater fish sometimes preys on waterbirds and unsuspecting beasts that venture too close to the water's edge.

5138.name: Black Ghost
5138.desc: This freshwater fish gives off light from inside its body.

5139.name: Betta
5139.desc: This beautifully colored fish possesses a strong fighting instinct. People often raise bettas to watch them fight or simply for viewing.

5140.name: Kalkanbaligi
5140.desc: This legendary giant saltwater fish is also referred to as a "Gigas's Shield."

5141.name: Veydal Wrasse
5141.desc: This enormous saltwater fish is named after a legendary admiral.

5142.name: Bison Steak
5142.desc: A juicy slab of medium rare bison meat. STR+6 AGI+1 INT-3 Attack+18% (Max.90) Ranged Attack+18% (Max.90) "Lizard Killer"+5

5143.name: Goblin Stir-Fry
5143.desc: A typical stir-fry often served at festivals. The seasoning has been adjusted to meet the tastes of all non-Goblins. VIT+2 AGI+5 CHR-5

5144.name: Crimson Jelly
5144.desc: The detoxified gelatinous remains of a clot, whipped up into a tasty dessert. MP+12% (Max. 85) INT+6 MP recovered while healing +2

5152.name: Buffalo Meat

5158.desc: This is about as tasty as you can make the detoxified gelatinous remains of a clot. MP+12% (Max. 90) INT+6 MP recovered while healing +2

5160.desc: If the urchin eggs on this sushi were any fresher, they'd hatch. HP+40 STR+1 VIT+6 Accuracy+16% (Max. 76) Ranged Accuracy+16% (Max. 76)

5184.desc: Vegetarians all over Vana'diel adore this specially prepared batagreen saute. VIT-1 AGI+1 Ranged Accuracy+7% (Max.20)

5185.desc: Tavnazian salad with a twist! HP+20 MP+20 DEX+4 VIT+6 AGI+4 CHR+4 DEF+25% (Max.160)

5195.desc: This refreshing herb is used to accent many types of cuisine.

5196.name: Buffalo Jerky

5197.name: Peperoncino +1
5197.desc: This spaghetti peperoncino is so hot, you will need at least Water IV to cool off the fire in your mouth. HP+30% (Max. 75) VIT+2 "Store TP"+6 "Resist Virus"+12

5198.name: Vongole Rosso +1
5198.desc: In a recent survey, 87% of all vongola clams replied spaghetti vongole +1 is the way they'd like to go. HP+20% (Max. 95) VIT+2 MND-1 DEF+25% (Max. 35) "Store TP"+6

5200.desc: 3 1/2 times better than the spaghetti pescatora your mom used to make. HP+15% (Max. 160) VIT+3 MND-1 DEF+22% (Max. 70) "Store TP"+6

5202.name: Nero Seppia +1

5206.desc: Sweet and sticky Tarutaru rice wrapped up in a fragrant tree leaf. This Far Eastern treat has been modified for local festivals.

5419.desc: A jet black, goblin-engineered, incendiary device designed for subterranean placement. Propels iron shrapnel in all directions upon detonation.

5420.name: Paralyzing Tube
5420.desc: An ingenious levitating smart-mine conceived of by Bastokan officer Adelheid Sturm. Causes only nearby enemies to become paralyzed upon detonation.

5421.name: Silencing Tube
5421.desc: An ingenious levitating smart-mine conceived of by Bastokan officer Adelheid Sturm. Causes only nearby enemies to become silenced upon detonation.

5422.name: Binding Tube
5422.desc: An ingenious levitating smart-mine conceived of by Bastokan officer Adelheid Sturm. Causes only nearby enemies to become bound upon detonation.

5423.name: Bottled Fay
5423.desc: Rumored to possess the power to return the bearer to a previous location if released. You remembered to punch holes in the lid.

5424.name: Serene Serinette
5424.desc: An exquisite miniature barrel organ housed in a wooden case of finely polished walnut. Turning its tiny crank causes it to play a gently soothing melody.

5425.name: Joyous Serinette
5425.desc: An exquisite miniature barrel organ housed in a wooden case of finely polished walnut. Turning its tiny crank causes it to play a rousing celebratory march.

5426.name: Maze Compass
5426.desc: A makeshift compass manufactured by Moblin Maze Mongers. It allows for a quick and easy escape from any maze.

5427.name: Maze Pearl
5427.desc: A refurbished linkpearl provided free of charge by Moblin Maze Mongers. It can be used to contact Chatnachoq, who will provide valuable advice for spelunking adventurers.

5428.name: Instant Retrace
5428.desc: The black magic spell Retrace is inscribed on this parchment.

5429.name: Tjn. Wildgrass
5429.desc: A type of wildgrass used to heighten the olfactory senses of chocobos.

5431.name: Dusty Potion
5431.desc: This potion instantly restores a few HP.

5432.name: Dusty Ether
5432.desc: This potion instantly restores a few MP.

5433.name: Dusty Elixir
5433.desc: This potion instantly restores a few HP and MP.

5434.name: Fanatic's Drink
5434.desc: This medicine temporarily nullifies all physical damage.

5435.name: Fool's Drink
5435.desc: This medicine temporarily nullifies all magical damage.

5436.name: Dusty Reraise
5436.desc: The white magic spell Reraise is inscribed on this parchment.

5438.name: Strange Juice
5438.desc: Juice, freshly squeezed from something strange...

5439.name: Vicar's Drink
5439.desc: This medicine instantly cures most status ailments.

5440.name: Dusty Wing
5440.desc: When used, these man-made wings instantly raise TP. Due to muscle strain, repeated use is prohibited.

5441.name: Angelwing
5441.desc: A supranatural firework created by a Goblin alchemist.

5442.name: Thunderguard Dart
5442.desc: This dart has been dipped in a powerful anesthetic used for subduing large game.

5443.name: Mg.Stone Pouch
5443.desc: This small velvet pouch is used to carry large quantities of magnus stones.

5444.name: Grauberg Greens
5444.desc: These pleasantly piquant leafy vegetables are favored by chocobos.

5445.name: Prismatic Elixir
5445.desc: When held into the light, this elixir shimmers with all the colors of the rainbow.

5446.name: Red Bubble-Eye
5446.desc: As its name implies, the area around the eyes of this red freshwater goldfish from the Far East is round and swollen.

5447.name: Denizanasi
5447.desc: This monstrous jellyfish has caused Al Zahbi fishermen many headaches.

5448.name: Kalamar
5448.desc: This cephalopod's most recognizable feature is its pointy shell.

5449.name: Hamsi
5449.desc: This saltwater fish lives in the Sicklemoon Straits.

5450.name: Lakerda
5450.desc: This enormous saltwater fish wanders the Cyan Deep.

5451.name: Kilicbaligi
5451.desc: This enormous saltwater fish infests the Cyan Deep, and is referred to as the "Sword of Cyan."

5452.name: Uskumru
5452.desc: A saltwater fish that inhabits the Cyan Deep.

5453.name: Istakoz
5453.desc: This crustacean lives on the sea bottom around the Arrapago Islands.

5454.name: Mercanbaligi
5454.desc: This saltwater fish inhabits the seas around the Arrapago Islands.

5455.name: Ahtapot
5455.desc: This shelled cephalopod inhabits the seas around the Arrapago Islands.

5456.name: Istiridye
5456.desc: A variety of bivalve that can be found on the reefs of the Arrapago Islands.

5457.name: Dil
5457.desc: This saltwater fish inhabits the seas around the Arrapago Islands.

5458.name: Yilanbaligi
5458.desc: This freshwater fish inhabits the rivers of the Near East.

5459.name: Sazanbaligi
5459.desc: This freshwater fish inhabits the rivers of the Near East.

5460.name: Kayabaligi
5460.desc: This freshwater fish lives on the river bottoms of the Near East. Its orange body is covered in bright silver stripes.

5461.name: Alabaligi
5461.desc: This freshwater fish lives in the clear waters of the Near East.

5463.name: Yayinbaligi
5463.desc: An enormous freshwater fish that inhabits the lakes and marshes of the Aradjiah continent.

5464.name: Kaplumbaga
5464.desc: A simple-minded variety of turtle that inhabits the lakes and marshes of the Near East.

5465.name: Caedarva Frog
5465.desc: A variety of amphibian that inhabits the Caedarva Mire.

5466.name: Trumpet Shell
5466.desc: This carnivorous mollusk with a conical shell is found only in the Vunkerl Inlet.

5467.name: Megalodon
5467.desc: Also known as the "Don of the Deep," this colossal saltwater fish is said to reside in the cold waters of the Bastore Sea.

5468.name: Matsya
5468.desc: This legendary saltwater fish is said to prowl the fathomless depths.

5469.name: Brass Loach
5469.desc: A freshwater fish found only in the Grauberg wetlands.

5470.name: Pirarucu
5470.desc: A giant freshwater fish found in the rivers of Grauberg.

5472.name: Garpike
5472.desc: A saltwater fish found only in the sparsely salinated waters of the Vunkerl Inlet.

5473.name: Bastore Sweeper
5473.desc: A tasty shrimp found in the Vunkerl Inlet.

5474.name: Ca Cuong
5474.desc: A carnivorous insect that lives in waters of the Grauberg wetlands.

5475.name: Gigant Octopus
5475.desc: This gargantuan cephalopod makes its home beneath the boulders that litter the floor of the Bastore Sea.

5477.name: Warrior Die
5477.desc: COR Lv.49 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Fighter's Roll."  Improves "Double Attack" rate for party members within area of effect.

5478.name: Monk Die
5478.desc: COR Lv.31 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Monk's Roll." Enhances "Subtle Blow" effect for party members within area of effect.

5479.name: White Mage Die
5479.desc: COR Lv.20 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Healer's Roll."  Increases potency of "Cure" effect for party members within area of effect.

5480.name: Black Mage Die
5480.desc: COR Lv.58 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Wizard's Roll."  Enhances magic attack for party members within area of effect.

5481.name: Red Mage Die
5481.desc: COR Lv.46 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Warlock's Roll."  Enhances magic accuracy for party members within area of effect.

5482.name: Thief Die
5482.desc: COR Lv.43 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Rogue's Roll."  Improves critical hit rate for party members within area of effect.

5483.name: Paladin Die
5483.desc: COR Lv.55 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Gallant's Roll."  Enhances defense for party members within area of effect.

5484.name: Dark Knight Die
5484.desc: COR Lv.14 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Chaos Roll."  Enhances attacks and ranged attacks for party members within area of effect.

5485.name: Beastmaster Die
5485.desc: COR Lv.34 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Beast Roll."  Enhances pet attacks and ranged attacks for party members within area of effect.

5486.name: Bard Die
5486.desc: COR Lv.26 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Choral Roll."  Decreases spell interruption rate for party members within area of effect.

5487.name: Ranger Die
5487.desc: COR Lv.11 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Hunter's Roll."  Enhances accuracy and ranged accuracy for party members within area of effect.

5488.name: Samurai Die
5488.desc: COR Lv.37 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Samurai Roll."  Enhances "Store TP" effect for party members within area of effect.

5489.name: Ninja Die
5489.desc: COR Lv.8 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Ninja Roll."  Enhances evasion for party members within area of effect.

5490.name: Dragoon Die
5490.desc: COR Lv.23 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Drachen Roll."  Enhances pet accuracy and ranged accuracy for party members within area of effect.

5491.name: Summoner Die
5491.desc: COR Lv.40 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Evoker's Roll."  Gradually restores MP for party members within area of effect.

5492.name: Blue Mage Die
5492.desc: COR Lv.17 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Magus's Roll."  Enhances magic defense for party members within area of effect.

5493.name: Corsair Die
5493.desc: COR Lv.5 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Corsair's Roll."  Increases the amount of experience points earned by party members within area of effect.

5494.name: Puppetmaster Die
5494.desc: COR Lv.52 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Puppet Roll."  Enhances pet magic attack and magic accuracy for party members within area of effect.

5495.name: Dancer Die
5495.desc: COR Lv.61 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Dancer's Roll."  Gradually restores HP for party members within area of effect.

5496.name: Scholar Die
5496.desc: COR Lv.64 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Scholar's Roll."  Grants Conserve MP effect to party members within area of effect.

5497.name: Bolter's Die
5497.desc: COR Lv.76 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Bolter's Roll." Increases movement speed for party members within area of effect.

5498.name: Caster's Die
5498.desc: COR Lv.79 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Caster's Roll." Enhances "Fast Cast" effect for party members within area of effect.

5499.name: Courser's Die
5499.desc: COR Lv.81 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Courser's Roll." Enhances "Snapshot" effect for party members within area of effect.

5500.name: Blitzer's Die
5500.desc: COR Lv.83 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Blitzer's Roll." Reduces melee attack delay for party members within area of effect.

5501.name: Tactician's Die
5501.desc: COR Lv.86 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Tactician's Roll." Gradually increases TP for party members within area of effect.

5502.name: Allies' Die
5502.desc: COR Lv.89 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Allies' Roll." Enhances skillchain damage and skillchain accuracy for party members within area of effect.

5503.name: Miser's Die
5503.desc: COR Lv.92 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Miser's Roll." Grants "Save TP" effect to party members within area of effect.

5504.name: Companion's Die
5504.desc: COR Lv.95 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Companion's Roll." Grants "Regain" and "Regen" effects to pets of party members within area of effect.

5505.name: Avenger's Die
5505.desc: COR Lv.97 Teaches the Phantom Roll ability "Avenger's Roll." Increases chance of countering for party members within area of effect.

5506.name: E. Tincture (IB)
5506.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Immutable Boulder in Yahse Hunting Grounds. Temporarily increases maximum HP.

5507.name: E. Tincture (LI)
5507.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Luminous Isle in Yahse Hunting Grounds. Temporarily increases INT, MND, and CHR.

5508.name: E. Tincture (FI)
5508.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Flourishing Island in Ceizak Battlegrounds. Temporarily increases maximum MP.

5509.name: E. Tincture (BS)
5509.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Bud of the Swarm in Ceizak Battlegrounds. Temporarily increases attack power.

5510.name: E. Tincture (IS)
5510.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Immaculate Sands in Foret de Hennetiel. Temporarily increases STR and VIT.

5511.name: E. Tincture (DD)
5511.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Dragon Driftwood in Foret de Hennetiel. Temporarily increases magic attack power and magic accuracy.

5512.name: E. Tincture (FF)
5512.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Fruit of Fecundity in Foret de Hennetiel. Temporarily increases DEX and AGI.

5513.name: E. Tincture (TB)
5513.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Torchbloom in Foret de Hennetiel. Temporarily increases the likelihood "Double Attack" will activate.

5514.name: E. Tincture (LL)
5514.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Lake of Light in Sih Gates. Temporarily grants the effect of "Fast Cast."

5515.name: E. Tincture (PG)
5515.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Prominence of the Gales in Sih Gates. Temporarily reduces physical damage taken.

5516.name: E. Tincture (SS)
5516.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Sweltering Spring in Moh Gates. Temporarily increases accuracy.

5517.name: E. Tincture (PF)
5517.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Prominence of the Flame in Moh Gates. Temporarily increases critical hit rate.

5518.name: E. Tincture (SL)
5518.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Sanctum of Life in Cirdas Caverns. Temporarily grants the effect of "Regen."

5519.name: E. Tincture (PS)
5519.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Prominence of the Soil in Cirdas Caverns. Temporarily grants the effect of "Refresh."

5520.name: E. Tincture (SA)
5520.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Snowdrift Arbor in Morimar Basalt Fields. Temporarily grants a defense bonus.

5521.name: E. Tincture (FB)
5521.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Frostbloom in Morimar Basalt Fields. Instantly increases TP.

5522.name: E. Tincture (SP)
5522.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Spring of Prosperity in Morimar Basalt Fields. Temporarily reduces magic damage taken.

5523.name: E. Tincture (PC)
5523.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Pool of Clarity in Yorcia Weald. Temporarily increases likelihood of blocking with a shield.

5524.name: E. Tincture (BB)
5524.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Bryophitic Boulder in Yorcia Weald. Temporarily increases chance of countering.

5525.name: E. Tincture (OG)
5525.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Overgrown Grove in Yorcia Weald. Temporarily increases evasion.

5526.name: E. Tincture (FrB)
5526.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Bud of the Fragrant Breeze in Marjami Ravine.

5527.name: E. Tincture (WA)
5527.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Whitewater Arbor in Marjami Ravine. Temporarily grants the effects of "Phalanx."

5528.name: E. Tincture (CT)
5528.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Crag of the Triumvirate in Marjami Ravine. Temporarily increases enmity over time.

5529.name: E. Tincture (SaS)
5529.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Saliferous Spring in Dho Gates. Temporarily reduces chance of having a spell interrupted.

5530.name: E. Tincture (PR)
5530.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Prominence of the Ripple in Dho Gates. Temporarily increases magic evasion.

5531.name: Lucerewe Meat
5531.desc: This massive hunk of raw lucerewe flesh has a unique pungency not completely unlike that of a sopping wet Mithra.

5532.name: Ichinin. Koma
5532.desc: A traditional type of firework that mimics the twirls and twists of a spinning top. The technology behind it has supposedly been handed down from generation to generation in the Far East.

5534.name: Apkallufa
5534.desc: This freshwater fish's resemblance to an apkallu is so striking that it fools many dim-witted Adoulinian collectors into believing it is the real bird. The only thing the two animals truly share is their habitat of Marjami Ravine.

5535.name: Deademoiselle
5535.desc: Despite its elegant appearance, the fact that this fish navigates the estuaries it calls home with its belly up takes most Adoulinians aback the first time they lay eyes on it.

5536.name: Yorchete
5536.desc: Shaped like a knife, this aquatic cutlery slices its way through the waters of Yorcia Weald with a force unrivaled by any of its other finned brethren.

5537.name: Soryu
5537.desc: A legendary serpent of prehistoric times.

5538.name: Sekiryu
5538.desc: A legendary serpent of prehistoric times.

5539.name: Hakuryu
5539.desc: A legendary serpent of prehistoric times.

5540.name: Kokuryu
5540.desc: A legendary serpent of prehistoric times.

5541.name: Royal Grape
5541.desc: The flavor of these grapes is so exquisite that they were once available only to royalty.

5542.name: Gat. aux Fraises
5542.desc: A Bastokan cake made for the Starlight Celebration. Symbolizes the fire of civilization, come to melt away the snow. HP+8 MP+8% (Max.50) INT+1 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5543.name: Midwinter Dream
5543.desc: Made by a master patissier, this gleaming gateau aux fraises is likely to show up in your best dreams. HP+10 MP+8% (Max.60) INT+2 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +2

5544.name: Crab Stewpot
5544.desc: A Far Eastern dish created with land crab meat, along with vegetables and a few other ingredients. Each piece of food may be removed and eaten as soon as it has finished cooking. HP+10% (Max. 50) MP+10 Evasion+5 DEF+20% (Max. 50) HP recovered while healing +5 MP recovered while healing +1

5545.name: Prm. Crab Stewpot
5545.desc: The cooking of this crab stewpot was strictly supervised to ensure that the ingredients were added in the proper manner. HP+10% (Max. 75) MP+15 VIT+1 AGI+1 MND+2 Evasion+6 DEF+20% (Max. 75) HP recovered while healing +7 MP recovered while healing +2

5546.name: Prz. Crab Stewpot
5546.desc: The cooking of this crab stewpot was directed so that the ingredients were masterfully utilized. Guaranteed to make your mouth water. HP+10% (Max. 100) MP+20 VIT+2 AGI+2 MND+4 Evasion+7 DEF+20% (Max. 100) HP recovered while healing +9 MP recovered while healing +3

5547.name: Beef Stewpot
5547.desc: A Far Eastern dish created with buffalo meat, along with vegetables and a few other ingredients. Each piece of food may be removed and eaten as soon as it has finished cooking. HP+10% (Max. 50) MP+10 Attack+18% (Max. 40) Evasion+5 HP recovered while healing +5 MP recovered while healing +1

5548.name: Prm. Beef Stewpot
5548.desc: The cooking of this beef stewpot was strictly supervised to ensure that the ingredients were added in the proper manner. HP+10% (Max. 75) MP+15 STR+2 AGI+1 MND+1 Attack+18% (Max. 60) Evasion+6 HP recovered while healing +7 MP recovered while healing +2

5549.name: Prz. Beef Stewpot
5549.desc: The cooking of this beef stewpot was directed so that the ingredients were masterfully utilized. Guaranteed to make your mouth water. HP+10% (Max. 100) MP+20 STR+4 AGI+2 MND+2 Attack+18% (Max. 80) Evasion+7 HP recovered while healing +9 MP recovered while healing +3

5550.name: Buche au Choco.
5550.desc: A San d'Orian cake made for the Starlight Celebration. Symbolizes thankfulness to the forest for firewood to keep homes warm. HP+8 MP+3% (Max.13) INT+2 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +4

5551.name: Sylvan Excursion
5551.desc: Made by a master patissier, this gleaming buche au chocolat is designed to melt on your tongue. HP+10 MP+3% (Max.15) INT+3 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +5

5552.name: Black Pudding
5552.desc: A Windurstian dessert made for the Starlight Celebration. Symbolizes a legendary monster feared by the Mithra. HP+8 MP+5% (Max.25) INT+4 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5553.name: Dusky Indulgence
5553.desc: Made by a master patissier, this gleaming black pudding simply begs for a taste. HP+10 MP+5% (Max.30) INT+5 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +2

5554.name: Squirrel's Delight
5554.desc: Made by a master patissier, this gleaming marron glace is sure to delight anyone who takes a bite. MP+13% (Max.95) MP recovered while healing +2

5555.name: Flurry Courante
5555.desc: Made by a master patissier, the taste of this gleaming icecap rolanberry will make you dance with joy. MP+19% (Max. 65) INT+2 +5 MP recovered while healing +1

5556.name: Seraph's Kiss
5556.desc: Made by a master patissier, one taste of this gleaming snoll gelato will leave you craving more. HP+15 MP+16% (Max.85) MP recovered while healing +2

5557.name: Mont Blanc
5557.desc: A San d'Orian cake heaped with chestnuts to symbolize the white mountains of the Uleguerand Range. HP+8 MP+10 INT+1 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5558.name: Golden Royale
5558.desc: A serving of mont blanc baked to perfection using the skills of a patissier. HP+10 MP+10 INT+2 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +2

5559.name: Mille-Feuille
5559.desc: A Windurstian cake made by stacking thin slices of pie crust to symbolize the leaves of the Great Star Tree. HP+8 MP+15 INT+1 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5560.name: Elysian Eclair
5560.desc: A serving of mille-feuille baked to perfection using the skills of a patissier. HP+10 MP+15 INT+2 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +2

5561.name: Moogle Pie
5561.desc: This apple pie is made by (not of) moogles. Although slightly burnt, a magical spell placed upon it by the baker makes it seem more delicious than it really is. HP+20 MP+20 STR+1 DEX+1 VIT+1 AGI+1 INT+1 MND+1 CHR+1

5562.name: White Honey
5562.desc: Pephredos make this sweet honey from flower nectar.

5563.name: Orobon Meat
5563.desc: The meat, skin, and insides of an orobon. Used as a main ingredient in a Far Eastern stewpot dish.

5564.name: Hydra Meat
5564.desc: The meat of the hydra, known as the finest delicacy in the Near East.

5565.name: Cerberus Meat
5565.desc: This Cerberus meat gives off heat even though it has never been cooked.

5566.name: Date
5566.desc: The sweet, sticky fruit of a date palm tree.

5567.name: Dried Date
5567.desc: This date has become even sweeter after dehydration. HP+10 MP+20 AGI-1 INT+3

5568.name: Apkallu Egg
5568.desc: This apkallu egg is a healthy addition to every balanced meal.

5569.name: Puk Egg
5569.desc: Puk eggs are a famous delicacy in the Near East.

5570.name: Chai
5570.desc: A black tea popular in Aht Urhgan. VIT-2 CHR+2

5571.name: Karakul Meat
5571.desc: Slightly tough meat from a variety of ram. A Near Eastern staple.

5572.name: Irmik Helvasi
5572.desc: A Near Eastern cake topped with pine nuts. Its subtle sweetness makes it go well with chai. HP+10% (Max.75) MP+3% (Max.13) INT+1

5573.name: Irmik Helvasi +1
5573.desc: An irmik helvasi baked to perfection by a master patissier. HP+10% (Max.100) MP+3% (Max.15) INT+2 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5574.name: Dried Date +1
5574.desc: These dried dates are unbelievably sweet. HP+12 MP+22 AGI-1 INT+4

5576.name: Ayran
5576.desc: A drink made from thinned yogurt.

5577.name: Sutlac
5577.desc: A pudding-style dessert made from rice, milk, and eggs. HP+8 MP+10 INT+1

5578.name: Sutlac +1
5578.desc: Only a master patissier could have made this exceptionally delicious serving of sutlac. HP+10 MP+10 INT+2 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5579.name: Yayla Corbasi
5579.desc: A Near Eastern soup made from yogurt and mint. HP+20 DEX-1 VIT+2 HP recovered while healing +3 MP recovered while healing +1

5580.name: Yayla Corbasi +1
5580.desc: You have never tasted a more delicious bowl of yayla corbasi. HP+25 DEX-1 VIT+3 HP recovered while healing +5 MP recovered while healing +2

5581.name: Ziz Meat
5581.desc: Ziz meat is widely known for its exquisite flavor.

5582.name: Patlican Salata
5582.desc: A salad formed from a blend of eggplants and yogurt. VIT-1 AGI+4 Evasion+6 HP recovered while healing +2

5583.name: Patlican Salata +1
5583.desc: Eggplants mixed with yogurt couldn't possibly taste any better than this. VIT-2 AGI+5 Evasion+7 HP recovered while healing +3

5584.name: Ic Pilav
5584.desc: A stir-fry dish made from rice, meat, and pine nuts. HP+14% (Max.65) STR+4 VIT-1 INT-1 Attack+22% (Max.65) Ranged Attack+22% (Max.65) HP recovered while healing +1

5585.name: Ic Pilav +1
5585.desc: Just looking at this delectable plate of ic pilav makes your mouth water. HP+14% (Max.70) STR+5 VIT-2 INT-2 Attack+22% (Max.70) Ranged Attack+22% (Max.70) HP recovered while healing +1

5586.name: Menemen
5586.desc: This Near Eastern omelette is packed with nutritious value. HP+30 MP+30 AGI+1 INT-1 HP recovered while healing +1 MP recovered while healing +1

5587.name: Menemen +1
5587.desc: This fluffy omelette is fit for the empress's table. HP+35 MP+35 AGI+2 INT-2 HP recovered while healing +2 MP recovered while healing +2

5588.name: Karni Yarik
5588.desc: An eggplant stuffed with meat and topped with tomatoes. VIT-1 AGI+3 Attack+20% (Max.65) Ranged Attack+20% (Max.65) Evasion+6

5589.name: Karni Yarik +1
5589.desc: This serving of karni yarik is roasted to perfection. VIT-2 AGI+4 Attack+22% (Max.70) Ranged Attack+22% (Max.70) Evasion+7

5590.name: Balik Sandvici
5590.desc: Uskumru meat sandwiched between two slices of white bread. DEX+3 AGI+1 INT+3 MND-2 Ranged Accuracy+5

5591.name: Blk. Sandvici +1
5591.desc: The most delicious balik sandvici you've ever tasted. DEX+3 AGI+1 INT+3 MND-2 Ranged Accuracy+6

5592.name: Imperial Coffee
