5907.desc: Easily identified by its flowering top, this vegetable is rich in both flavor and nutrients.

5908.name: Butterpear
5908.desc: Prized for its nutty flavor and melt-in-your-mouth texture, this nutritious vegetable is best consumed when it is neither too firm nor too soft to the touch.

5909.name: Pickled R. Tail
5909.desc: A speciality of the Rarab Tail Hostelry. Marinated with Chamama's secret blend of herbs and spices.

5910.name: Hvy. Metal Pouch
5910.desc: A pouch containing a sheet of heavy metal.

5911.name: Olde Rarab Tail
5911.desc: Marinated for fifty years according to a secret recipe passed down for generations at the Rarab Tail Hostelry, this pickled delicacy is only for the discerning palate.

5912.name: Gargou. Quiver
5912.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of gargouille arrows.

5913.name: A. Bolt Quiver
5913.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of adaman bolts.

5914.name: O. Bull. Pouch
5914.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of orichalcum bullets.

5915.name: A. Bull. Pouch
5915.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of adaman bullets.

5916.name: Temple Truffle
5916.desc: A Valentione's gift from Curilla of the San d'Orian Temple Knights. The message accompanying the exquisite confection reads, "I shall protect you always."

5917.name: Choco-scroll
5917.desc: A Valentione's gift from Regine of Regine's Magicmart. Teaches no spells whatsoever, but it sure tastes good.

5918.name: Choco-katana
5918.desc: A Valentione's gift from Ayame of the Mythril Musketeers. So sharp is the edge of the chocolate blade that it appears as if it could draw blood.

5919.name: Choco-ligar
5919.desc: This stylish Valentione's gift from fashion maven Brygid is a chocolate confection in the shape of a subligar. You'd best exercise after eating, lest it go straight to your hips!

5920.name: Fudgy-Wudge
5920.desc: A cloyingly sweet Valentione's confection from Kupipi, cleric of Heavens Tower. The rich chocolate flavor is si～mply divi～ne!

5921.name: Choco R. Tail
5921.desc: A Valentione's delicacy conceived by Chamama of the Rarab Tail Hostelry, this chocolate-covered pickle is said to be far more appetizing than it appears.

5922.name: Walnut Cookie
5922.desc: A crunchy and fragrant walnut cookie with a hint of cinnamon. "Bird Killer"+10 "Resist Paralyze"+10 HP recovered while healing +3 MP recovered while healing +6

5923.name: Juglan Jumble
5923.desc: A scrumptious cookie featuring sizable chunks of roasted walnut and an assortment of exotic sugars and spices. "Bird Killer"+12 "Resist Paralyze"+12 HP recovered while healing +5 MP recovered while healing +8

5924.name: S. Salis. Steak
5924.desc: This salisbury steak made from Cerberus meat emits thin wisps of smoke that never seem to die. HP+30 STR+7 INT-5 Attack+20% (Max. 160) Ranged Attack+20% (Max. 160) "Dragon Killer"+5

5925.name: C. Salis. Steak
5925.desc: This unsightly salisbury steak looks as if it has been blackened and charred by the flames of the underworld. HP+32 STR+8 INT-6 Attack+22% (Max. 165) Ranged Attack+22% (Max. 165) "Dragon Killer"+5

5926.name: Date Tea
5926.desc: An exotic black tea with the faint sweet aroma of dates. HP+20 MP+30 VIT-1 INT+3 CHR+5

5927.name: Caravan Tea
5927.desc: A particularly aromatic cup of date tea. Its delightful taste promises to soothe one's soul on even the longest, most arduous of journeys. HP+22 MP+32 INT+4 CHR+6

5928.name: Himesama R. Ball
5928.desc: A rice ball so dainty and tasty it's fit to be dined upon by a princess. HP+25 DEX+4 VIT+4 CHR+4 Effect: Attack+60 DEF+40 "Triple Attack"+1%

5929.name: Ojo Rice Ball
5929.desc: A himesama rice ball so perfectly formed one might think it was rolled by the loving hands of an actual princess. HP+50 DEX+5 VIT+5 CHR+5 Effect: Attack+60 DEF+40 "Triple Attack"+2%

5930.name: Sprightly Soup
5930.desc: This flavorful and spicy mushroom soup is sure to put a spring in your step. MP+30 MND+4 Enmity-4 MP recovered while healing +4

5931.name: Shimmy Soup
5931.desc: A mushroom soup so savory and scrumptious that it will have you dancing through the streets in joy. MP+32 MND+5 Enmity-5 MP recovered while healing +5

5934.name: Chocobiscuit
5934.desc: A crispy, crunchy cookie charmingly cut in the shape of a baby chocobo. CHR+3 Evasion+2 "Aquan Killer"+10 "Resist Silence"+10 MP recovered while healing +3

5935.name: Moogurt
5935.desc: A delightful dairy delicacy made with farm-fresh milk from the MHMU's Buffalo Bonanza Ranch. HP+20% (Max. 20) VIT+3 HP recovered while healing +3

5936.name: Mog Missile
5936.desc: A traditional moogle firework popular since the days of the lost kingdom of Kupalika.

5937.name: Bubble Breeze
5937.desc: An ephemeral firework designed around the theme of fleeting beauty.

5938.name: Hiatus Whistle
5938.desc: Used in dire emergencies, this whistle calls nearby Meebles to help its bearer escape and fight another day.

5939.name: Terminus Whistle
5939.desc: This whistle calls nearby Meebles to help the bearer's whole party escape after completing an expedition.

5940.name: Trail Cookie
5940.desc: The most popular souvenir from Adoulin, this cookie packs enough energy from the dried fruit inside to carry a weary trailblazer across the whole continent. "Aquan Killer"+12 "Resist Sleep"+10 MP recovered while healing +5

5941.name: Campfire Choco
5941.desc: The third most popular souvenir from Adoulin, this bar of chocolate saves starving pioneers' tongues by not being overly sweet. MND+1 MP recovered while healing +2

5942.name: Cascade Candy
5942.desc: The second most popular souvenir from Adoulin, the flavor of this candy is often likened to releasing a waterfall inside one's mouth. MND+4 CHR+4

5943.name: Smoked Mackerel
5943.desc: The faint aroma of a warm summer's day exudes from the lightly smoked flesh of this mackerel. VIT-3 AGI+4 Evasion+5

5944.name: Frontier Soda
5944.desc: This carbonated beverage is made with only the cleanest and freshest spring water in all of Adoulin.

5945.name: Prize Powder
5945.desc: This resplendent pulverization gives off the tantalizing scent of a newly opened treasure chest.

5946.name: Frayed Sack (D)
5946.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of tumbling dice.

5947.name: Frayed Sack (L)
5947.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of dream-like dust.

5948.name: Black Prawn
5948.desc: A crustacean that dances in the waters of the Senroh Sea, camouflaged by its murkily pigmented shell.

5949.name: Mussel
5949.desc: A type of bivalve, this shellfish can be found clinging desperately to boulders and reefs across the islands that dot Ulbuka's coastline.

5950.name: Mackerel
5950.desc: This saltwater fish makes its home in the Senroh Sea, which may just be the reason its smoked and sliced brethren taste so delicious.

5951.name: Bloodblotch
5951.desc: This gigantic fish that swims off the shores off Foret de Hennetiel intimidates not only with its size, but also the vast amounts of deadly poison it stores within its body.

5952.name: Ruddy Seema
5952.desc: A poisonous freshwater fish that populates the Zoldeff River in Foret de Hennetiel.

5953.name: Dragonfly Trout
5953.desc: A freshwater fish whose fins resemble the delicate wings of a dragonfly.

5954.name: Barnacle
5954.desc: An arthropod that once clung desperately to an underwater prominence in the Senroh Sea.

5955.name: Yawning Catfish
5955.desc: Roaming the waters deep within Ulbuka's continental rifts, this freshwater fish is surprisingly elusive for its massive size.

5957.name: Shockfish
5957.desc: A long, thin fish whose scales can emit light electric pulses.

5959.name: Dragonfish
5959.desc: Found mainly in estuaries, this fish is adorned with scales so refulgent that fishkeepers all over Adoulin covet its brethren.

5960.name: Ulbukan Lobster
5960.desc: A crustacean that thrives on the Ulbukan continent, possibly due to the strength of the poison it possesses, which can incapacitate creatures much larger than itself.

5961.name: Contortopus
5961.desc: A miniscule cephalopod that roams the Senroh Sea, protecting its life by squeezing into the tiniest of cracks.

5962.name: Contortacle
5962.desc: This tentacle was ripped mercilessly from a frightened contortopus.

5963.name: Senroh Sardine
5963.desc: One of the myriad fish that swim in the Senroh Sea near Ulbuka.

5964.name: Felicifruit
5964.desc: The savory and juicy pulp of this popular fruit alone might be to die for, but the fruit itself actually increases people's lifespan.

5965.name: Isleracea
5965.desc: Native to the Ulbukan continent, this vegetable's leaves have nourished many a traveler for days on end.

5966.name: Ulbuconut
5966.desc: This fruit is indigenous to the Ulbukan hinterland, and any who exert the tremendous effort required to bypass its rock-like exterior are rewarded with pulp from a succulent center.

5967.name: Verboshroom
5967.desc: Lore has it that those who eat this odd mushroom will inevitably fail to stay their tongues, no matter how hard they try.

5968.name: Seafood Paella
5968.desc: A paella composed of bounties from various seas. HP+40 DEX+5 MND-1 Accuracy+15% (Max. 80) "Undead Killer"+5

5969.name: Piscator's Paella
5969.desc: A paella so salty that Ulbukan fishermen cannot help but be reminded of their many trials and tribulations on the Senroh Sea. HP+45 DEX+6 Accuracy+16% (Max. 85) "Undead Killer"+6

5970.name: Mushroom Paella
5970.desc: A paella made with a plethora of mushrooms. HP+40 STR-1 MND+5 Magic Accuracy+5 "Undead Killer"+5

5971.name: Mush. Paella +1
5971.desc: One bite of this spectacular example of paella releases the earthy aroma of Ulbuka's jungle upon one's tongue. HP+45 MND+6 Magic Accuracy+6 "Undead Killer"+6

5972.name: Beef Paella
5972.desc: A paella made with heaping helpings of beef. HP+40 STR+5 INT-1 Attack+18% (Max. 90) "Undead Killer"+5

5973.name: Beef Paella +1
5973.desc: A paella that exhibits a special harmony between juicy beef and mild seafood. HP+45 STR+6 Attack+19% (Max. 95) "Undead Killer"+6

5974.name: Barnacle Paella
5974.desc: A paella expertly prepared, bringing forth every onz of flavor present in the myriad barnacles that serve as ingredients. HP+40 VIT+5 MND-1 CHR-1 DEF+25% (Max. 150) "Undead Killer"+5

5975.name: Flapano's Paella
5975.desc: Flapano's legendary barnacle paella. So many ingredients were used to create this delicacy that it is wholly understandable why his restaurant runs out of foodstuffs when he makes a plate of it. HP+45 VIT+6 DEF+26% (Max. 155) "Undead Killer"+6

5976.name: Ulbuconut Milk
5976.desc: This all-natural milk made of pure squeezed ulbuconuts is a favorite of all Adoulinians. VIT-2 CHR+3

5977.name: Ulbuconut Milk +1
5977.desc: This milk tastes even creamier than usual, despite the only difference being that the ulbuconut in which it is contained was cut cleaner. VIT-1 CHR+4

5978.name: Felicifruit Gelatin
5978.desc: A pleasurable dessert fit for any occasion, this felicifruit gelatin pairs exceedingly well with the compote that sits atop it. MP+5% (Max. 100) INT+7 MP recovered while healing +3

5979.name: Dulcet Panettones
5979.desc: A felicifruit fashioned into one of the most succulent desserts Adoulin has to offer, thanks to the expert hands of local patissiers. MP+6% (Max. 105) INT+8 MP recovered while healing +4

5980.name: Boiled Barnacles
5980.desc: Though heated thoroughly, these barnacles are nothing to write home about. CHR-3 DEF+25% (Max. 130)

5981.name: Bld. Barnacles +1
5981.desc: Steamed expertly in their own juices, these barnacles are certainly not typical fare from the sea. CHR-2 DEF+26% (Max. 135)

5982.name: Senroh Skewer
5982.desc: An Adoulinian tradition, this skewer is decked with some of the tastiest catches from the Senroh Sea. DEX+2 VIT+3 MND-1 DEF+25% (Max. 150)

5983.name: Piscator's Skewer
5983.desc: A skewer with ingredients from the Senroh Sea grilled with the prowess of a fisherman who knows his prey inside and out. DEX+3 VIT+4 DEF+26% (Max. 155)

5984.name: Gnatbane
5984.desc: Putting this plant anywhere near one's mouth is apt to cause certain death. Even grotesque gnats avoid it at all costs.

5985.name: Hemlock
5985.desc: Ingesting this plant induces paralysis, no matter how brawny one may be.

5986.name: Coalition Potion
5986.desc: A potion developed by the Peacekeepers' Coalition to aid pioneers in their quest to colonize Ulbuka.

5987.name: Coalition Ether
5987.desc: An ether developed by the Peacekeepers' Coalition to aid pioneers in their quest to colonize Ulbuka.

5988.name: Instant Protect
5988.desc: The white magic spell Protect is inscribed on this parchment.

5989.name: Instant Shell
5989.desc: The white magic spell Shell is inscribed on this parchment.

5990.name: Instant Stoneskin
5990.desc: The white magic spell Stoneskin is inscribed on this parchment.

5991.name: Farewell Fly
5991.desc: This butterfly may be colored black as midnight, but it aids pioneers in finding the light of Vana'diel by extracting them from otherworldly skirmishes.

5992.name: Fenestral Key
5992.desc: Integral in opening the treasure coffers after otherworldly skirmishes, this key is shaped like a skull.

5993.name: Senroh Frog
5993.desc: An amphibian that hops about the banks of the Ulbukan shore.

5995.name: Malicious Perch
5995.desc: True to its name, this estuary-dwelling species of fish has both an insatiable appetite and an undying supply of poison.

5998.name: Adoulinian Soup
5998.desc: This pedestrian style of seaweed soup is enjoyed by families throughout the Sacred City of Adoulin. HP+3% (Max. 40) VIT+3 DEF+15% (Max. 70) HP recovered while healing +6

5999.name: Adoulinian Soup +1
5999.desc: This soup is as nostalgic as it is delicious, and many Adoulinians have returned halfway through their journeys just to taste this soup once more. HP+4% (Max. 45) VIT+4 DEF+16% (Max. 75) HP recovered while healing +7

6001.name: Clotflagration
6001.desc: A diminutive clot that, through generations of evolution, has adapted to living in extremely heated environs.

6002.name: Trbl. Pickaxe
6002.desc: A pickaxe modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the excavation of resources from only the harshest of Eastern Ulbuka's mines.

6003.name: Trbl. Pickaxe +1
6003.desc: A pickaxe modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the excavation of resources from only the harshest of Eastern Ulbuka's mines.

6004.name: Trbl. Hatchet
6004.desc: A hatchet modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the felling of trees in only the thickest of Eastern Ulbuka's forests.

6005.name: Trbl. Hatchet +1
6005.desc: A hatchet modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the felling of trees in only the thickest of Eastern Ulbuka's forests.

6006.name: Trbl. Sickle
6006.desc: A sickle modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the harvesting of resources amongst only the wildest brush in Eastern Ulbuka.

6007.name: Trbl. Sickle +1
6007.desc: A sickle modified by the Pioneers' Coalition to aid in the harvesting of resources amongst only the wildest brush in Eastern Ulbuka.

6008.name: Copse Candy
6008.desc: The flavor of this candy is often likened to pastorally strolling through a dew-covered copse.

6009.name: Mog Pudding
6009.desc: Made by the meticulous mitts of moogles, this palate-pleasing pudding contains milk from backwoods-bred bovines and eggs from the choicest of chickens. HP+7 MP+7 VIT+3

6011.name: C. Ygg. Shard I
6011.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a greenish hue.

6012.name: C. Ygg. Shard II
6012.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a greenish hue.

6013.name: C. Ygg. Shard III
6013.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a greenish hue.

6014.name: C. Ygg. Shard IV
6014.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a greenish hue.

6015.name: C. Ygg. Shard V
6015.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a greenish hue.

6016.name: Z. Ygg. Shard I
6016.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a bluish hue.

6017.name: Z. Ygg. Shard II
6017.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a bluish hue.

6018.name: Z. Ygg. Shard III
6018.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a bluish hue.

6019.name: Z. Ygg. Shard IV
6019.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a bluish hue.

6020.name: Z. Ygg. Shard V
6020.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a bluish hue.

6021.name: A. Ygg. Shard I
6021.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a reddish hue.

6022.name: A. Ygg. Shard II
6022.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a reddish hue.

6023.name: A. Ygg. Shard III
6023.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a reddish hue.

6024.name: A. Ygg. Shard IV
6024.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a reddish hue.

6025.name: A. Ygg. Shard V
6025.desc: A fragmented piece of yggrete, tinted a reddish hue.

6026.name: Celadon Yggzi I
6026.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally green.

6027.name: Celadon Yggzi II
6027.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally green.

6028.name: Celadon Yggzi III
6028.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally green.

6029.name: Celadon Yggzi IV
6029.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally green.

6030.name: Celadon Yggzi V
6030.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally green.

6031.name: Zaffre Yggzi I
6031.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally blue.

6032.name: Zaffre Yggzi II
6032.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally blue.

6033.name: Zaffre Yggzi III
6033.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally blue.

6034.name: Zaffre Yggzi IV
6034.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally blue.

6035.name: Zaffre Yggzi V
6035.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally blue.

6036.name: Alizarin Yggzi I
6036.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally red.

6037.name: Alizarin Yggzi II
6037.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally red.

6038.name: Alizarin Yggzi III
6038.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally red.

6039.name: Alizarin Yggzi IV
6039.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally red.

6040.name: Alizarin Yggzi V
6040.desc: A crystallized mass of yggrete that shines preternaturally red.

6041.name: Pyrohelix
6041.desc: SCH Lv.24 Teaches the black magic Pyrohelix. Deals fire damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6042.name: Hydrohelix
6042.desc: SCH Lv.20 Teaches the black magic Hydrohelix. Deals water damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6043.name: Ionohelix
6043.desc: SCH Lv.28 Teaches the black magic Ionohelix. Deals lightning damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6044.name: Cryohelix
6044.desc: SCH Lv.26 Teaches the black magic Cryohelix. Deals ice damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6045.name: Geohelix
6045.desc: SCH Lv.18 Teaches the black magic Geohelix. Deals earth damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6046.name: Anemohelix
6046.desc: SCH Lv.22 Teaches the black magic Anemohelix. Deals wind damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6047.name: Luminohelix
6047.desc: SCH Lv.32 Teaches the black magic Luminohelix. Deals light damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6048.name: Noctohelix
6048.desc: SCH Lv.30 Teaches the black magic Noctohelix. Deals darkness damage that gradually reduces a target's HP. Damage dealt is greatly affected by the weather.

6049.name: Firestorm
6049.desc: SCH Lv.44 Teaches the white magic Firestorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "hot."

6050.name: Rainstorm
6050.desc: SCH Lv.42 Teaches the white magic Rainstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "rainy."

6051.name: Thunderstorm
6051.desc: SCH Lv.46 Teaches the white magic Thunderstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "thundery."

6052.name: Hailstorm
6052.desc: SCH Lv.45 Teaches the white magic Hailstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "snowy."

6053.name: Sandstorm
6053.desc: SCH Lv.41 Teaches the white magic Sandstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "dusty."

6054.name: Windstorm
6054.desc: SCH Lv.43 Teaches the white magic Windstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "windy."

6055.name: Aurorastorm
6055.desc: SCH Lv.48 Teaches the white magic Aurorastorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "auroras."

6056.name: Voidstorm
6056.desc: SCH Lv.47 Teaches the white magic Voidstorm. Changes the weather around target party member to "gloomy."

6058.name: Klimaform
6058.desc: SCH Lv.46 Teaches the black magic Klimaform. Increases the magic accuracy for spells of the same element as the current weather.

6059.name: Animus Augeo
6059.desc: SCH Lv.85 Teaches the white magic Animus Augeo. Facilitates enmity gain for target party member.

6060.name: Animus Minuo
6060.desc: SCH Lv.85 Teaches the white magic Animus Minuo. Suppresses enmity gain for target party member.

6061.name: Adloquium
6061.desc: SCH Lv.88 Teaches the white magic Adloquium. Gradually increases target's TP.

6063.name: Fruit Parfait
6063.desc: A tasty treat made of iced cream and topped with an abundance of delectable fruits piled as high as its popularity. MP+5% (Max. 50) STR-3 INT+3 MND+2 CHR+1 Magic Accuracy+3 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+6

6064.name: Queen's Crown
6064.desc: An exemplar of icy elegance, this parfait is modeled after the crown that once adorned the head of Marelinne R. d'Oraguille, San d'Oria's former Sentinel Queen. MP+6% (Max. 55) STR-2 INT+4 MND+3 CHR+2 Magic Accuracy+4 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+7

6065.name: Tiny Macaron
6065.desc: Developed by the Inventors' Coalition, this petite macaron has a smooth, buttery flavor that puts craftsmen at ease while taking a load off. Synthesis success rate +5% Increases synthesis skill gain rate by 5%

6066.name: Tiny Rusk
6066.desc: Developed by the Inventors' Coalition, this petite rusk has a crunchy texture that is the perfect pick-me-up for craftsmen. Rate of synthesis material loss -4% High-quality success rate +2

6067.name: Ontic Extremity
6067.desc: Cleaved from the hand of a thaumaturge, this dexterous digit points the meek toward their home world.

6068.name: Gabbrath Meat
6068.desc: A thick slice of grilled gabbrath meat.

6069.name: Riverfin Soup
6069.desc: A sufficiently tasty soup in which the dorsal fin of a rockfin is boiled under high temperature for extended periods of time. Accuracy+14% (Max. 90) Attack+18% (Max. 80) Ranged Accuracy+14% (Max. 90) Ranged Attack+18% (Max. 80) "Amorph Killer"+5

6070.name: Oceanfin Soup
6070.desc: An exquisite example of boiled rockfin, the broth perfectly accentuates the marine flavors inherent in the beast's dorsal appendage. Accuracy+15% (Max. 95) Attack+19% (Max. 85) Ranged Accuracy+15% (Max. 95) Ranged Attack+19% (Max. 85) "Amorph Killer"+6

6071.name: Magma Steak
6071.desc: A slice of meat so hot it practically bubbles and boils like lava. STR+8 Attack+23% (Max. 180) Ranged Attack+23% (Max. 180) "Vermin Killer"+5

6072.name: Magma Steak +1
6072.desc: This thick steak of gabbrath meat erupts inside one's stomach, leaving an incredible warmth behind even after its last remains are digested. STR+9 Attack+24% (Max. 185) Ranged Attack+24% (Max. 185) "Vermin Killer"+6

6073.name: Indi-Regen
6073.desc: GEO Lv.15 Teaches the geomancy Indi-Regen. Gradually restores HP for party members near the caster.

6074.name: Indi-Poison
6074.desc: GEO Lv.1 Teaches the geomancy Indi-Poison. Poisons enemies near the caster and gradually reduces their HP.

6075.name: Indi-Refresh
6075.desc: GEO Lv.30 Teaches the geomancy Indi-Refresh. Gradually restores MP for party members near the caster.

6076.name: Indi-STR
6076.desc: GEO Lv.48 Teaches the geomancy Indi-STR. Enhances Strength for party members near the caster.

6077.name: Indi-DEX
6077.desc: GEO Lv.45 Teaches the geomancy Indi-DEX. Enhances Dexterity for party members near the caster.

6078.name: Indi-VIT
6078.desc: GEO Lv.42 Teaches the geomancy Indi-VIT. Enhances Vitality for party members near the caster.

6079.name: Indi-AGI
6079.desc: GEO Lv.39 Teaches the geomancy Indi-AGI. Enhances Agility for party members near the caster.

6080.name: Indi-INT
6080.desc: GEO Lv.36 Teaches the geomancy Indi-INT. Enhances Intelligence for party members near the caster.

6081.name: Indi-MND
6081.desc: GEO Lv.33 Teaches the geomancy Indi-MND. Enhances Mind for party members near the caster.

6082.name: Indi-CHR
6082.desc: GEO Lv.30 Teaches the geomancy Indi-CHR. Enhances Charisma for party members near the caster.

6083.name: Indi-Fury
6083.desc: GEO Lv.34 Teaches the geomancy Indi-Fury. Enhances attack for party members near the caster.

6084.name: Indi-Barrier

6137.name: Chapuli Quiver
6137.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of chapuli arrows.

6138.name: Mantid Quiver
6138.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of mantid arrows.

6139.name: Mid. Bolt Quiver
6139.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of midrium bolts.

6140.name: Dm. Bolt Quiver
6140.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of damascus bolts.

6141.name: O. Bolt Quiver
6141.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of oxidant bolts.

6142.name: Mid. Bul. Pouch
6142.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of midrium bullets.

6143.name: Dm. Bul. Pouch
6143.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of damascus bullets.

6144.name: Frigorifish
6144.desc: The colder the water, the more this fish thrives, for it can only be found in rivers and lakes in Ulbuka that have been frozen over.

6145.name: Dwarf Remora
6145.desc: A new hatchling from the remora, a species of fish that has managed to survive for countless eons.

6146.name: Remora
6146.desc: A legendary fish that has bloodied the waters in which it swims since times of yore.

6147.name: Mikhe's Memo
6147.desc: A memo scrawled by Mikhe Aryohcha that matter-of-factly states, "Just throw your fist at your opponent and it'll all work out." Adventurers say that their hand-to-hand skill increases after reading this note.

6148.name: Dgr. Enchiridion
6148.desc: A guide to the finer points of wielding a dagger, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's dagger skill.

6149.name: Swing and Stab
6149.desc: Ulla wrote this guide on sword wielding--from how to grip the hilt to tips on footwork--so others could follow in her footsteps. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's sword skill.

6150.name: Mieuseloir's Diary
6150.desc: An account penned by Mieuseloir B Enchelles of his encounters with the Gigas in Beaucedine Glacier. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great sword skill.

6151.name: Bull's Diary
6151.desc: An account penned by Striking Bull during the Second Battle of Konschtat. It details the Republic's victory over King Raigegue the Lupine's San d'Orian army. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's axe skill.

6152.name: Death for Dim.
6152.desc: A guide to the finer points of hacking everything in one's way to pieces with a two-handed axe, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great axe skill.

6153.name: Ludwig's Report
6153.desc: A report made by Ludwig Eichberg regarding the Battle of Grauberg. It details the constant changes in the position of the front line and the withdrawal of Republic troops. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's scythe skill.

6154.name: Clash of Titans
6154.desc: An article detailing the duel between  Yrvaulair S Cousseraux and Alphonimile M Aurchiat that became all the rage in San d'Oria after appearing in a popular newspaper. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's polearm skill.

6155.name: Kagetora's Diary
6155.desc: A diary written by Kagetora. In it he details each and every one of his ninety-eight victories and ninety-nine losses against Yomi. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's katana skill.

6156.name: Noillurie's Log
6156.desc: A record Noillurie kept of her personal experiences. The one that stands out the most is the battle with the colossal beast she undertook to prove herself to her order. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's great katana skill.

6157.name: Ferreous's Diary
6157.desc: A diary written by Ferreous Coffin that describes his encounters with Orcs in the north. So many were there that his war hammer became coated with a thick layer of blood after all was said and done. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's club skill.

6158.name: K-P's Memoirs
6158.desc: Memoirs penned by Kayeel-Payeel. They describe in particular detail the time he received Claustrum from the Warlock Warlord Robel-Akbel. Adventurers note that reading them increases one's staff skill.

6159.name: Perih's Primer
6159.desc: A guidebook Perih Vashai jotted down for the edification of new recruits. It discusses everything from the various ways of holding a bow to methods of judging distance. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's archery skill.

6160.name: Barrels of Fun
6160.desc: An educational text authored by Elivira Gogol. It discusses how to dismantle, clean, and reconstruct firearms in careful detail. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's marksmanship skill.

6161.name: T.W. Enchiridion
6161.desc: A guide to the finer points of hurling projectile objects at opponents, written by an anonymous individual. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's throwing skill.

6162.name: Mikhe's Note
6162.desc: A memo scrawled by Mikhe Aryohcha that matter-of-factly states, "Just throw your guard up and it'll all work out." Adventurers note that reading it increases one's guarding skill.

6163.name: Sonia's Diary
6163.desc: A compilation of memories from Sonia. Of particular note are her observations on how to replicate the sheer brilliance of Annika Brilioth and the esoteric steps known as the Kriegstanz. Adventurers say that reading it increases their evasion skill.

6164.name: The Successor
6164.desc: An essay authored by Cerane I Virgaut, mainly concerning the night Perseus bequeathed unto her an exemplary shield. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's shield skill.

6165.name: Kage. Journal
6165.desc: A journal kept by Kagetora that delineates the extent to which he and Yomi lost themselves in their studies of the martial arts. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's parrying skill.

6166.name: Altana's Hymn
6166.desc: A copy of Febrenard C Brunnaut's favorite read. The simple lyrics and enchanting meter were said to have soothed his soul. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's divine magic skill.

6167.name: Coveffe Musings
6167.desc: A collection of thoughts scribbled out by Ferreous Coffin on his visit to Coveffe Barrows. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's healing magic skill.

6168.name: Aid for All
6168.desc: A guide to the finer points of heightening the potential of one's compatriots, written by Rainemard. It also includes some tips on how to swing special swords. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's enhancing magic skill.

6169.name: Inv. Report
6169.desc: A collection of observations made by Rainemard on a particular exploration. It goes into such detail on so much minutiae that many admit to never finishing it. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's enfeebling magic skill.

6170.name: Bounty List
6170.desc: A simple list of known criminals who are better off dead. Every single name is crossed out in Azima's handwriting. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's elemental magic skill.

6171.name: Dark Deeds
6171.desc: A guide to the finer points of insidious dark magic, as compiled by Azima. Proceeds from this tome have gone to fund her various purchases dealing with alchemical research. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's dark magic skill.

6172.name: Breezy Libretto
6172.desc: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize a fragrant, early morning summer breeze. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's singing skill.

6173.name: Cavernous Score
6173.desc: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize the damp and musty air that stagnates within an underground cave. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's string instrument skill.

6174.name: Beaming Score
6174.desc: A musical score composed by Lewenhart. Its notes symbolize the gently glowing beams of light that filter through the leaves of a deciduous tree in the late afternoon. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's wind instrument skill.

6175.name: Yomi's Diagram
6175.desc: A meticulously drawn diagram Yomi made for Kagetora explaining how to construct certain ninja tools. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's ninjutsu skill.

6176.name: Astral Homeland
6176.desc: A specious work written by an unknown individual that both reads and feels like an illusion from the ancient past. It discusses the world avatars inhabit, but seems too absurd to be true. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's summoning magic skill.

6177.name: Life-form Study
6177.desc: A report written and filed by an anonymous individual. It covers the behavioral patterns and diets of almost every known family of beasts in existence. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's blue magic skill.

6178.name: Hrohj's Record
6178.desc: A record of what happened on the fateful day the lifestream overflowed, as kept by Hrohj Wagrehsa. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's geomancy skill.

6179.name: The Bell Tolls
6179.desc: An essay penned by Hrohj Wagrehsa concerning the transmission of Sih Renaye's handbell and the voice of the land. Adventurers note that reading it increases one's handbell skill.

6180.name: Pluton Case
6180.desc: A small wooden box that contains a few plutons.

6181.name: Beitetsu Parcel
6181.desc: A tiny parcel that contains a few chunks of beitetsu.

6182.name: Boulder Case
6182.desc: A small bronze box that contains a few riftborn boulders.

6183.name: Pluton Box
6183.desc: A large wooden box that contains a plethora of plutons.

6184.name: Beitetsu Box
6184.desc: A large wooden box that contains a plethora of beitetsu.

6185.name: Boulder Box
6185.desc: A large bronze box that contains a plethora of riftborn boulders.

6186.name: Slime Rocket
6186.desc: A firework created with a certain otherworldly entity in mind.

6187.name: Slime. Candy
6187.desc: A hard candy that features the image of a globular entity from another world.

6188.name: She-Slime Candy
6188.desc: A hard candy that features the image of a globular entity from another world.

6189.name: M. Slime Candy
6189.desc: A hard candy that features the image of a globular entity from another world.

6190.name: Spriggan Spark
6190.desc: A firework created with a certain otherworldly entity in mind.

6191.name: G. Sprigg. Lolli
6191.desc: A lollipop molded in the image of a hairy faerie from another world.

6192.name: R. Sprigg. Lolli

6193.name: P. Sprigg. Lolli
6193.desc: A lollipop molded in the image of a hairy faerie from another world.

6194.name: E. Tincture (LF)
6194.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Loch of Flux in Kamihr Drifts. Temporarily enhances the effect of Subtle Blow.

6195.name: E. Tincture (LP)
6195.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Lambent Pillar in Kamihr Drifts. Temporarily enhances the effect of Conserve MP.

6196.name: E. Tincture (HI)
6196.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Prominence of the Rime in Woh Gates. Temporarily enhances the effect of Store TP.

6197.name: E. Tincture (PRi)
6197.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Halcyon Icefall in Woh Gates. Temporarily grants a magic defense bonus.

6198.name: E. Tincture (CC)
6198.desc: A magical elixir containing ergon might extracted from the Crystalline Claw in Kamihr Drifts. Temporarily enhances the effect of Snapshot.

6211.name: Marinara Slice
6211.desc: A slice of classic pizza employing a zesty marinara sauce, and topped with delectable chunks of fish and seafood. HP+20 Accuracy+10% (Max. 54) Attack+20% (Max. 50) "Undead Killer"+5

6212.name: Marinara Slice +1
6212.desc: Full stomachs ahead, Captain! You've cleaved off a heaping portion of the ultimate in seafood pizzas, simply loaded with succulent morsels of only the finest ocean delights. HP+25 Accuracy+11% (Max. 58) Attack+21% (Max. 55) "Undead Killer"+5

6213.name: Margherita Slice
6213.desc: A single slice of the pizza that derives its popularity from the tried-and-tested combinatorial deliciousness of cheese, basil, and tomato. HP+30 Accuracy+10% (Max. 8) Attack+10% (Max. 10)

6214.name: Marg. Slice +1
6214.desc: The perfect harmony of basil and tomato adorns this slice of margherita among margheritas. HP+35 Accuracy+10% (Max. 9) Attack+10% (Max. 11)

6215.name: Pepperoni Slice
6215.desc: A veritable sheet of pepperoni on this slice delicately floats upon the layers of cheese and sauce below. A mainstay of any pizza repertoire. HP+30 STR+1 Accuracy+9% (Max. 10) Attack+10% (Max. 15)

6216.name: Pepp. Slice +1
6216.desc: The width of this pepperoni slice, the consistency of its crust, the proportion of cheese to sauce. It's all here. HP+35 STR+2 Accuracy+9% (Max. 11) Attack+10% (Max. 16)

6217.name: Anchovy Slice
6217.desc: A slice of an often-slighted pizza varietal that makes up for its lack of adherents with lots of salt. HP+30 DEX+1 Accuracy+9% (Max. 15) Attack+10% (Max. 20)

6218.name: Anch. Slice +1
6218.desc: Only the choicest of anchovies are given the privilege of topping this transcendental pizza slice. HP+35 DEX+2 Accuracy+9% (Max. 16) Attack+10% (Max. 21)

6219.name: Warthog Stewpot
6219.desc: Centered around the unique taste of warthog meat, this Far Eastern delicacy is accented with a hefty helping of vegetables and other bounties of the forest. HP+10% (Max. 50) Attack+10% (Max. 20) Ranged Attack+19% (Max. 40) HP recovered while healing +5

6220.name: Prm. Wt. Stewpot
6220.desc: This rendition of one of the most iconic pieces of Vana'dielian cooking could barely get any better. HP+10% (Max. 75) STR+3 MND+1 Attack+10% (Max. 30) Ranged Attack+19% (Max. 60) HP recovered while healing +7

6221.name: Prz. Wt. Stewpot
6221.desc: This extraordinary version of the classic dish makes use of rare ingredients that take years to collect. HP+10% (Max. 100) STR+5 MND+2 Attack+10% (Max. 40) Ranged Attack+19% (Max. 80) HP recovered while healing +9

6222.name: Warthog Meat
6222.desc: The polarizingly gamey flavor of this raaz meat is further emphasized by its heinous smell, which resembles that of wet leaves and yztarg dung.

6223.name: C. Snow Cone
6223.desc: A rare and delectable frozen treat, this colossal cluster of shaved ice was originally whittled from ice shards shattered from a fallen cehuetzi's back. MP+20% (Max. 100) INT+5 MND+5 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+13 "Lizard Killer"+5

6224.name: A. Snow Cone
6224.desc: An exceedingly tasty rendition of the iconic ice shard treat, eating it is often compared to a romp in freshly fallen snow. MP+25% (Max. 105) INT+6 MND+6 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+14 "Lizard Killer"+6

6225.name: C. Coalescence
6225.desc: A masterfully delicious rendition of the iconic ice shard treat, it pours lavish and exotic flavors over the tongue while it freezes not the brain. MP+30% (Max. 110) INT+7 MND+7 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+15 "Lizard Killer"+7

6226.name: Pixioche
6226.desc: Miniscule bread for miniscule beings. INT+1

6257.name: Jungle Nectar
6257.desc: This herbal beverage is made of juices squeezed from the endless varieties of flora that cover Ulbuka. Contrary to its name, many state that it has a mouth-puckeringly, tongue-shrivelingly bitter flavor. MP+10 VIT-2 MND+3 Magic Accuracy+1 "Magic Atk. Bonus"+1

6258.name: Shiromochi
6258.desc: A delectable treat from the Far East, this simple confection consists of steamed rice rolled into a ball. HP+20 VIT+3 Accuracy+14% (Max. 70) Ranged Accuracy+14% (Max. 70) Pet: HP+20 VIT+3 Accuracy+14% (Max. 108) Ranged Accuracy+14% (Max. 108)

6259.name: Shiromochi +1
6259.desc: Rolled and steamed to a perfect consistency, this exceptionally delicious shiromochi could almost be considered art. HP+30 VIT+4 Accuracy+15% (Max. 74) Ranged Accuracy+15% (Max. 74) Pet: HP+30 VIT+4 Accuracy+15% (Max. 114) Ranged Accuracy+15% (Max. 114)

6260.name: Akamochi
6260.desc: A shiromochi topped with a layer of red bean paste. HP+20 VIT+3 Accuracy+10% (Max. 50) Ranged Accuracy+10% (Max. 50) Attack+16% (Max. 50) Ranged Attack+16% (Max. 50) Pet: HP+20 VIT+3 Accuracy+10% (Max. 75) Ranged Accuracy+10% (Max. 75) Attack+16% (Max. 75) Ranged Attack+16% (Max. 75)

6261.name: Akamochi +1
6261.desc: A shiromochi with a layer of red bean paste. HP+30 VIT+4 Accuracy+11% (Max. 54) Attack+17% (Max. 54) Ranged Accuracy+11% (Max. 54) Ranged Attack+17% (Max. 54) Pet: HP+30 VIT+4 Accuracy+11% (Max. 81) Attack+17% (Max. 82) Ranged Accuracy+11% (Max. 81) Ranged Attack+17% (Max. 82)

6262.name: Kusamochi
6262.desc: Kneaded with medicinal herbs, this verdant variation of mochi possesses a strong--but not overpowering--aroma reminiscent of spring. HP+20 VIT+3 Attack+20% (Max. 72) Ranged Attack+20% (Max. 72) Pet: HP+20 VIT+3 Attack+20% (Max. 113) Ranged Attack+20% (Max. 113)

6263.name: Kusamochi +1
6263.desc: A kusamochi with an intense flavor. HP+30 VIT+4 Attack+21% (Max. 77) Ranged Attack+21% (Max. 77) Pet: HP+30 VIT+4 Attack+21% (Max. 120) Ranged Attack+21% (Max. 120)

6264.name: Frayed Sack (H1)
6264.desc: A hempen sack, worn and frayed by time. It bears an image of a refined stone that can only be described as screams of the dead made manifest.

6265.name: Toolbag (Ranka)
6265.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of the ninja tool ranka.

6266.name: Toolbag (Furu)
6266.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of the ninja tool furusumi.

6267.name: Aged Box (Bayld)
6267.desc: Though its exterior may be rusted and scuffed with age, this box still properly holds the almost overflowing amount of bayld that has been poured into it over the years.

6268.name: Komanezumi
6268.desc: A special type of firework from the Far East that gracefully spins 'round and 'round while unleashing sparks into the air.

6269.name: Eminent Quiver
6269.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of eminent arrows.

6270.name: Em. Bolt Quiver
6270.desc: This leather quiver is used to carry large quantities of eminent bolts.

6271.name: Em. Bul. Pouch
6271.desc: This leather pouch is used to carry large quantities of eminent bullets.

6272.name: Fried Popoto
6272.desc: This soft treat made of freshly mashed popotoes warms both the body and the soul. HP+30 VIT+2 +20 DEF+20% (Max. 145) "Subtle Blow"+8

6273.name: Fried Popoto +1

6670.name: ??? Ear.: PUP +2

6679.name: ??? Ear.: GEO +2

6680.desc: An earring made for a rune fencer. Use it to reveal its capabilities.

6681.desc: An earring made for a sturdy rune fencer. Use it to reveal its capabilities.

6682.name: ??? Ear.: RUN +2
6682.desc: An earring made for an indomitable rune fencer. Use it to reveal its capabilities.

6683.name: Porcelain Mine
6683.desc: A celebratory firework developed by Jeuno's finest technicians in celebration of the 20th Vana'versary.

6684.name: Popper II
6684.desc: A special firework developed by young alchemists. Best used to celebrate the power of friendship.

6685.name: Obsid. Wing
6685.desc: A pitch-black wing. Tossing it high into the air will return you to your original location.

6686.name: Gyudon
6686.desc: A piping-hot bowl of boiled and seasoned meat, wild onions, and more resting on a bed of rice. Enmity-5 "Double Attack"+5% Weapon skill damage +5%

6687.name: Gyudon +1
6687.desc: A gyudon whose aroma and flavor are beyond compare.  Enmity-6 "Double Attack"+6% Weapon skill damage +6%

6688.name: Cha. Knapsack
6688.desc: A special grab bag containing a plethora of gifts specially selected by Chacharoon.

6689.name: Large Wh. Tail
6689.desc: A fluffy white tail of improbably large size.

6690.name: Starlight Tunes
6690.desc: Sheet music that can be played on an orchestrion.  Music List: Jeuno -Starlight Celebration-

6691.name: Mapitoto Tunes
6691.desc: Sheet music that can be played on an orchestrion.  Music List: Full Speed Ahead!

6692.name: Exper. Fluid
6692.desc: A vial of fluid taken from an experimental laboratory. It does appear to be consumable, for whatever that's worth...

6693.name: Impish Box
6693.desc: A square box left behind by an imp. It appears that anyone should be able to use it.

6694.name: Choco. Chair II
6694.desc: A kit used to construct a chocobo chair II.

6695.name: Aden. Bench
6695.desc: A kit used to construct an adenium bench.

6696.name: Near East Tunes
6696.desc: Sheet music that can be played on an orchestrion.  Music List: Bustle of the Capital/Jeweled Boughs Bandits' Market/Illusions in the Mist

6697.name: Instant Reraise III
6697.desc: The white magic spell Reraise III is inscribed on this parchment.

6698.name: Whitegate Glyph
6698.desc: A sheet of paper inscribed with a high-level transportation spell.  Destination: Aht Urhgan Whitegate.

6699.name: Evanessence
6699.desc: A triply effective tonic that renders its imbiber imperceptible to the eye, ear, and nose.

6700.name: Moogle Memo.
6700.desc: This memorandrum contains a variety of information on Odyssey straight from the mouth of the pilgrim moogle.

6701.name: Lamia Powder
6701.desc: A mysterious powder capable of transforming the applying individual into a lamia.

6702.name: Peiste Conc.
6702.desc: A fascinating concoction capable of transforming the imbibing individual into a peiste.

6703.name: Lungo's Thesis
6703.desc: A thesis on magical anomalies penned by Lungo-Nango. And if anyone knew about anomalies, it was Lungo-Nango.

6704.name: Divine Tunes
6704.desc: Sheet music that can be played on an orchestrion.  Music List: Fighters of the Crystal

6705.name: Magicked Flyer
6705.desc: An advertisement for a magic store written on sheep wool. It gleams with latent energies.

6706.name: Pom-pom Fruit
6706.desc: A fruit that bears an uncanny resemblance to a moogle's pom-pom.

6707.name: Fishing Tunes
6707.desc: Sheet music that can be played on an orchestrion.  Music List: Selbina Mhaura

6709.name: He. C. Gr. Coffer
6709.desc: A large wooden coffer containing heroism crystals.

6710.name: He. A. Gr. Coffer
6710.desc: A large bronze coffer containing heroism aggregate.

6711.name: S. Ast. Det. Gr. Co.
6711.desc: A large wooden coffer containing swart astral detritus.

6712.name: M. Ast. Det. Gr. Co.
6712.desc: A large bronze coffer containing murky astral detritus.

6714.name: Ulthalam's Chr.
6714.desc: An ancient tome detailing the struggles of the first Empress, Ulthalam, leading up to the founding of the Majab Dynasty.

6715.name: Book of Deeds
6715.desc: A book issued by the A.M.A.N. Reading it grants 10 deeds.

6716.name: Trust Primer
6716.desc: A primer on the basics of Trust magic.

6717.name: Trust Tome
6717.desc: A tome detailing various information on Trust magic.

6718.name: Marjory's Thesis
6718.desc: A thesis on Trust magic penned by Marjory.

6719.name: Regine's Fifth Eye
6719.desc: A special treat prepared by Regine, who claims it will help you become "someone special."

6721.name: L. Sack of Beads
6721.desc: A sack containing a large amount of escha beads.

8451.name: Inhibitor
8451.desc: Store TP Improves TP usage efficiency

8455.name: Flame Holder
8455.desc: Converts the energy of a Fire Maneuver into attack power during weapon skills However, this adds burden to Fire Maneuvers

8456.name: Heat Capacitor
8456.desc: Erases all Fire Maneuver effects to restore TP However, this adds burden to Fire Maneuvers

8459.name: Inhibitor II
8459.desc: Store TP Improves TP usage efficiency

8461.name: Heat Capacitor II
8461.desc: Erases all Fire Maneuver effects to restore TP However, this adds burden to Fire Maneuvers

8462.name: Speedloader
8462.desc: Focuses on taking advantage of skillchains and grants a skillchain bonus.

8463.name: Speedloader II
8463.desc: Focuses on taking advantage of skillchains and grants a skillchain bonus.

8487.name: Ice Maker
8487.desc: Converts the energy of an Ice Maneuver into magic attack power when casting magic spells However, this adds burden to Ice Maneuvers

8491.name: Amplifier
8491.desc: Increass magic burst damage Attempts to use magic bursts more

8494.name: Amplifier II
8494.desc: Increass magic burst damage Attempts to use magic bursts more

8515.name: Pattern Reader
8515.desc: Analyzes enemy attack patterns and gradually enhances evasion

8519.name: Replicator
8519.desc: When HP is low, converts all Wind Maneuvers into shadow images However, this adds burden to Wind Maneuvers

8520.name: Barrage Turbine
8520.desc: Erases all Wind Maneuver effects to occasionally grant a Barrage effect However, this adds burden to Wind Maneuvers.

8547.name: Analyzer
8547.desc: Increases chance of mitigating the effects of special attacks previously absorbed

8549.name: Equalizer
8549.desc: Reduces damage received according to damage taken

8550.name: Schurzen
8550.desc: May erase an Earth Maneuver effect to reduce severe physical damage

8551.name: Hammermill
8551.desc: Increases the damage of Shield Bash May also induce "Slow" effect

8552.name: Barrier Module II
8552.desc: Increases chance of blocking with shield Earth Maneuver: Adds Shield Mastery and shortens Shield Bash recast time

8555.name: Barrier Module
8555.desc: Increases chance of blocking with shield Earth Maneuver: Adds Shield Mastery and shortens Shield Bash recast time

8579.name: Heat Seeker
8579.desc: Analyzes enemy evasion patterns and gradually enhances accuracy

8583.name: Coiler
8583.desc: Occasionally grants the automaton bonus melee attacks

8586.name: Coiler II
8586.desc: Occasionally grants the automaton bonus melee attacks

8613.name: Mana Channeler
8613.desc: Increases magic recast time in exchange for increased magic attack

8614.name: Condenser
8614.desc: May erase a Water Maneuver effect to prevent an overload

8615.name: Steam Jacket
8615.desc: Reduces damage taken from successive attacks of the same type

8622.name: Mana Channeler II
8622.desc: Increases magic recast time in exchange for increased magic attack

8641.name: Auto-Repair Kit
8641.desc: Increases HP Adds "Regen" effect to Light Maneuver

8644.name: Auto-Repair Kit II
8644.desc: Increases HP Adds "Regen" effect to Light Maneuver

8645.name: Eraser
8645.desc: Erases enfeebling and Light Maneuver effects However, this adds burden to Light Maneuvers.

8646.name: Optic Fiber
8646.desc: Increases the performance of other attachments

8650.name: Auto-Repair Kit III
8650.desc: Increases HP Adds "Regen" effect to Light Maneuver

8653.name: Auto-Repair Kit IV
8653.desc: Increases HP Adds "Regen" effect to Light Maneuver

8654.name: Optic Fiber II
8654.desc: Increases the performance of other attachments

8673.name: Mana Tank
8673.desc: Increases MP Adds "Refresh" effect to Dark Maneuver

8674.name: Mana Converter
8674.desc: Consumes HP to gradually restore MP

8676.name: Mana Tank II
8676.desc: Increases MP Adds "Refresh" effect to Dark Maneuver

8678.name: Economizer
8678.desc: When MP is low, may erase all Dark Maneuver effects to restore MP However, this adds burden to Dark Maneuvers

8681.name: Mana Tank III
8681.desc: Increases MP Adds "Refresh" effect to Dark Maneuver

8683.name: Mana Tank IV
8683.desc: Increases MP Adds "Refresh" effect to Dark Maneuver

8704.name: Bismuth Ingot
8704.desc: An ingot of smelted bismuth ore.

8705.name: Bsm. Bolt Heads
8705.desc: The striking tips of bismuth bolts.

8706.name: Raaz Arrowhd.
8706.desc: The striking tips of raaz arrows.

8707.name: Raaz Hide
8707.desc: The tangled and dirt-caked fur of a fallen raaz.

8708.name: Raaz Leather
8708.desc: A thick and hardy square of leather made from the skin of raaz found prowling the dense jungle of Ulbuka.

8709.name: Raaz Tusk
8709.desc: The sheer size of this blood-soaked and chipped raaz cuspid is enough to make adventurers' sides burn with the pain that accompanies impalement.

8710.name: Buried Vestige
8710.desc: A fragment of some long lost, cursed trait deeply chiseled into the subconscious of each and every Vana'dielian.

8711.name: Copper Voucher
8711.desc: Given to adventurers who have proven themselves in Records of Eminence, this tawny and reflective ticket is indispensable for acquiring rewards from the Adventurers' Mutual Aid Network.

8712.name: Slime Fetish
8712.desc: A conduit containing the spirit of an otherworldly slime, painstakingly carved in the creature's likeness. As the entity is currently harbored within, small amounts of magic seep out at regular intervals.

8713.name: Spriggan Fetish
8713.desc: A conduit containing the spirit of an otherworldly beast called a spriggan, painstakingly carved in the creature's likeness. As the entity is currently harbored within, small amounts of magic seep out at regular intervals.

8714.name: Mog Pell (Red)
8714.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment ribboned with red and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a bevy of beautiful baubles.

8715.name: Mog Pell (Green)
8715.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment glazed with green and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a bevy of beautiful baubles.

8716.name: Mog Pell (Gold)
8716.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment gilt with gold and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a bevy of beautiful baubles.

8719.name: Maliya. Coral
8719.desc: Harvested from the Maliyakaleya Reef, this fragment of white coral is so pulchritudinous that more than a handful of adventurers have wasted away after days of staring at it in sheer awe.

8720.name: Maliya. Coral Orb
8720.desc: This geometrically perfect specimen of a pearl was carved from the notoriously beautiful Maliyakaleya coral.

8721.name: Hepatizon Ore
8721.desc: This ore contains the metal hepatizon.

8722.name: Hepatizon Ingot
8722.desc: An ingot of smelted hepatizon ore.

8723.name: Beryllium Ore
8723.desc: This ore contains the metal beryllium.

8724.name: Beryllium Ingot
8724.desc: An ingot of smelted beryllium ore.

8725.name: Exalted Log
8725.desc: This log is emanating an imposing yet sublime aura of holiness.

8726.name: Exalted Lumber
8726.desc: This processed lumber is emanating an imposing yet sublime aura of holiness.

8727.name: Sif's Lock
8727.desc: A strikingly golden lock of ineffable softness snipped from Sif's head. Judging from the soil matted between each strand, it seems she was tending to the fields when the lock was cut.

8728.name: Sif's Macrame
8728.desc: This decorative rope has been woven from Sif's hair.

8729.name: Kupon I-AF109
8729.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for chapters of Rem's Tale used in reforging job-specific equipment.

8730.name: Kupon W-EWS
8730.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for weapons used in acquiring empyrean weaponskills.

8731.name: Kupon AW-WK
8731.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for weapons and armor from wildskeeper reives.

8732.name: Kupon I-S3
8732.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for rank III simulacrum segments.

8733.name: Kupon A-PK109
8733.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for item level 109 Peacekeepers' Coalition armor.

8734.name: Kupon I-S1
8734.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for rank I simulacrum segments.

8735.name: Kupon I-Skill
8735.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for skill-boosting tomes.

8736.name: Ake-Ome Spirit
8736.desc: This crystallized mass of Ake's and Ome's souls is as black as midnight. The mandragoras' essences squirm so incessantly within their mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

8737.name: Star Sprinkles
8737.desc: A stopper-stuffed glass bottle chockablock full of shining stars. Rumor has it that the stars' gossamer glimmer is highly coveted by the Green Thumb Moogle.

8738.name: Kupon I-RME
8738.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for pluton, beitetsu, and riftborn boulders.

8739.name: Wyrm Blood
8739.desc: A vial containing blood that once purportedly coursed through the veins of an ancient dragon.

8740.name: Pizza Cutter
8740.desc: A blade designed specifically to proportion pizza without leaving any cheese clinging to its metal surface. It is also purported to cut through even the toughest of crusts in a single stroke.

8741.name: Ra'Ka. Visage I
8741.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

8742.name: Ra'Ka. Visage II
8742.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

8743.name: Ra'Ka. Visage III
8743.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

8744.name: Ra'Ka. Visage IV
8744.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

8745.name: Ra'Ka. Visage V
8745.desc: A head segment of a weathered and cryptic simulacrum found lying amidst the wilderness of Eastern Ulbuka.

8746.name: Dullahan Armor
8746.desc: Able to be donned only by headless ghouls called dullahans, this hulking suit of armor is fashioned from a special ore found within the confines of Ra'Kaznar.

8747.name: Ra'Kaznar Ore
8747.desc: This ore contains the eponymous metal harvested from the labyrinthine walls of Outer Ra'Kaznar.

8748.name: Ra'Kaznar Ingot
8748.desc: An ingot of smelted Ra'Kaznar ore.

8749.name: Ra. Bolt Heads
8749.desc: The striking tips of Ra'Kaznar bolts.

8750.name: Ra. Arrwhd.
8750.desc: The striking tips of Ra'Kaznar arrows.

8751.name: Ancestral Cloth
8751.desc: Seemingly woven countless generations ago, this occult fabric has absorbed vast amounts of the mystic powers that drift upon the air inside Ra'Kaznar.

8752.name: Cehuetzi Claw
8752.desc: Once a terrorizing adornment of a vicious cehuetzi's paw, this glacially frigid nail could supposedly freeze its prey's flesh while simultaneously tearing through it.

8753.name: C. Ice Shard
8753.desc: Eternally frigid, this crystalline aggregate once remained immutably faceted upon the back of a cehuetzi.

8754.name: Cehuetzi Pelt
8754.desc: Each individual strand of fur within this coarse pelt is said to cleave its way through the surrounding air as its owner walked, creating a horrific sonic disturbance that would cause even the most stalwart Galka's legs to buckle in fright.

8755.name: Phlox Yggrete
8755.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral purple glow.

8756.name: Russet Yggrete
8756.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral brown glow.

8757.name: Aster Yggrete
8757.desc: This stony hunk of a petrified tree contains flecks of a substance that emits an unsettlingly spectral light blue glow.

8758.name: She-Slime Spirit
8758.desc: The crystallized soul of a she-slime as red as a clown's nose. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

8759.name: M. Slime Spirit
8759.desc: The crystallized soul of a metal slime as shiny as freshly smelted silver. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

8760.name: P. Sprigg. Spirit
8760.desc: The crystallized soul of a spriggan as purple as royal robes. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

8761.name: R. Sprigg. Spirit
8761.desc: The crystallized soul of a spriggan as flush red as a berserker's face. It squirms so incessantly within its mineral prison that it emits a palpable warmth.

8793.name: Kupon W-Job
8793.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for job-specific weapons crafted by Oboro.

8794.name: Kupon I-Mat

8973.name: SP Gobbie Key
8973.desc: This slightly rusted key, found in a locked chest on a long-sunken ship, can be used to open the special dial of the Goblin mystery box.

8974.name: Harold's Ore
8974.desc: This gargantuan lump of ore is still covered in spittle from the mouth of Hugemaw Harold.

8975.name: Belinda's Hide

8976.name: Pitriv's Thread
8976.desc: This stinky loop of thread was procured from the lifeless body of Prickly Pitriv.

8977.name: Baldurno's Horn
8977.desc: Though slightly cracked, this horn is an uncontestable symbol of your victory over Ironhorn Baldurno.

8978.name: Sleepy Mabel's Fur

8979.name: Imperator's Wing
8979.desc: This lightweight wing serves as a physical reminder of your triumph over the Valkurm Imperator.

8980.name: Ninlil's Bone

8981.name: Abyssdiver Feather

8982.name: Intuila's Hide
8982.desc: This rough patch of hide was torn from the corpse of Intuila.

8983.name: Arthro's Shell
8983.desc: A soft yet durable shell torn from the back of Emperor Arthro.

8984.name: Orcfeltrap's Leaf
8984.desc: This prickly orange abomination procured from the Orcfeltrap glistens in the morning sun.

8985.name: Jill's Spittle
8985.desc: This disgusting saliva was gathered from the lifeless husk of Lumber Jill.

8986.name: Joyous's Moss

8987.name: Strix's Tailfeather
8987.desc: This tailfeather from Strix once cut through the wind like a sharpened blade.

8988.name: Warblade's Hide
8988.desc: This strip of hide from Warblade Beak gives off a pungent odor.

8989.name: Arke's Wing
8989.desc: A tattered wing wrested from the corpse of Arke.

8990.name: Largantua's Shard
8990.desc: This shard of an indeterminate mineral fell off Largantua's lifeless body.

8991.name: Beist's Blood
8991.desc: This blackened liquid from the Beist has begun to coagulate.

8992.name: Malatrix's Shard
8992.desc: This gruesome trophy serves as a reminder of your triumphant victory over Jester Malatrix.

8993.name: Veloz's Needle
8993.desc: This sharp, dull green needle can prick a hole in even the toughest of leathers.

8994.name: Mender's Log
8994.desc: This piece of petrified wood was chopped off the slain Woodland Mender.

8995.name: C. Mata. Shell
8995.desc: This shell is cracked from having seen one too many bumps against a stone wall.

8996.name: Slashed Vine
8996.desc: The surface of this green rafflesia vine is marred by a thousand tiny cuts.

8997.name: Sliced Thread
8997.desc: This thread from a diremite web was slashed nearly in twain.

8998.name: Wrecked Pincer
8998.desc: This craklaw pincer has seen better days...

8999.name: Sullied Feather
8999.desc: This dirt-caked hippogryph wing is virtually in tatters.

9000.name: Florid Leaf Mold
9000.desc: This leaf mold smells of the sweetest honey and can be attached to one's arboreal grove.

9001.name: Rockoil
9001.desc: This magical ointment can be applied to one's mineral vein.

9002.name: Plovid Effluvium
9002.desc: This yellowish-brown liquid emits a noxious odor that sends your head spinning.

9003.name: Plovid Flesh
9003.desc: Gangrenous discolorations mar the surface of this piece of rancid flesh.

9004.name: Macuil Plating
9004.desc: An otherworldly stench permeates this sheer sheet of metal.

9005.name: Macuil Horn
9005.desc: This fragment, torn from the head of a macuil, crackles with electricity.

9006.name: Defiant Scarf
9006.desc: The unclean scarf is not fit for any of Altana's children to wear.

9008.name: Befouled Water
9008.desc: Foul energies have left streaks of black murk in this clouded, muddy liquid.

9009.name: Etched Memory
9009.desc: This small piece of magical parchment is inscribed with the memories of your recent battles.

9010.name: Dense Cluster
9010.desc: This large lump of a metal of unknown origins weighs more than even the heftiest pig iron.

9011.name: ??? Sword +1
9011.desc: This sword looks just like any other old sword to you.

9028.name: Heiligtum's Moss
9028.desc: A piece of soft moss culled from the Keeper of Heiligtum.

9029.name: Douma's Shard
9029.desc: A dull shard ripped from the Douma Weapon.

9030.name: Uropygid's Needle
9030.desc: A pungent poisoned needle procured from King Uropygid.

9031.desc: A dilapidated wing torn from Vedrfolnir.

9046.name: Tiyanak's Fang
9046.desc: The teeth of some creatures are said to be harder than steel, and this brilliant white fang shows nary a crack or scratch.

9047.name: Immani. Hide
9047.desc: The bumps and indentations on this tough hide are doubtless scars of the battles waged to defeat Immanibugard.

9048.name: Muut's Vestment
9048.desc: The demonic aura permeating this pitch black shroud prevents it from being worn by any of the five enlightened races.

9049.name: Refractive Crystal
9049.desc: The pale yellow light concentrated in this translucent gem threatens to burst forth and escape at any instant.

9050.name: Mellidopt Wing
9050.desc: The wing of a black twitherym that flutters within the deepest recesses of otherworldly skirmishes. Its frayed edges belie a minacious aura that is unceasing and overwhelming.

9051.name: Camahueto's Fur
9051.desc: This matted tuft from Camahueto reeks of sweat and blood.

9052.name: Golden Coil
9052.desc: The spiraling coil made of pure gold is cold to the touch.

9053.name: Platinum Gear
9053.desc: A lonely platinum gear that clearly must fit somewhere.

9054.name: Voso's Hide
9054.desc: Voso's lustrous hide is soft to the touch.

9055.name: Mephitas's Claw
9055.desc: This claw, won from the mangled corpse of Mephitas, is dripping with a sickly green venom.

9056.name: Coca's Wing
9056.desc: Coca's wings are said to be able to cause storms that send all of Mindartia and Quon reeling.

9057.name: Ayapec's Shell
9057.desc: There is nary a scratch in this diamond-hard shell.

9058.name: Specter's Ore
9058.desc: This small nugget of an indeterminate metal was torn from the stomach of the Specter Worm.

9059.name: Azrael's Eye
9059.desc: One of Azrael's many eyes stares at you from beyond the abyss.

9060.name: Ethereal Incense
9060.desc: Burning the conglomeration of spiritual energy contained within this incense will cure many of life's ills.

9061.name: Hades' Claw
9061.desc: This sinister-looking claw once belonged to the entity known as Hades.

9062.name: Dark Matter
9062.desc: This building block of the netherworld absorbs light from the area around it.

9063.name: Tartarian Soul
9063.desc: One of but many souls to be trapped within the confines of Tartarus.

9064.name: Tartarian Chain
9064.desc: This chain is used by the jailers of Tartarus to chain their charges to cell walls.

9065.name: Inhibitor II
9065.desc: A fire-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Store TP  ・Improves TP usage efficiency

9066.name: Amplifier
9066.desc: An ice-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Increass magic burst damage ・Attempts to use magic bursts more

9067.name: Repeater
9067.desc: A wind-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Occasionally executes multiple ranged attacks

9068.name: Barrier Module
9068.desc: An earth-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases chance of blocking  　with shield  ・Earth Maneuver: Adds Shield Mastery  　and shortens Shield Bash recast time

9069.name: Dynamo II
9069.desc: A thunder-based automaton attachment.  Functionality:  ・Increases critical hit rate

9070.name: Resister
9070.desc: A water-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Increases status ailment resistance

9071.name: Resister II
9071.desc: A water-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Increases status ailment resistance

9072.name: Arcanic Cell
9072.desc: A light-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Enhances Occult Acumen

9073.name: Arcanic Cell II
9073.desc: A light-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Enhances Occult Acumen

9074.name: Regulator
9074.desc: A darkness-based automaton attachment.  Functionality: ・Enables use of Absorb-Attri

9075.name: Vulcanite Ore
9075.desc: A dull metal ore used in the construction of edifices in Escha - Zi'Tah.

9076.name: Gravewood Log
9076.desc: A grey log whose energy has been consumed by the necrotic energies of Escha.

9077.name: Duskcrawler
9077.desc: A grey fish whose energy has been consumed by the necrotic energies of Escha.

9078.name: Ashweed
9078.desc: A grey weed whose energy has been consumed by the necrotic energies of Escha.

9079.name: Kitchen Brick
9079.desc: A small red brick used in the construction of a Galley Kitchen. Item one of three.

9080.name: Kitchen Stove
9080.desc: A piping hot stove used in the construction of a Galley Kitchen. Item two of three.

9081.name: Kitchen Plate
9081.desc: A set of dishes used to flesh out a Galley Kitchen. Item three of three.

9082.name: Bee Pollen
9082.desc: A round clump of pollen. Beware of allergic reactions.

9083.name: Mandra. Dewdrop
9083.desc: A clear dewdrop found on the leaf of a mandragora bud.

9084.name: Eschalixir
9084.desc: The ashen swirls within this concoction can draw forth unparalleled energies in certain types of equipment.

9085.name: Eschalixir +1
9085.desc: The ashen swirls within this concoction can draw forth unparalleled energies in certain types of equipment.

9086.name: Eschalixir +2
9086.desc: The ashen swirls within this concoction can draw forth unparalleled energies in certain types of equipment.

9087.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from Perfidien and Plouton in Vagary.

9088.name: Kupon AW-VgrII
9088.desc: A prize redemption coupon from the Mog Bonanza event. Issued by the Mog House Management Union (MHMU). Tradeable for spoils from Palloritus, Putraxia, and Rancibus in Vagary.

9091.name: B. Lu. Rod +1
9091.desc: An unfortunate happenstance caused this most decorated of fishing rods to snap in twain.

9092.name: G. Gel's Mucus
9092.desc: Foul-smelling glop extracted from the corpse of Garbage Gel.

9093.name: Bakunawa's Ink
9093.desc: Black, sticky ink gleaned from the corpse of Bakunawa.

9094.name: Clawberry's Coat
9094.desc: A colorful coat once worn by Azure-toothed Clawberry. Unable to be equipped by others.

9095.name: Vermillion's Wing
9095.desc: A wing torn from the mangled corpse of the Vermillion Fishfly.

9096.name: V. Cluster's Ash
9096.desc: This pile of darkened ash from the Volatile Cluster reeks of explosive fumes.

9097.name: Mhuufya's Beak
9097.desc: A pristine beak wrested from the corpse of Mhuufya.

9098.name: G. Grenade's Ash
9098.desc: Even the tiniest flint could reignite the remnants of the Grand Grenade.

9099.name: Soryu's Liver
9099.desc: The darkened liver of the legendary fish soryu.

9147.desc: The light, yet sturdy wing of an eschan porxie.

9148.name: Eschan Droplets

9149.name: Hidhaegg's Scale
9149.desc: This scale plucks light from the surrounding air.

9150.name: Tolba's Shell
9150.desc: The overwhelming weight of this shell threatens to tear a hole in your bags.

9151.name: Sovereign's Hide
9151.desc: Mild toxins cause tingling feelings in your fingers as you run them across this hide's tattered surface.

9152.name: Mog Pell (Rainbow)
9152.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment ribboned with the seven colors of the rainbow and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a bevy of beautiful baubles.

9158.name: Mog Pell (Ochre)
9158.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment ribboned with ochre and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a cadre of colossal ciphers.

9166.name: Mog Pell (Silver)
9166.desc: A perfectly preserved piece of parchment ribboned with silver and manufactured by moogles. It can be bartered for a bevy of beautiful baubles.

9199.desc: This flavorful liquid serves as the base for making delicious ramen cuisine.

9206.desc: This feathery mane was plucked from the corpse of Shedu.
